jobs.title: Quantitative Investment Analyst
jobs.description:
Position Summary

We are seeking a Quantitative Analyst with three or more years of experience to help advance our research and investment analytics capabilities, with a particular emphasis on data science. This highly capable person will build and maintain a thoughtful data layer across portfolios, managers, and asset classes, while developing analytical tools and workflows that improve decision-making and efficiency. This is a hybrid role that blends investment domain knowledge with data science and applied AI, offering an opportunity to help shape the evolution of our investment platform.

This role is located in Boston, MA.

Primary Responsibilities

  • Partner with senior investment professionals and other colleagues to translate strategic priorities into practical analytical solutions, workflow enhancements, and automation opportunities.
  • Support the Investment team in monitoring existing investments and underwriting new opportunities, including internal discussions related to portfolio construction, performance, and risk.
  • Build and maintain scalable data pipelines and workflows to support reliable data ingestion, transformation, and quality across investment, portfolio, and client datasets.
  • Help develop and enhance the firm’s investment data layer, with a focus on making information more structured, accessible, and actionable across asset classes and investment processes.
  • Analyze large and complex datasets to identify trends, behavioral patterns, performance drivers, and other insights that can inform investment decision-making.
  • Design, validate, and operationalize analytical models and AI-enabled tools to support areas such as asset allocation, manager research, portfolio monitoring, and risk assessment.
  • Maintain strong documentation and adhere to appropriate data governance, control, and compliance standards consistent with industry best practices.

Qualifications

Required

  • Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Data Science, Computer Science, Statistics, Finance, or a quantitative field.
  • Three or more years of relevant experience in data science, analytics, investment research, or a related role, ideally within financial services, asset management, or wealth management.
  • Experience using Git and GitHub for version control, collaborative development, code reviews, and managing production-grade analytical or quantitative applications.
  • Strong programming skills with experience building analytical workflows, models, and data pipelines.
  • Experience working with modern data infrastructure, including Snowflake or similar environments.
  • Solid understanding of financial markets, portfolio construction, investment performance, and risk concepts.
  • Excellent communication skills and the ability to explain complex analyses and technical concepts clearly.

Preferred

  • Experience with business intelligence and data visualization platforms such as Power BI.
  • Familiarity with cloud environments such as AWS, Azure, or GCP.
  • Experience applying machine learning or AI tools in production or business workflows.
  • Experience with agentic workflows using tools such as Copilot Studio or Claude.
  • Familiarity with modern LLM ecosystems and related concepts such as retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), vector databases, agent frameworks, or tool/function calling.
  • Exposure to investment manager research, underwriting, or portfolio analytics is a plus.

This position is an exempt position. The annualized base pay range for this role is expected to be between $150,000–$250,000 base salary compensation range. Actual base pay may vary based on factors including, but not limited to, experience, subject matter expertise, geographic location where work will be performed, and the applicant’s skill set. The base pay is just one component of the total compensation package. Other rewards may include an annual cash bonus and a comprehensive benefits package, including but not limited to medical, dental, vision, life insurance, and 401(k). Please note that the job title is subject to change based on the selected candidate’s experience and education.

SCS is a fiduciary wealth and investment management firm serving some of the world’s most ambitious wealth creators — ultra‑high‑net‑worth individuals, families, and institutions. SCS exists to transform wealth into possibility, helping its clients protect and amplify their capital, align it with their purpose, and build multi‑generational legacies.

SCS delivers an integrated family office and investment platform that brings together sophisticated multi‑asset class portfolio management, access to private investments, holistic wealth and estate planning, next‑generation education, and purpose‑driven advisory. SCS’s team is supported by a modern, scalable technology infrastructure that enhances SCS’s ability to deliver a seamless, proactive, white‑glove client experience.

SCS is part of Focus Financial Partners, a leading financial services firm comprised of wealth management, family office, and business management services.

jobs.title: Research Analyst
jobs.description:
Position Summary

As a Research Analyst, you will be an integral member of the Manager Research team within the Investment Department, responsible for sourcing, evaluating, and monitoring investment managers, as well as conducting research that informs investment decisions and client communications. This role requires analysis across a broad range of investment strategies, asset classes, and geographies, with a focus on generating actionable insights and supporting portfolio construction and oversight.

Focus provides team members the flexibility to work a hybrid schedule. Our hybrid model is defined as 3 days in office work required per week, while giving team members the option to work remotely 2 day per week, or as assigned by their team leader.

Primary Responsibilities

  • Source, evaluate, and conduct due diligence on investment managers across asset classes
  • Identify and research emerging investment opportunities and thematic initiatives to inform investment strategy and client communications
  • Develop and enhance qualitative and quantitative manager research reports
  • Leverage analytical platforms to monitor portfolio exposures, characteristics, and risks, including scenario and stress-test analysis
  • Perform cross-asset portfolio analysis to assess performance, monitor risk, and identify optimization opportunities
  • Maintain and enhance the investment manager database, ensuring data accuracy and seamless integration across systems
  • Improve analytics workflows through automation and process enhancements
  • Produce formal research reports and quarterly manager updates
  • Communicate investment insights clearly and effectively, both verbally and in writing, to investment committee

Qualifications

  • 2+ years of relevant investment research experience (e.g., endowment & foundation, OCIO, RIA/Family Office or as a principal investor), with a focus on active manager due diligence

Education/Training

  • B.S. Accounting, Economics, Engineering, Finance, or Math
  • Interest in pursuing CFA Charter (or comparable certifying exam) preferred

Knowledge, Skills, and Abilities

  • Proficiency in Microsoft Office and financial databases (e.g., Bloomberg, Morningstar, Bipsync, FactSet)
  • Coding or data analytics experience is a strong plus
  • Solid understanding of qualitative and quantitative investment principles
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to translate complex ideas clearly

This position is an Exempt position. The annualized base pay range for this role is expected to be between $100,000 - $150,000/year. Actual base pay could vary based on factors including but not limited to experience, subject matter expertise, geographic location where work will be performed, and the applicant's skill set.  The base pay is just one component of the total compensation package for employees.  Other rewards may include an annual cash bonus and a comprehensive benefits package.

jobs.title: Senior Investment Strategist
jobs.description:
Position Summary

The Senior Investment Strategist partners with advisors, clients, and prospects to provide confidence, clarity, and insight regarding Focus’s investment philosophy, solutions, and market perspectives. They develop and maintain deep expertise in Focus’s investment platform to support advisors in constructing personalized portfolios aligned with client needs and objectives. They create clear, compelling collateral that articulates the value of Focus’s investment offering and equips advisors to apply it effectively in client portfolios. They engage directly with clients and prospects to discuss portfolio positioning and market conditions, increasing client confidence and driving business growth.

Primary Responsibilities

  • Build relationships with advisors as an extension of their advisory team to provide investment strategy and proposal support
  • Serve as investment strategy subject matter-expert in meetings with clients and/or advisors including providing commentary on current market trends
  • Work closely with Manager Research, Proprietary Strategies, and Marketing to create and maintain educational content on Focus investment strategies
  • Serve as deeper educational resource expert in multiple investment strategy areas
  • Present Investment Strategy related topics at firm and industry events
  • Execute on special projects as needed

Qualifications 

  • Bachelors Degree in Accounting, Economics, Engineering, Finance, Math, or related field
  • 7+ years of work experience in an investment-related, communications, or technical positions
  • Ability to work effectively as a team player and collaboratively across disciplines
  • Skilled at building trust and respect of colleagues and clients
  • Excel in a fast-paced environment with the ability to balance multiple demands with a high attention to detail
  • Comprehensive understanding of public and private markets, portfolio design, complex investment vehicles, and investment models
  • Demonstrated professionalism, integrity, a high degree of ethics and maintain confidentiality
  • Ability to effectively interface with advisors, clients and prospects in group and one-on-one settings
  • Ability to offer succinct, value-added recommendations, exercise sound judgment, and make effective decisions with sometimes incomplete information
  • Ability to take initiative and work independently
  • Excellent verbal and written communication skills particularly with regards to conveying technical investment approaches
  • Knowledge of global investment market conditions and trends
  • Ability to speak to groups, in public or via electronic media

This position is an Exempt position. The annualized base pay range for this role is expected to be $140,000 - $160,000 per year. Actual base pay could vary based on factors including but not limited to experience, subject matter expertise, geographic location where work will be performed, and the applicant's skill set. The base pay is just one component of the total compensation package for employees. Other rewards may include an annual cash bonus and a comprehensive benefits package.

jobs.title: Wealth Manager
jobs.description:
Position Summary

Badgley Phelps Wealth Managers, a trusted name in wealth management for over 50 years, distinguishes itself with its distinctive approach to wealth management. Since 1966, we've been more than just fiduciaries; we've been partners in our clients' wealth journey. Our base in Seattle, Washington, is the hub for our independent and unbiased investment advisory services, catering to high-net-worth individuals, families, and institutions. Our team thrives in a collaborative environment dedicated to improving our clients' financial lives.

We are seeking a dynamic investment professional to join our rapidly expanding team. As an investment-focused Wealth Manager, you will have two main responsibilities: researching, recommending, and managing stocks for the firm’s Large Cap Growth portfolio; and working with clients to manage their investments.

This is a hybrid role: 3 days in office / 2 days remote

Primary Responsibilities

This role is a key contributor within the investment team. You will support the firm’s overall research effort, focusing specifically on the Large Cap Growth portfolio. A successful candidate will:

  • Collaborate with co-managers of the Large Cap Growth equity team to source new ideas, conduct fundamental analysis, and contribute to the development and delivery of stock recommendations. This may involve taking both the research analyst and portfolio manager perspectives.
  • Provide ongoing research and recommendations on existing equity positions and recommend changes to the model portfolio as necessary.
  • Contribute to research materials and articulate the team’s views on individual stocks both within the firm and to clients.

The Wealth Manager will manage the investment aspect of the client relationship and collaborate with other members of the client’s team, including the financial planner, associate wealth manager, and client service. You will work independently and in collaboration with other Wealth Managers to help clients achieve their financial goals. For example, you should be comfortable:

  • Managing client portfolios and communicating with clients of varying levels of sophistication regarding their investments and the markets.
  • Leading client meetings and ensuring questions and concerns are addressed. Documenting client contact and follow-up work is part of our process.
  • Delegating general relationship, financial planning, and administrative tasks to teammates as appropriate. You may back up the Relationship Manager as needed.

Successful Wealth Managers may also excel at developing relationships with new clients and other professionals. Growing our business is a core value, and identifying new opportunities and retaining existing relationships is a goal across the firm.

Qualifications

The Wealth Manager role is highly impactful within the firm and with clients. Therefore, the following qualities are crucial:

  • Demonstrated ability to deliver exceptional client service. Strong client relationship skills—listening, relatability, communication, empathy, compassion, and thoughtfulness. Excellent verbal and written communication skills, both internally and externally (with clients and professional partners).
  • Trustworthy and accountable with attention to detail. Professional poise, patience, understanding, and tact, particularly when working with clients under stress.
  • Ability to prioritize tasks and put the client first. Able to work independently as a self-motivated and proactive manager, while also communicating effectively with a team to serve clients.
  • Strong investment knowledge of both current and historical markets. Aligns with active management and individual securities. Feels comfortable discussing investment strategies and positions with clients in all types of markets.
  • Dedicated to continuously learning and growing. CFA designation preferred. Being active in the community and serving those around them.

The annualized base pay range for this role is expected to be between $150,000-$250,000 base salary compensation range. Actual base pay may vary based on factors including, but not limited to, experience, subject matter expertise, geographic location where work will be performed, and the applicant’s skill set. The base pay is just one component of the total compensation package. Other rewards may include an annual cash bonus and a comprehensive benefits package, including but not limited to medical, dental, vision, life insurance, and 401(k). Please note that the job title is subject to change based on the selected candidate’s experience and education.

#LI-JS1

As part of the partnership, Badgley Phelps remains independently operated but gains the support of a broader organization. Whether focused on growth, enhancing capabilities, or planning for the future, being part of Focus means you are part of something larger while still working in an entrepreneurial, firm-led environment.

jobs.title: Senior Analyst, Portfolio Manager - GC Wealth
jobs.description:
Who We Are

General Catalyst is a global investment and transformation company that partners with the world’s most ambitious entrepreneurs to drive resilience and applied AI. We support founders with a long-term view who challenge the status quo, partnering with them from seed to growth stage and beyond.

With offices in the U.S., Europe, and India, we have supported the growth of 800+ businesses, including Airbnb, Anduril, Anthropic, Applied Intuition, Commure, Glean, Gusto, Helsing, Hubspot, Kayak, Livongo, Mistral, Ramp, Samsara, Snap, Stripe, Sword, and Zepto.

GC Wealth serves as an independent wealth and asset management business serving high net worth individuals, family offices, foundations and not for profit organizations.

About The Role

We are seeking an experienced Senior Analyst to join our GC Wealth team. 

The Senior Analyst is a core member of the GC Wealth investment team, driving portfolio research and analysis across asset classes and leading client-facing investment reporting. This experienced professional works closely with Wealth Managers, and the GC Wealth Chief Investment Officer to shape investment strategy, execute trades, and deliver market and portfolio updates to clients and prospects, serving as a trusted resource for both clients and internal teams. The ideal candidate brings deep knowledge of multi-asset portfolio management, custodial operations and an understanding of the tax and estate planning considerations that high net worth clients navigate.

Key Responsibilities

  • Ongoing research and analysis of existing and potential portfolio holdings, spanning all asset classes and alternative investments
  • Oversee and execute on estate planning and tax strategies for high net worth clients
  • Generate asset allocation, performance, and comprehensive balance sheet reporting for clients
  • Collaborate with other Analysts, Senior Analysts, Wealth Managers and Relationship Managers to establish strategic direction for client ongoing investment portfolio management
  • Develop and refine dashboards and other analytics for internal and external review utilizing investment research and artificial intelligence tools
  • Manage equity and fixed income investments across accounts including management of cash positions and client liquidity and income needs
  • Providing regular market, economic, and portfolio updates to clients, prospects, and centers of influence
  • Oversee, mentor, and develop Analysts and other team members 
  • Collaborate with Chief Investment Officer to participate in strategic research and portfolio evaluation initiatives
  • Proactively identify opportunities for process improvement using AI and activate introductions across the General Catalyst organization where appropriate
  • Oversee client meetings and advise on investment strategy

Qualifications

  • Bachelor's degree in Finance, Economics, Business, or related field
  • Minimum 5+ years experience in investment management
  • CFA strongly preferred
  • Strong analytical and communication skills
  • Strong quantitative and academic portfolio management knowledge
  • Proven ability to manage multiple priorities and deliver exceptional service
  • Demonstrated leadership and mentoring skills with a collaborative team approach
  • Excellent attention to detail, communication, and problem-solving skills

At General Catalyst, we believe the best ideas and the boldest outcomes come from people who bring different perspectives to the table. We know that outlier outcomes require us to think differently than everyone else. That’s why we’re committed to building teams that reflect a wide range of backgrounds, experiences and ways of thinking. Diverse perspectives are essential to our mission of responsible innovation and creating inclusive prosperity.

jobs.title: Energy Portfolio & Solutions Manager
jobs.description:
Always wanted to challenge the obvious and make great things happen? At Goodman we create space for your greatness.

Goodman is evolving as a prominent player in both real estate and the dynamic energy landscape. As we navigate this transformative journey, we are actively looking for an Energy Portfolio and Solutions Manager for Belgium and The Netherlands to join our team. In this role, you will work on commercializing and rolling out our energy products and services while defining and implementing pricing strategies. You will collaborate with the different teams to upgrade our portfolio and to achieve the commercial goals and objectives. You will also oversee developing and optimizing cost and revenue streams in our energy ecosystem.

Here is what this opportunity entails:

Strategic Leadership & Market Expertise

  • Serve as the primary energy expert for internal and external stakeholders across assigned markets.
  • Monitor energy market regulations and policy developments and translate their impact on leases, agreements, and asset operations.
  • Assess the relevance and feasibility of on-site and off-site energy schemes for Goodman assets (e.g., solar PV, storage, grid services).
  • Define and standardize energy procurement strategies for Goodman assets, including both energy off-take and energy injection.
  • Develop and implement pricing strategies for Goodman’s energy-related products across relevant markets.

Business Development & Customer Engagement

  • Engage proactively with customers, partners, and third parties to develop and offer energy-related products such as Power Purchase Agreements (PPAs), Battery Energy Storage Systems (BESS), and Electric Vehicle Charging Infrastructure (EVCI).
  • Build and maintain strong relationships with Goodman’s customers, prospects, and suppliers.
  • Lead tender processes and secure subsidies for photovoltaic (PV) installations in coordination with internal stakeholders.
  • Support the design and execution of commercial campaigns to promote Goodman’s energy solutions and services.

Contract Management & Execution

  • Act as the end-to-end owner of energy contracts, including negotiation, execution, monitoring, and renegotiation with suppliers and customers.
  • Negotiate commercial and contractual terms within established company guidelines.
  • Conduct supplier and customer due diligence, including credit checks, KYC verification, and reference checks.
  • Prepare, review, and execute energy-related agreements and contracts.

Do you recognize yourself?

  • > 5 yrs. of industry experience in a trading, origination, sales, energy management.
  • Experience in the energy market, with a deep understanding of energy pricing, market dynamics and regulations, understanding of different structures of energy contracts, when they are applied and how they are negotiated.
  • Proven track record of successful energy procurement and negotiating buyer and seller side. Good understanding of PPA’s is a plus.
  • Ability to structure, negotiate and execute deals with commercial approach.
  • Project and process management skills with experience collaborating on simultaneous projects.
  • Excellent analytical, problem-solving, and decision-making skills.
  • Proficiency in data analysis and modelling tools (e.g., Python) is considered a strong advantage to support energy market analysis and strategic decision-making.
  • Demonstrated interest in conducting analyses across diverse energy topics, including areas such as Energy Management Systems (EMS) and other emerging energy solutions.
  • Strong interpersonal and communication skills, with the ability to build relationships with energy providers and clients.
  • A collaborative and team-oriented mindset, thriving in an environment where collective leadership and individual accountability drive success.
  • Business fluency in English and Dutch

Not sure if you fit the profile? Let’s find out together!

But first, let us introduce ourselves a little more:

Goodman Group is a provider of essential infrastructure. We own, develop and manage high quality, sustainable logistics properties and data centers in major global cities, that are critical to the digital economy. Goodman operates in key consumer markets in 15 countries across Asia Pacific, Europe and the Americas. We are the largest property group listed on the Australian Securities Exchange and invest significantly alongside our capital partners in our investment Partnerships. Our Continental European headquarter is based in Belgium and we are active in 7 European countries, including offices in Amsterdam, Brussels, Düsseldorf, Hamburg, Frankfurt, Munich, Luxembourg, Madrid, Barcelona, Milan and Paris.

Our culture is based on collaboration, ownership and seizing the opportunity. We take full responsibility for our work, support each other’s success and make room for growth for everyone.

How do we look at the world of tomorrow?

At Goodman we believe we all have a role to play in safeguarding the future for next generations. Therefore, sustainability is the driving force behind everything we do. Not only do we foster a sustainable and inclusive working environment for all our employees but also, throughout all our projects, we are continuously engaged in a process to assess its sustainability. Each aspect of the properties’ lifecycle is scrutinized on its future proofness for decades to come, resulting in a low ecological footprint over time. At Goodman, we believe it’s not enough to talk the talk — we also need to walk the walk.

jobs.title: Property Accounting Lead Continental Europe
jobs.description:
Always wanted to challenge the obvious and make great things happen? At Goodman we create space for your greatness.

As Goodman continues to grow, we are expanding our Asset Management team to include a dedicated Team Lead Property Accounting for our Continental European (CE) logistics real estate portfolio. In this role, you will report directly into the lead of asset management, will be responsible to lead the property accounting teams across CE, set the standards, ensure compliance, and coordinate with local teams to maintain high quality standards across a portfolio of 100+ properties. You will join a dynamic team of professionals, all committed to excel and eager to welcome you onboard.

More concretely, this means you will get the opportunity to:

  • Strategic Leadership
    • Develop and execute a comprehensive property accounting strategy for the CE logistics portfolio
    • Guide and coach teams spread across Europe to ensure a harmonized and qualitative property management approach
    • Maintain and further build a strong team to ensure strategic goals are met through upskilling and recruitment
    • Sourcing and onboarding of new team resources
  • Define and implement standards for property accounting
    • Develop and document CE-wide processes and best practices for property accounting (collections, processing of incoming invoices, invoicing,...)
    • Coordinate the rollout of operational standards and training of the teams
    • Monitor consistency and quality of execution across countries
  • Ensure correct customer invoicing and collections across the CE portfolio
    • Oversee correctness of customer invoicing through rent invoicing, service charge reconciliations, recharges etc.
    • Ensure to maintain high standards of data quality and correctness of data for property financial and technical data
    • Ensure cross-portfolio compliance of our customers with lease obligations from a financial point of view (e.g. guarantees etc)
    • Oversee collection activities of late payments
  • Drive digitalisation and technical system adoption
    • Act as subject matter expert for the roll out and further optimization of our softwares (Microsoft Dynamics)
    • Ensure system usage aligns with operational needs and KPIs
    • Identify further opportunities for automation and efficiency
  • Monitor performance and enable continuous improvement
    • Track financial KPIs
    • Escalate KPIs at risk and initiate action plans with relevant stakeholders
    • Support implementation of CE-wide initiatives to improve efficiency and performance
    • Facilitate knowledge sharing across teams and geographies
  • Coordinate with internal and external stakeholders
    • Act as central point of coordination for CE-wide property accounting topics
    • Liaise with local team members and other Goodman CE teams on staffing of projects/initiatives and prioritisation
    • Ensure clear and consistent communication across all levels

Do you recognize yourself?

  • 7+ years of experience
  • Willingness to travel bi-weekly within Continental Europe (Paris, Düsseldorf, Amsterdam)
  • Experience in property accounting, lease accounting or similar experience (AP/AR, service charge accounting, reconciliations)
  • Experience in people management (international experience is a plus)
  • Knowledge of the Real Estate sector is a plus
  • Systemic thinking and process mindset
  • Can do and problem-solving attitude
  • Improvement mindset – seeking efficiencies
  • Strong communicator
  • Affinity and interest in technology & IT tools
  • Excellent written and spoken knowledge of English
  • Team player: The ability to work both independently and within a team environment, coordinating several internal stakeholders simultaneously
  • Ability to combine a helicopter view with a good eye for detail

jobs.title: Analyst, Financial Advisory (New York and San Francisco)
jobs.description:
About Investment Management 

Investment Management strives to deliver exceptional investment portfolios for some of the world’s most influential families and an array of mission-driven institutions. Our goal is to compound returns from diverse sources by harnessing the differentiated capital and strategic strength of the ICONIQ community, cultivated on a foundation of uncommon care.               

The Opportunity: Financial Advisory, Analyst

The Financial Advisory Analyst is a critical member of the relationship management team for high net worth and ultra-high net worth clients.

Principal Responsibilities: 

  • Prepare client balance sheets and develop asset allocation analyses.
  • Monitor client investment performance and manage quarterly portfolio reports, ensuring accuracy and consistency.
  • Develop a deep understanding of portfolio construction, asset classes, investment products, macroeconomics, client customizations, and professional communication.
  • Perform ad hoc analyses for clients’ financial and estate planning needs.
  • Participate in equity and option trading activities with increasing responsibilities for investment execution.
  • Gather, prepare, and coordinate client tax data and documentation.
  • Be exposed to and involved in direct private investments, client interactions, and other critical family office operations.

Qualifications: 

  • Two years of relevant professional/financial services experience.
  • Passion for investment management and client service.
  • Detail-oriented and thorough.
  • Superior communication and analytical skills.
  • Highly motivated, trustworthy, intellectually curious, and mature.
  • Excellent academic background and strong cognitive ability.
  • Bachelor’s degree required.

Location: Applicants are expected to work onsite in our New York or San Francisco office in accordance with our hybrid working policy.

Please note: We are continuously seeking talent for opportunities within our Client Advisory business and this job has been posted to support talent pipelining efforts. To register your interest in future Financial Advisory roles within ICONIQs Client Advisory team, apply here and we will be in touch.  We are interested in speaking with applicants open to working in either our San Francisco or New York office.

Compensation Range: ICONIQ is required by law to include a reasonable estimate of the salary range for this role. There are several factors taken into consideration in making compensation decisions including but not limited to skill sets; experience and training; licensure and certifications; and other business and organizational needs. At ICONIQ, we apply a total reward philosophy where all aspects of compensation and benefits are taken into consideration in determining compensation terms. A reasonable estimate of the current salary range is $90,000 - $105,000 together with the benefits listed above.

jobs.title: Associate, Valuations
jobs.description:
ICONIQ is seeking a Valuations Manager to support valuation analysis and performance reporting across its Venture & Growth and Real Assets investment portfolios. This role partners closely with investment teams to evaluate portfolio companies, deliver valuation insights, and contribute to cross-functional projects requiring strong analytical, communication, and project management skills.

What You'll Do: 

  • Develop subject matter expertise in all things valuation, including industry and investment specific valuation methodologies and reporting requirements. Specialize in growth equity, debt, real estate, and complex securities valuation
  • Prepare / Review valuation analysis and memos for all Level 3 investments in accordance with valuation policy and ASC 820
  • Prepare summary exhibits and review fund level valuations in connection with quarterly Valuation Committee meetings
  • Prepare and maintain performance calculations for investment level and fund level portfolio monitoring
  • Maintain data set used for preparation of portfolio company valuations including tracking private company financials, financings, cap tables, and projections as needed
  • Manage iLevel data collection platform and other analytics software tools
  • Consult with investment associates and senior team on a regular basis to gather qualitative portfolio company updates and feedback for valuation analysis
  • Provide audit and investor relations support for portfolio company valuation related queries
  • Support the finance and investment teams with ad-hoc projects including preparing materials for meetings and providing individual reporting with respect to portfolio company valuations and fund/investment performance metrics
  • Review and assist with back-testing and maintenance of valuation policy / procedures / governance

Ideal Candidate Background

  • 4+ years of experience, including but not limited to: Big 4 Valuations/Transaction Services, and/or Valuations Consulting. Valuations/Portfolio Monitoring experience at a PE/VC firm is strongly preferred
  • Demonstrated experience reviewing and providing feedback on valuation work product prepared by junior team members
  • Superior Excel and PowerPoint skills
  • Proficiency with Capital IQ / Factset / Bloomberg; Experience with valuation software is a strong plus
  • Bachelor’s degree with concentration in finance / accounting / economics
  • CFA / ASA / CPA / CEIV is a plus
  • Experience with real estate / debt / complex security valuation is a strong plus
  • Technology / Real estate sector knowledge is a plus

What We Offer

  • Comprehensive Medical, Dental, and Vision Insurance
  • Life and disability insurance plus wellness, fertility, and mental health benefits
  • 401(k) with matching contributions
  • Commuter benefits and wellness stipends
  • Tuition reimbursement and ongoing professional development support
  • Smart-casual dress code and a vibrant, collaborative workplace

Location: Applicants are expected to work onsite in our New York City office in accordance with our hybrid working policy.

Compensation Range: ICONIQ is required by law to include a reasonable estimate of the salary range for this role, together with other compensation being offered such as a discretionary bonus, health insurance, and other perks. At ICONIQ, we apply a total reward philosophy where all aspects of compensation and benefits are taken into consideration in determining compensation terms. jobs.title: Senior Analyst- Associate, Financial Advisory (New York and San Francisco)
jobs.description:
Senior Analyst or Associate, Financial Advisory (Talent Pipeline)

As a Senior Analyst or Associate within our Financial Advisory (FA) team, you will operate at the intersection of legacy and innovation, serving as a trusted partner to some of the world’s most influential individuals and families. You will help clients navigate the complexities of their financial lives with precision, empathy, and uncommon care, ensuring that every element of their balance sheet, investment strategy, and long-term plan is managed with clarity and foresight.

You will be joining a collaborative advisory team that values learning, partnership and shared ownership. We work closely across levels and alongside specialists so that every client benefits from the full strength of the ICONIQ platform.

This is a role for professionals who are energized by complexity, motivated by service, and driven to make a lasting impact. If you take pride in anticipating needs, delivering exceptional outcomes, and making complexity feel effortless, this is your opportunity to join a team built on trust, care, and excellence.


Given our ongoing talent needs, we are consistently looking for high quality individuals to join our team. However, please note that this opening is intended to pipeline candidates for future opportunities, and we will reach out when the timing is right.

Core Responsibilities for All Candidates:

You will support clients across a broad set of investment, planning, and relationship activities, including:

  • Preparing and analyzing balance sheets, asset allocations, investment performance reports, and other financial materials.
  • Assisting with investment operations, including trading coordination, cash flow monitoring, and private investment updates.
  • Conducting financial and estate planning analyses such as liquidity modeling, lending evaluations, and gifting illustrations.
  • Partnering with accountants, attorneys, and external advisors to gather information and support planning conversations.
  • Preparing client meeting materials, managing follow ups, and maintaining accurate data in Salesforce, Addepar, and other systems.
  • Collaborating closely with Investment Strategy Group, Legal, Compliance, and Operations to ensure seamless client service.

If You Are a Senior Analyst:

Senior Analysts take on increasing ownership, judgment, and communication responsibility. In addition to the core responsibilities, you will:

  • Own key deliverables such as preparing asset allocation reviews, rebalancing recommendations, and planning analyses.
  • Support advanced planning work, including Monte Carlo simulations, executive compensation summaries, and scenario-based analyses.
  • Build knowledge of trusts, estate strategies, and gifting structures.
  • Participate in parts of client meetings and proactively identify opportunities such as refinancing, liquidity needs, or strategic planning considerations.
  • Mentor Analysts on processes, systems, and best practices as you grow into a leadership mindset.

Sr. Analyst Qualifications:
A bachelor’s degree and 3+ years of relevant experience in financial services or wealth management.

If You Are an Associate:

Associates deepen their role as strategic advisors and trusted client partners. You will:

  • Lead client relationships in partnership with senior team members, helping clients interpret investment results, plan across generations, and make informed decisions.
  • Drive portfolio discussions, rebalancing recommendations, and investment rationale with a strong understanding of asset classes and the ICONIQ investment philosophy.
  • Guide trust and estate planning conversations and coordinate with attorneys on structures, gifting strategies, and long-term planning.
  • Manage complex client processes such as 10b5 1 plans, lending evaluations, private investment coordination, and other high impact deliverables.
  • Coach junior teammates, strengthen team workflows, and help elevate the quality and clarity of client outputs.

Associate Qualifications:
A bachelor’s degree and 5+ years of experience working with high net worth or ultra-high net worth clients.

Who You Are:

Across both levels, successful candidates demonstrate:

  • Strong analytical and communication skills and comfort explaining complex topics simply.
  • Strong attention to detail and a commitment to accuracy.
  • Intellectual curiosity, proactive thinking, and a passion for client service.
  • High integrity in handling confidential and sensitive information.
  • A collaborative mindset and enthusiasm for working as part of a team.

Location: Applicants are expected to work onsite in our New York or San Francisco office in accordance with our hybrid working policy.

Compensation Range:  ICONIQ is required by law to include a reasonable estimate of the salary range for this role. There are several factors taken into consideration in making compensation decisions including but not limited to skill sets; experience and training; licensure and certifications; and other business and organizational needs. At ICONIQ, we apply a total reward philosophy where all aspects of compensation and benefits are taken into consideration in determining compensation terms. A reasonable estimate of the current salary range is $110,000 - $120,000 for Senior Analysts and $130,000 - $150,000 for Associates together with a discretionary bonus and the benefits listed above.

The Financial Advisory, Senior Associate is a critical member of the client coverage team for high net worth and ultra-high net worth clients.

Principal Responsibilities:

  • Prepare client balance sheets and develop asset allocation analyses.
  • Monitor client investment performance and manage quarterly portfolio reports, ensuring accuracy and consistency.
  • Develop a deep understanding of portfolio construction, asset classes, investment products, macroeconomics, client customizations, and professional communication.
  • Lead and support comprehensive analyses for clients’ financial and estate planning needs, including liquidity planning, cash flow forecasting, entity-level analysis, tax-aware considerations, wealth transfer strategies, and scenario modeling to inform client decision-making.
  • Strong understanding and familiarity with capital markets trading (stock, bonds, structured notes, FX) with increasing responsibilities for investment execution.
  • Gather, prepare, and coordinate client tax data and documentation.
  • Be exposed to and involved in direct private investments, client interactions, and other critical family office operations.
  • Mentor and guide junior team members on client deliverables and day-to-day workflows.

Qualifications:

  • 10 to 12 years of relevant professional services experience. We are also open to considering candidates at the Associate level for this role, depending on experience.
  • Passion for investment management and client service.
  • Detail-oriented and thorough.
  • Superior communication and analytical skills.
  • Highly motivated, trustworthy, intellectually curious and mature.
  • Excellent academic background and strong cognitive ability.
  • Bachelor's degree.

Location:

Applicants are expected to work onsite in our Singapore office in accordance with our hybrid working policy.

About ICONIQ

ICONIQ is a global investment firm elevated by an extraordinary community. With over $100B assets under management, we seek to build resilient investment portfolios, partner with inspired entrepreneurs transforming industries, manage our clients’ lives and legacies, and create uncommon opportunities across sectors and society. 

When you submit a job application as a prospective employee (“your” or “your”) of ICONIQ Capital LLC (“ICONIQ,” “we,” “us,” or “our”), we or our service providers may collect, use, disclose and otherwise process personal information about you. Personal information collected by the Greenhouse portal is subject to the Greenhouse's privacy policy, available here [https://www.greenhouse.com/privacy-policy] and ICONIQ’s Privacy Policies available here [https://www.iconiqcapital.com/privacy-policies]. 

Use of AI in the Application Process

We expect candidates to be fluent in using AI tools to enhance their work. You’re welcome to use AI in preparing your application where it adds value, but your materials must be accurate and reflect your own experience, judgement and voice.

We care most about how you think, what you’ve personally contributed, and how you use tools like AI to drive better outcomes. Strong candidates demonstrate both authenticity and leverage, using AI to elevate their work while maintaining clear ownership.

Throughout the interview process, you should be prepared to speak in depth about your experience and contributions in your own words.

jobs.title: 2027 Summer Analyst
jobs.description:
The Firm:

Lead Edge Capital is a $9 billion growth equity ﬁrm backing software, internet, and tech-enabled businesses around the world. Since 2011, we’ve partnered with over 100 companies, including Alibaba, Asana, Toast, Duo Security, Wise, and Benchling, and have seen more than 60 successful exits. We look for companies where our network can make a diﬀerence, and where speed, access, and execution matter. We’re powered by a global network of 700+ operators, executives, and entrepreneurs who do more than commit capital. They help our companies hire, grow, and win. Lead Edge has oﬃces in New York, London, and Santa Barbara.

Overview:

The Lead Edge Analyst Program is a summer internship designed to provide meaningful exposure to the buy-side investment landscape during junior summer. Summer Analysts play an integral role in generating deal flow and evaluating potential investments, working closely with Senior Investment Partners and Associates. Throughout the internship, Analysts engage in numerous one-on-one conversations with entrepreneurs to help source new investment opportunities for the firm. When an Analyst identifies a promising opportunity, they become deeply involved in the diligence and evaluation process, contributing meaningfully to investment discussions and decision-making.

Duties and Responsibilities:

  • Perform market research and collaborate with LEC’s network to develop insights into various markets
  • Build and maintain relationships with founders and management teams through proactive sourcing efforts
  • Screen deals and support team members in conducting quantitative analyses, including financial modeling and retention analysis
  • Maintain a pipeline of investment opportunities to present to team members and senior leadership
  • Creating materials and analysis for evaluation by LEC’s investment committee
  • Performing ﬁnancial modeling and valuation analysis
  • Monitor and support portfolio companies, providing operational insight

Requirements:

  • Strong interest in growth equity, and technology investing
  • Excellent analytical and critical-thinking skills; detail-oriented, proactive, and highly self-motivated
  • Strong communication and interpersonal skills with the ability to build relationships with entrepreneurs, founders, and teammates
  • Collaborative mindset and comfort working independently or on lean teams
  • Strong analytical, critical thinking, and problem-solving skills
  • Proficient with the Microsoft Office suite
  • 3.5 GPA or higher
  • U.S. work authorization required

Compensation:

  • The budgeted base salary range is $100,000 pro-rated over the length of the internship.

jobs.title: Originations Analyst
jobs.description:
The Firm:

Lead Edge Capital is a $9 billion growth equity firm backing software, internet, and tech-enabled businesses around the world. Since 2011, we’ve partnered with over 100 companies, including Alibaba, Asana, Toast, Duo Security, Wise, and Benchling, and have seen more than 60 successful exits. We look for companies where our network can make a difference, and where speed, access, and execution matter. We’re powered by a global network of 700+ operators, executives, and entrepreneurs who do more than commit capital. They help our companies hire, grow, and win. Lead Edge has offices in New York, London, and Santa Barbara.

Overview:

As an Originations Analyst, you will be a key part of Lead Edge’s growing Originations practice. As LEC scales and markets evolve, sourcing from intermediary channels has become crucial to deal flow. The Originations group at Lead Edge focuses on deal generation, with an emphasis on intermediary relationships, and supporting the broader firm in identifying new opportunities.

Duties and Responsibilities:

Primary responsibilities of an Originations Analyst will be assisting with the operations of the Originations efforts such as internal reporting, CRM management, analysis projects on coverage, and process shepherding, as well as supporting outward-facing sourcing efforts such as relationship building and entertainment. With a small team today, the Originations team represents a growth opportunity within Lead Edge.

  • Develop a strong understanding of internal systems and workflows to ensure proper sourcing documentation
  • Own end-to-end data management, including data entry, maintenance, and reporting.
  • Conduct data-driven analyses on sourcing efforts to help guide coverage decisions
  • Support with “Air Traffic Control” developing the right institutional knowledge of LEC to make the best connections to external parties
  • Assist in preliminary processing and review of investment opportunities
  • Source, develop, and maintain new relationships with peers at relevant firms
  • Participate in entertainment such as in-person meetings, events, dinners, etc.

Requirements:

  • 0-2 years of adjacent experience such as sales operations, business development, investment banking, or consulting is a plus but not required
  • Demonstrated interest in working in or around the technology industry
  • Proficient with the Microsoft Office Suite
  • Organized, able to streamline processes for greater efficiency
  • Familiar with CRM tools such as Salesforce
  • Demonstrated interest in entrepreneurship and venture capital/growth equity
  • Team-first orientation with the ability to contribute to firm culture
  • This is an in-person role based in New York City. Applicants must be in or willing to relocate to the New York area.

Compensation

  • The budgeted base salary range is $100,000-$125,000. Total compensation will include base salary and performance-based bonus. Total package will be commensurate with qualifications and experience and will be highly competitive relative to market. Actual pay will be adjusted based on experience.

jobs.title: 2027 Summer Analyst
jobs.description:
2027 Private Equity Summer Analyst

Level Equity is a NYC-based private equity firm with a focus on growth-stage software and technology investments. Our mission is to generate best-in-class financial returns, operate in a highly transparent and ethical manner, and create an exceptional culture of innovation. We are building a firm where hard work and thoughtful analysis are married with intellect and creativity to identify, fund, and support a select number of companies to achieve great outcomes. Our portfolio of investments can be viewed at www.levelequity.com. Level currently has over $6.4 billion in assets under management and 70+ active investments. We are seeking Summer Analysts to join our investment team starting in May 2027.

Summer Analysts are responsible for identifying, qualifying, and analyzing new investment opportunities. Summer Analysts will manage a pipeline of potential deal prospects, research different technology sectors, build relationships with CEOs of rapidly growing companies, and help support the investment due-diligence process. They will also contribute in-depth analysis and M&A pipeline development work for existing portfolio companies. The ideal candidate is incredibly curious, energized by seeking out and engaging with prospective companies, and is a strong networker. The position requires strong communication skills and analytical capabilities, creativity, and drive. Understanding of and passion for both technology and the growth equity industry, while not necessary, are attractive. Qualifications are outlined in more detail below:

  • Strong record of academic and extra-curricular achievement with a proven track record of leadership skills;
  • Excellent interpersonal skills with an ability to network, communicate, and collaborate with colleagues, company executives, entrepreneurs, and business partners;
  • Exceptional analytical skills, business acumen, and knowledge of financial modeling and analysis;
  • Superb work ethic and a high energy, self-motivated entrepreneurial spirit with a strong desire to produce high quality deliverables;
  • Genuine interest in software and technology

Expectations and responsibilities of the position are as follows:

  • Generate high-quality deal flow by proactively identifying investment opportunities through outbound calling/emailing, coverage of various technology sectors, and pattern recognition;
  • Manage a high-volume pipeline of investment prospects;
  • Form thoughtful opinions on investment opportunities based on both key data points and market insights;
  • Assist senior investment team members with due diligence of potential investments and analysis for existing portfolio companies;
  • Monitor macro and micro industry trends to recommend new sectors of technology for evaluation and potential investment

Compensation:

  • Minimum Salary: $100,000 annual salary, paid pro-rated for the term of the summer program.
  • Maximum Salary: $100,000 annual salary, paid pro-rated for the term of the summer program.

jobs.title: Sales Leader II - PNC Wealth Management
jobs.description:
Position Overview

At PNC, our people are our greatest differentiator and competitive advantage in the markets we serve. We are all united in delivering the best experience for our customers. We work together each day to foster an inclusive workplace culture where all of our employees feel respected, valued and have an opportunity to contribute to the company’s success. As a Sales Leader II within PNC Wealth Management, you will be based in Pittsburgh, PA.PNC is an in-office company that fosters a supportive culture where employees can thrive and achieve balance. We encourage candidates to connect with their recruiter and hiring manager to understand workplace expectations and ensure the role aligns with their goals.PNC will not provide sponsorship for employment visas or participate in STEM OPT for this position.

Job Description

  • Manages and directs a team to achieve business results and customer experience goals. May have direct sales and client responsibilities. Oversees the execution of strategy.
  • Leads and coordinates the daily activities of team members engaged in customer and/or prospect management activities. Achieves business results, attracts new customers and/or grows existing customer relationships by coaching and managing to defined strategies and tactics. Develops and oversees the execution of account and sales plans. May have direct sales and client responsibilities.
  • Manages risk/return and drives quality for new and/or existing clients. Actively identifies and mitigates different types of risk, such as regulatory, reputational, operational and credit risks.
  • Manages effective network of senior internal and external relationships, such as community or industry relationships, to actively acquire new clients and/or expand existing clients and enhance the client experience. Focuses on bringing the full range of PNC's products and services to the client. Leverages reporting and sales tools to proactively identify and successfully convert sales opportunities.
  • Develops team members while monitoring progress against individual goals. Motivates and develops staff, including acquiring top talent and managing performance-based issues.Effectively plans, organizes, directs, analyzes and evaluates staff and processes. Connects strategy to day-to-day activities and updates the team on a regular basis.
  • As defined with the business, coaches team members on skills needed to analyze, evaluate and mitigate credit risk and to lead others on credit requests by utilizing advanced knowledge of credit fundamentals and internal credit policies.

PNC Employees take pride in our reputation and to continue building upon that we expect our employees to be:

  • Customer Focused - Knowledgeable of the values and practices that align customer needs and satisfaction as primary considerations in all business decisions and able to leverage that information in creating customized customer solutions.
  • Managing Risk - Assessing and effectively managing all of the risks associated with their business objectives and activities to ensure they adhere to and support PNC's Enterprise Risk Management Framework.

PNC also has fundamental expectations of our people managers. As a manager of talent in PNC, you will be expected to:

  • Include Intentionally - Cultivates diverse teams and inclusive workplaces to expand thinking.
  • Live the Values - Role models our values with transparency and courage.
  • Enable Change - Takes action to drive change and innovation that will transform our business.
  • Achieve Results - Takes personal ownership to deliver results. Empowers and trusts others in decision making.
  • Develop the Best - Raises the bar with every talent decision and guides the achievement of all employees and customer.

Qualifications

Successful candidates must demonstrate appropriate knowledge, skills, and abilities for a role. Listed below are skills, competencies, work experience, education, and required certifications/licensures needed to be successful in this position.

Competencies

Conflict Management, Decision Making and Critical Thinking, Effective Communications, Managing Multiple Priorities, Sales Management, Selling.

Work Experience

Roles at this level typically require a university / college degree. Higher level education such as a Masters degree, PhD, or certifications is desirable. Industry-relevant experience is typically 8+ years. At least 5 years of prior management experience is typically required. Proven leadership experience with a moderate to large scope of responsibility is required. In lieu of a degree, a comparable combination of education, job specific certification(s), and experience (including military service) may be considered.

Education

Bachelors

Certifications

No Required Certification(s)

Licenses

FINRA Series 7, 24 (or 9,10) and 66 (or 63,65) required.

Benefits

PNC offers a comprehensive range of benefits to help meet your needs now and in the future. Depending on your eligibility, options for full-time employees include: medical/prescription drug coverage (with a Health Savings Account feature), dental and vision options; employee and spouse/child life insurance; short and long-term disability protection; 401(k) with PNC match, pension and stock purchase plans; dependent care reimbursement account; back-up child/elder care; adoption, surrogacy, and doula reimbursement; educational assistance, including select programs fully paid; a robust wellness program with financial incentives.In addition, PNC generally provides the following paid time off, depending on your eligibility: maternity and/or parental leave; up to 11 paid holidays each year; 9 occasional absence days each year, unless otherwise required by law; between 15 to 25 vacation days each year, depending on career level; and years of service.

To learn more about these and other programs, including benefits for full time and part-time employees, visit pncthrive.com.

Disability Accommodations Statement

jobs.title: Directs Investment Analyst
jobs.description:
Firm Overview: 

Star Mountain is a rapidly growing, specialized private investment firm focused on the large and less competitive lower middle-market (“LMM”), comprised of businesses that generally have between $5 million and $50 million in annual EBITDA. Star Mountain employs three complementary strategies: (i) private credit, (ii) private equity and (iii) secondary private credit and private equity fund investing, all focused within the U.S. LMM. Star Mountain’s track record, business model, growth trajectory and award-winning culture provide compelling career opportunities for driven and talented individuals. Culture is a core principle which includes long-term alignment of interest with 100% of U.S. employees participating in investment profits. Star Mountain has ~$4.5 billion in assets under management (committed capital including debt facilities as of 10/31/25) and expects strong continued growth based on its performance, distinctive investment approach and its diversified investor base of domestic and global institutions (including public pensions, corporate pensions, insurance companies, commercial banks, endowments, foundations and family offices) and individuals. Star Mountain values team players with positive, solutions-based attitudes complemented by a strong work ethic and is heavily committed to long-term training and career development. Star Mountain is employee-owned with material long-term wealth creation opportunity for dedicated, entrepreneurial professionals. Dynamic team of 120+ full-time people and 40+ Senior Advisors / Operating Partners. The senior team has an average of 30 years of investment experience including prior leadership experience at Blackstone / GSO, Fortress, Goldman Sachs, Citigroup, J.P. Morgan and Merrill Lynch.

Position Description:

  • Support the analysis and execution of flexible capital solution investments including private credit, structured equity and select control buyouts in predominantly U.S. based businesses that have between $5 and $50 million in EBITDA.
  • You will compile investment committee memos/presentations, create financial models, contribute to transaction analysis and assist with the drafting of term sheets and legal documentation.
  • On-going monitoring of portfolio companies including enhancing financial and KPI reporting to drive data-driven insights and progress towards outlined value creation initiatives
  • Add value to other aspects of Star Mountain’s overall business as an aligned partner assisting in building a leading private investment business
  • Assist with junior team recruiting, training and development

Qualifications:

  • 1-4 years of relevant experience in Investment Banking, Private Equity and Private Credit experience investing to U.S. companies with between $5 to $50 million of EBITDA
  • Demonstrated breadth of experience in executing leveraged finance transactions across industries
  • Strong quantitative aptitude as well as communication skills, both written and verbal
  • Proven ability to take on material responsibility and accountability for transaction execution, asset management and outcomes
  • High attention to detail and proficiency with Microsoft product suite (e.g.; PowerPoint, Excel)
  • Team player with a positive attitude and great people skills.
  • Willingness to travel nationwide as needed for new investment due diligence, portfolio company board meetings and investor relations initiatives / events
  • Experience training and developing junior talent
  • Strong culture carrier with a great attitude.
  • Timing: Immediate.
  • Location: In-person, based in Tampa, Florida.

Compensation:

The exact salary will be determined based on the candidate's qualifications, experience, and performance during the selection process. Employees are eligible for an annual discretionary bonus and other economics. Total compensation may vary by role, location, department, and individual performance. The firm is 100% employee owned providing for material economic wealth creation opportunity.

Benefits:

Star Mountain Capital is committed to a healthy and engaged workforce. Our benefits are constantly evolving to support our employees’ needs today and in the future. We offer a highly competitive medical, dental, and vision package. Select other benefits include: 50% coverage on monthly gym memberships, a cell phone stipend, tuition support of job-related designations and learning and development opportunities, and other bespoke benefits

Interview Process

Thank you for your interest in a role at Star Mountain Capital. Please note that due to the high volume of applications we receive, we may not be able to respond to each one individually. If your resume is a match for the role you’ve applied for or other potential opportunities, we will reach to start the conversation. If you do not hear from us immediately, please understand that we are carefully reviewing all applications and will keep your resume on file for future opportunities that align with your experience and qualifications.

Please submit your resume through LinkedIn and if you would like to include additional information, such as a cover letter, previous projects, transcripts or references, you may submit this to careers@StarMountainCapital.com with the subject line: “Client Services Associate – Tampa, Florida – [Your Name]".

Investing in the Growth Engine of America ®

Note: Detailed award eligibility requirements for Crain’s can be found here and for Pensions & Investments here.

Investing in the Growth Engine of America ®

www.StarMountainCapital.com

jobs.title: Finance and Operations (Multiple Roles) | Private Credit & Private Equity
jobs.description:
Primary Responsibilities:

  • Liaise with third-party fund administrators to oversee the preparation and review of NAV packages, financial statements, investor capital statements, and related deliverables.
  • Review capital call and distribution notices, management fee and carried interest calculations, and investor reporting materials.
  • Prepare internal financial analyses and reporting packages for senior management and investment professionals.
  • Support the calculation and verification of performance metrics, including IRRs, multiples, DPI, etc.
  • Support ongoing investment monitoring, including compliance with loan and partnership agreements.
  • Liaise with external auditors and tax advisors to support annual audits, tax filings, and investor reporting.
  • Ensure proper documentation and compliance with accounting policies and fund governing documents.
  • Monitor fund cash positions, manage liquidity forecasting, and assist with credit facility utilization and covenant compliance.
  • Support treasury operations, including funding allocations and vendor disbursements.
  • Identify and implement best practices to enhance efficiency, strengthen internal controls, and streamline reporting workflows.
  • Review and interpret partnership agreements, private placement memorandums, and related legal documents for financial implications.
  • Assist in preparing quarterly investor communications, fund performance summaries, and related materials.
  • Support investor onboarding, including document review and coordination through the investor portal.

Required Qualifications:

  • Multiple roles ranging from 2 years to 10+ of fund accounting or financial reporting experience within private equity or private credit, either through audit or fund administration; Big 4 experience a plus.
  • Enthusiastic and motivated to work in an entrepreneurial, fast-paced environment building a growing organization.
  • Loan experience, including working with credit funds, Business Development Companies (BDC), and/or Small Business Investment Company (SBIC) fund experience, a plus.
  • Strong knowledge of partnership accounting, US GAAP, and financial reporting.
  • Advanced Excel skills are a plus.
  • Proven team player and self-starter with the ability to think independently and work autonomously.
  • Excellent interpersonal skills, with the ability to interact effectively with external parties and colleagues at all levels.
  • Tax structuring knowledge a plus.
  • Ability to analyze investment and fund legal documents.
  • Positive attitude and commitment to high-quality execution.
  • Must provide strong professional references (from peers, subordinates, and superiors) when appropriate.
  • Location: Tampa, Florida
  • Timing: Immediate

Compensation:

The exact salary will be determined based on the candidate's qualifications, experience, and performance during the selection process. Employees are eligible for an annual discretionary bonus and other economics. Total compensation may vary by role, location, department, and individual performance. The firm is 100% employee owned providing for material economic wealth creation opportunity.

Star Mountain Capital is committed to a healthy and engaged workforce. Our benefits are constantly evolving to support our employees’ needs today and in the future. We offer a highly competitive medical, dental, and vision package. Select other benefits include: 50% coverage on monthly gym memberships, a cell phone stipend, tuition support of job-related designations and learning and development opportunities, and other bespoke benefits.

Dynamic team of 120+ full-time people and 40+ Senior Advisors / Operating Partners. The senior team has an average of 30 years of investment experience including prior leadership experience at Blackstone / GSO, Fortress, Goldman Sachs, Citigroup, J.P. Morgan and Merrill Lynch.

Position Description

  • Originate and lead the underwriting and execution of private credit investments, including senior, unitranche, and structured capital solutions, in predominantly U.S.-based businesses with $5 to $50 million of EBITDA.
  • Focus on structuring investments to optimize risk-adjusted returns, with an emphasis on downside protection, credit quality, and cash flow durability.
  • Lead negotiation of definitive credit documentation, including credit agreements, intercreditor agreements, and related financing documents.
  • Add value to broader firm initiatives, contributing to the continued buildout of Star Mountain’s credit platform and investment capabilities.
  • Cultivate and broaden relationships with deal flow origination sources, financial sponsors, and prospective limited partners.
  •  Conduct ongoing monitoring of portfolio companies, including identifying and managing credit risks, anticipating/documenting credit events, and proactively working with borrowers on performance and liquidity needs.
  • Collaborate with portfolio companies and sponsors to support refinancings, amendments, and other capital solutions.
  •  Assist with junior team recruiting, training, and development.

Required Qualifications

  • 12+ years of relevant experience in private credit, direct lending, or cash-flow based lending to U.S. middle market companies ($5–$50 million EBITDA).
  • Demonstrated experience originating and leading the underwriting and execution of leveraged finance transactions.
  • Strong quantitative and credit analysis skills, along with excellent written and verbal communication abilities.
  • Proven ability to take responsibility for transaction underwriting, portfolio management, and credit outcomes.
  • Experience negotiating loan agreements, intercreditor agreements, and related credit documentation.
  • Team player with strong interpersonal skills and a collaborative mindset.
  • Willingness to travel nationwide for origination, diligence, and portfolio-related activities.
  • Timing: Immediate.
  • Location: Remote

Compensation

The exact salary will be determined based on the candidate's qualifications, experience, and performance during the selection process. Employees are eligible for an annual discretionary bonus and other economics. Total compensation may vary by role, location, department, and individual performance. The firm is 100% employee owned providing for material economic wealth creation opportunity.

Benefits

Star Mountain Capital is committed to a healthy and engaged workforce. Our benefits are constantly evolving to support our employees’ needs today and in the future. We offer a highly competitive medical, dental, and vision package. Select other benefits include: 50% coverage on monthly gym memberships, a cell phone stipend, tuition support of job-related designations and learning and development opportunities, and other bespoke benefits.

Please submit resume through LinkedIn. If we require any additional information, we will reach out directly.

jobs.description:
Firm Overview

Star Mountain is a rapidly growing, specialized private investment firm focused on the large and less competitive lower middle-market (“LMM”), comprised of businesses that generally have between $5 million and $50 million in annual EBITDA. Star Mountain employs three complementary strategies: (i) private credit, (ii) private equity and (iii) secondary private credit and private equity fund investing, all focused within the U.S. LMM. Star Mountain’s track record, business model, growth trajectory and award-winning culture provide compelling career opportunities for driven and talented individuals. Culture is a core principle which includes long-term alignment of interest with 100% of U.S. employees participating in investment profits. Star Mountain has ~$4.5 billion in assets under management (committed capital including debt facilities as of 10/31/25) and expects strong continued growth based on its performance, distinctive investment approach and its diversified investor base of domestic and global institutions (including public pensions, corporate pensions, insurance companies, commercial banks, endowments, foundations and family offices) and individuals. Star Mountain values team players with positive, solutions-based attitudes complemented by a strong work ethic and is heavily committed to long-term training and career development. Star Mountain is employee-owned with material long-term wealth creation opportunity for dedicated, entrepreneurial professionals. Dynamic team of 120+ full-time people and 40+ Senior Advisors / Operating Partners. The senior team has an average of 30 years of investment experience including prior leadership experience at Blackstone / GSO, Fortress, Goldman Sachs, Citigroup, J.P. Morgan and Merrill Lynch.

Position Description

  • Originate, evaluate, and lead the execution of control and minority equity investments in predominantly U.S.-based businesses with $5 to $50 million of EBITDA.
  • Drive the full investment lifecycle, including sourcing, due diligence, structuring, valuation, and closing of transactions across a variety of industries.
  • Lead negotiation of definitive purchase agreements, shareholder agreements, and related equity documentation.
  • Partner with management teams and boards to develop and execute value creation strategies, including operational improvements, growth initiatives, and strategic add-on acquisitions.
  • Play a leading role in evaluating and executing secondary investments, including acquisitions of interests in private equity funds and portfolios of operating companies.
  • Add value to broader firm initiatives, contributing to the continued buildout of Star Mountain’s private equity platform and investment capabilities.
  • Cultivate and expand relationships with intermediaries, company executives, co-investors, and limited partners to enhance proprietary deal flow and capital formation efforts.
  • Actively monitor portfolio company performance, working closely with management and boards to drive strategic initiatives, manage risks, and optimize exit outcomes.
  • Assist with recruiting, mentoring, and development of junior investment professionals.

Required Qualifications

  • 12+ years of experience in private equity, with a focus on leveraged buyouts of lower middle market businesses ($5–$50 million EBITDA).
  • Demonstrated experience leading the underwriting and execution of control-oriented equity investments across industries.
  • Strong financial modeling, valuation, and analytical capabilities, combined with excellent written and verbal communication skills.
  • Proven ability to take ownership of transactions from sourcing through exit, including portfolio company oversight and value creation execution.
  • Experience negotiating purchase agreements, equity structures, and governance frameworks.
  • Strong interpersonal skills with the ability to build relationships with management teams, advisors, and investors.
  • Willingness to travel nationwide for deal sourcing, diligence, board participation, and investor engagement.
  • Timing: Immediate.
  • Location: Remote

Compensation

The exact salary will be determined based on the candidate's qualifications, experience, and performance during the selection process. Employees are eligible for an annual discretionary bonus and other economics. Total compensation may vary by role, location, department, and individual performance. The firm is 100% employee owned providing for material economic wealth creation opportunity.

Benefits

Star Mountain Capital is committed to a healthy and engaged workforce. Our benefits are constantly evolving to support our employees’ needs today and in the future. We offer a highly competitive medical, dental, and vision package. Select other benefits include: 50% coverage on monthly gym memberships, a cell phone stipend, tuition support of job-related designations and learning and development opportunities, and other bespoke benefits.

Interview Process

Thank you for your interest in a role at Star Mountain Capital. Please note that due to the high volume of applications we receive, we may not be able to respond to each one individually. If your resume is a match for the role you’ve applied for or other potential opportunities, we will reach to start the conversation. If you do not hear from us immediately, please understand that we are carefully reviewing all applications and will keep your resume on file for future opportunities that align with your experience and qualifications.

Please submit your resume through LinkedIn and if you would like to include additional information, such as a cover letter, previous projects, transcripts or references, you may submit this to careers@StarMountainCapital.com with the subject line: “Directs Investment Analyst – Tampa, Florida – [Your Name]".

jobs.description:

Star Mountain is a rapidly growing, specialized private investment firm focused on the large and less competitive lower middle-market (“LMM”), comprised of businesses that generally have between $5 million and $50 million in annual EBITDA. Star Mountain employs three complementary strategies: (i) private credit, (ii) private equity and (iii) secondary private credit and private equity fund investing, all focused within the U.S. LMM. Star Mountain’s track record, business model, growth trajectory and award-winning culture provide compelling career opportunities for driven and talented individuals.
