Star Mountain has ~$4.5 billion in assets under management (committed capital including debt facilities as of 10/31/25) and expects strong continued growth based on its performance, distinctive investment approach and its diversified investor base of domestic and global institutions (including public pensions, corporate pensions, insurance companies, commercial banks, endowments, foundations and family offices) and individuals. Star Mountain values team players with positive, solutions-based attitudes complemented by a strong work ethic and is heavily committed to long-term training and career development. Star Mountain is employee-owned with material long-term wealth creation opportunity for dedicated, entrepreneurial professionals. Dynamic team of 120+ full-time people and 40+ Senior Advisors / Operating Partners. The senior team has an average of 30 years of investment experience including prior leadership experience at Blackstone / GSO, Fortress, Goldman Sachs, Citigroup, J.P. Morgan and Merrill Lynch.

Position Description:

Portfolio Company Oversight

  • Assist senior Underwriting and Portfolio Management professionals in managing portfolio company relationships and preparing for board meetings, including direct support on operational reviews, performance improvement initiatives, and management accountability tracking
  • Review and analyze monthly financials, quarterly covenant compliance certifications, annual audited financial statements from portfolio companies, with a focus on identifying emerging risks, liquidity constraints, and operational inefficiencies
  • Analyze company-level operating metrics and financials data to disaggregate drivers of under/outperformance relative to budget and prior year, developing actionable insights to support margin improvement, cost optimization, and revenue enhancement initiatives
  • Support value creation initiatives (i.e., Getting Middle Market Ready, working capital management, and reporting, ), including operational benchmarking, KPI enhancement, and execution tracking across portfolio companies
  • Provide analytical and operational support during periods of underperformance or disruption, including liquidity stress, covenant pressure, management transitions, or macro-driven volatility (light crisis management)
  • Assist in evaluating and supporting restructuring, reorganization, and turnaround initiatives, including capital structure optimization, cost rationalization efforts, and strategic realignment analyses.

Internal Reporting

  • Maintain historical portfolio company financial, capital structure and qualitative attributes in firm data management platforms (S&P Global iLEVEL and WSO), ensuring data accuracy to support operational decision-making and performance diagnostics
  • Support valuations processes on portfolio company securities on a quarterly basis, including scenario and downside analyses reflecting operational and restructuring considerations
  • Prepare quarterly and semi-annual credit and equity reviews on individual portfolio companies, highlighting operational trends, execution risks, and value creation progress
  • Craft thoughtful responses to investor requests related to portfolio company security details and performance, including explanations of operational initiatives, performance remediation plans, and restructuring outcomes where applicable

Portfolio Financing and Amendment Support

  • Support drafting and presentation of internal amendment and financing memoranda for relevant approvals from Chief Credit Officer and Investment Committee.
  • Prepare detailed scenario-based spreadsheet models to project portfolio company operating and financial performance, including downside, stress-case, and restructuring scenarios, as well as credit statistics and projected returns
  • Assist investment analysts in completing pre-investment and post-close workflows, procedures and documentation, with added involvement in post-close performance monitoring, amendment execution, and reorganization processes
  • Support cross-functional coordination with legal, restructuring advisors, consultants, and portfolio company management teams during amendments, workouts, and operational improvement initiatives

Required Qualifications:

  • 5+ years of investment-related (private equity, private credit), corporate banking, or other finance-related experience (investment banking, accounting, audit, consulting), with exposure to portfolio monitoring, operational analysis, restructurings, or performance improvement initiatives preferred
  • Microsoft Excel power user, ability to build 3 statement LBO models, including experience with scenario modeling, sensitivity analysis, and operational KPIs
  • Excellent organizational skills with a proactive and detail-oriented approach to managing multiple tasks and priorities
  • Strong verbal, written and interpersonal communication skills
  • Team player with a positive attitude and great people skills

Timing: Immediate

Location: Tampa, Florida

Compensation:

The exact salary will be determined based on the candidate's qualifications, experience, and performance during the selection process. Employees are eligible for an annual discretionary bonus and other economics. Total compensation may vary by role, location, department, and individual performance. The firm is 100% employee owned providing for material economic wealth creation opportunity.

Benefits:

Star Mountain Capital is committed to a healthy and engaged workforce. Our benefits are constantly evolving to support our employees’ needs today and in the future. We offer a highly competitive medical, dental, and vision package. Select other benefits include: 50% coverage on monthly gym memberships, a cell phone stipend, tuition support of job-related designations and learning and development opportunities, and other bespoke benefits.

Please submit resume through LinkedIn. If we require any additional information, we will reach out directly.

jobs.title: Vice President, Private Equity
jobs.description:
Position Description

The Vice President, Private Equity will be responsible for helping lead the evaluation, execution and management of private equity investments, including control buyouts, structured equity investments and other flexible equity-oriented capital solutions, primarily in U.S.-based lower middle market businesses.

Responsibilities:

  • Lead analysis and execution of private equity investments, including control buyouts, structured equity investments and other flexible equity-oriented capital solutions, in predominantly U.S.-based lower middle market businesses.
  • Oversee and execute all phases of due diligence, including business, industry, competitive, customer, financial, accounting, tax, legal, regulatory and management diligence, culminating in investment recommendations and presentations to the firm’s Investment Committee.
  • Help lead transaction structuring and negotiation of letters of intent, purchase agreements, financing documents, management equity arrangements and other transaction-related legal documentation.
  • Develop trusted relationships with management teams, founders, independent sponsors, operating executives and advisors, and provide strategic guidance throughout the investment lifecycle.
  • Support ongoing management of portfolio companies, including enhancing financial and KPI reporting, tracking performance against value creation plans, supporting add-on acquisition activity, and helping drive data-driven insights and progress against strategic initiatives.
  • Assist in the development of investment theses, target pipelines and executive relationships in attractive lower middle market subsectors.
  • Add value to other aspects of Star Mountain’s overall business as an aligned partner and help build a leading private investment business.
  • Cultivate and broaden relationships with intermediaries, independent sponsors, executives, operating advisors, lenders, prospective co-investors, prospective limited partners and third-party diligence providers.
  • Assist with junior team recruiting, training and development.

Qualifications

  • 5+ years of relevant experience in private equity, with meaningful experience evaluating and executing investments in U.S. lower middle market companies.
  • Demonstrated experience executing transactions across multiple industries, including financial modeling, valuation, diligence management, investment committee materials and transaction structuring.
  • Experience reviewing and negotiating transaction documentation, including letters of intent, purchase agreements, debt financing documents, management equity plans and other equity-related documentation.
  • Strong quantitative aptitude and excellent written and verbal communication skills.
  • Proven ability to take on material responsibility and accountability for transaction execution, asset management and outcomes.
  • High attention to detail and proficiency with Microsoft Office, including Excel, PowerPoint and Word.
  • Team player with a positive attitude, strong interpersonal skills and sound judgment.
  • Willingness to travel nationwide as needed for new investment due diligence, portfolio company board meetings, management meetings and investor relations initiatives/events.
  • Experience training and developing junior talent.
  • Strong culture carrier with high integrity, humility, intellectual curiosity, accountability and a collaborative mindset.

jobs.title: 2025-2026 Private Equity Intern
jobs.description:
We are global private markets specialists delivering tailored investment solutions, advisory services, and impactful, data driven insights to the world’s investors. Leveraging the power of our platform and our peerless intelligence across sectors, strategies, and geographies, we help identify the advantages and the answers our clients need to succeed.

The team you’ll join

StepStone’s Private Equity (PE) team diligences and invests in opportunities across the private equity landscape. The team will invest directly in companies alongside other sponsors, complete LP and GP - led secondary transactions, and evaluate primary investments on behalf of StepStone’s advisory and discretionary clients. Through analysis of leveraged buyout deals and funds, StepStone’s PE team works across a wide array of industries and capital structures.

About the role

StepStone is currently seeking penultimate and final year undergraduate students or students enrolled in a master’s degree with excellent quantitative, qualitative and interpersonal skills for an intern position on StepStone’s Private Equity Team. StepStone offers Interns a unique opportunity to achieve a valuable learning experience working across the private equity industry. Through analysis of private equity funds and investments, Interns gain exposure to a wide array of industries and capital structures, and become accustomed to a variety of private equity funds and investment strategies.

What you’ll do

Interns will work in a team environment to target and perform due diligence, including financial modelling and preparation of investment memorandums, on opportunities across the Private Equity team’s three investment verticals: private equity fund investments, co-investment transactions and secondaries transactions.

Key responsibilities

  • Analyze and conduct diligence on co-investment opportunities and secondary transactions
  • Interact with fund managers/portfolio company management teams to conduct in-depth portfolio and asset-level underwriting and analysis
  • Perform quantitative and qualitative research and financial modeling/analysis on companies and assets across industries and geographies
  • Conduct due diligence on potential fund investments (team, strategy, performance), and synthesize findings in an investment memorandum to be presented at the Firm’s Investment Committee
  • Speak with senior executives to gather insights regarding the style and effectiveness of target fund managers
  • Perform market research using publicly available information and proprietary databases to identify, quantify and analyze macroeconomic trends across a variety of industry sectors

What we’re looking for

  • Pursuing a bachelor’s or master’s degree, expected to graduate in 2026 or later
  • Completed at least one relevant 3- or 6-month internship, and able to work on-site at StepStone’s Beijing office at least four days per week 
  • High proficiency in MS Word, Power Point and Excel
  • Exceptional written and verbal communication skills
  • Demonstrable analytical capabilities, including strong quantitative/modeling skills
  • Ability to represent the Firm credibly in written and interpersonal communications
  • Flexibility-StepStone is a dynamic and entrepreneurial organization; all professionals are expected to perform multiple functions as the firm continues to grow and develop
  • Promote and demonstrate a positive, team-oriented attitude
  • Foreign language skills (desirable)

Why join us?

At StepStone, we foster a supportive and inclusive team culture where collaboration and learning are the core of what we do. Our collegial atmosphere encourages teamwork, mentorship, and professional development. This summer internship is a great opportunity to gain practical experience and develop critical skills that will help you launch a successful career in Private Equity. 

#LI-Hybrid

At StepStone, we believe that our people are our most important asset and crucial to our success.  We are an Equal Opportunity Employer that strives to create an environment that empowers our employees and allows them to be heard, regardless of title or tenure.  Our organizational community features multiple Employment Resource Groups as well as mentorship programs to enhance the employee experience for all.  

As an Equal Opportunity Employer, StepStone does not discriminate on the basis of race, creed, color, religion, sex, national origin, citizenship status, age, disability, marital status, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, genetic information or any other characteristic protected by law.

Candidates must be at least 18 years old to apply.

Developing People at StepStone

jobs.title: 2027 Private Equity Infrastructure & Real Assets Summer Analyst
jobs.description:
We are global private markets specialists delivering tailored investment solutions, advisory services, and impactful, data driven insights to the world’s investors. Leveraging the power of our platform and our peerless intelligence across sectors, strategies, and geographies, we help identify the advantages and the answers our clients need to succeed.

Application deadline: Rolling basis

The team you’ll join

StepStone Infrastructure and Real Assets (SIRA), established in 2014, is part of StepStone Group, specializing in private market investments across infrastructure and real assets. As of December 31, 2025, SIRA had responsibility for approximately US$132 billion in capital, deploying an average of over US$13 billion annually across primaries, secondaries, and co-investments. The global team comprises more than 75 investment professionals, offering deep expertise across infrastructure and real assets sub-sectors, including energy, transport, natural capital, agriculture, and other essential assets, with a presence across North America, Europe, and Australia.

About the role

StepStone seeks motivated undergraduates graduating between December 2027 and June 2028 to join our SIRA team as Summer Analysts. This role offers a valuable opportunity to gain insights into the private equity industry. As a Summer Analyst, you will learn about various sectors such as transportation, telecommunications, utilities, power, energy, logistics, renewables, social projects, sustainability, and impact investing. Additionally, you will be exposed to diverse companies, assets, and capital structures, providing you with a comprehensive understanding of infrastructure investments.

What you’ll do

As a Summer Analyst, you’ll work collaboratively to conduct due diligence on opportunities within SIRA's investment verticals, including infrastructure fund investments, co-investments, and secondaries. You will also prepare and present recommendations with the investment team for SIRA's Investment Committee.

Key responsibilities

  • Conduct thorough analysis and due diligence on co-investment opportunities, as well as primary and secondary transactions in private equity, specifically within the infrastructure, agriculture, and natural resources sectors
  • Perform financial modeling and analytical tasks including creating or modifying financial models, analyzing financial statements and forecasts, preparing valuation bridges, running discounted cash flow analyses, comparing public companies, and conducting sensitivity/scenario analyses
  • Develop and present a well-constructed argument regarding investment decisions, effectively communicating these arguments in written materials such as Microsoft Word documents and PowerPoint presentations

  • Undergraduate student graduating anywhere from December 2027 through June 2028
  • GPA of 3.5 or higher
  • Strong proficiency in Microsoft Word, PowerPoint and Excel
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills
  • Demonstrable analytical capabilities, including strong quantitative/modeling skills
  • Collaborative mindset with a proactive and team-oriented approach

Why join us?

At StepStone, we foster a supportive and inclusive team culture where collaboration and learning are the core of what we do. Our collegial atmosphere encourages teamwork, mentorship, and professional development. This summer internship is a great opportunity to gain practical experience and develop critical skills that will help you launch a successful career in private markets.

The team you’ll join

StepStone’s Private Equity (PE) team diligences and invests in opportunities across the private equity landscape. The team will invest directly in companies alongside other sponsors, complete LP and GP-led secondary transactions, and evaluate primary investment capabilities on behalf of StepStone’s advisory and discretionary clients. Through analysis of leveraged buyout deals and funds, StepStone’s PE team works across a wide array of industries and capital structures.

About the role

StepStone is looking for highly motivated undergraduate students graduating between December 2027 and June 2028 to join our Private Equity team as Summer Analysts. This internship offers an exciting opportunity to gain hands-on experience across the private equity industry. Summer Analysts will analyze leveraged buyouts, growth equity and distressed private equity funds and investments. Through this experience, Summer Analysts will develop a strong understanding of various industries, capital structures, and private equity funds and investment strategies.

As a Summer Analyst, you’ll work collaboratively in a team environment to conduct due diligence, financial modeling and preparation of investment memorandums across our investment verticals: private equity fund investments, co-investments, and secondaries transactions.   • Conduct due diligence on potential fund investments (team, strategy, performance), and synthesize findings in an investment memorandum to be presented at the Firm’s Investment Committee
  • Speak with senior executives to gather insights regarding the style and effectiveness of target fund managers
  • Perform market research using publicly available information and proprietary databases to identify, quantify and analyze macroeconomic trends across a variety of industry sectors
  • Construct an argument around an opinion and effectively communicate that argument

  • Undergraduate student graduating anywhere from December 2027 through June 2028
  • GPA of 3.5 or higher

Our collegial atmosphere encourages teamwork, mentorship, and professional development. jobs.title: Analyst/Associate Product Management - Private Wealth
jobs.description:
We are global private markets specialists delivering tailored investment solutions, advisory services, and impactful, data driven insights to the world’s investors. Leveraging the power of our platform and our peerless intelligence across sectors, strategies, and geographies, we help identify the advantages and the answers our clients need to succeed.

Position Overview:         

StepStone Private Wealth Solutions is seeking an Analyst/Associate with excellent quantitative, qualitative, and interpersonal skills to support the product management efforts of financial platforms globally as they consider the suite of funds under management. The team oversees and manages data across five portfolio strategies and controls the information being shared externally.

The Analyst/Associate’s primary role is to draft high-quality, consistent responses, fund materials, and other analyses in response to requests from current and prospective platforms. The Analyst/Associate will work closely with the existing members of the product management team as they learn the process and expectations with the opportunity to begin collaborating with and have exposure to all levels across the firm. The candidate is expected to show a willingness to learn the underlying products and the comparable product landscape to support these efforts appropriately. The person should also have a basic understanding and interest in the private markets investment space (specifically, private equity, infrastructure, real estate, and private debt), strong interpersonal skills and project management skills. The candidate should be highly analytical and naturally curious to understand the underlying data. Strong written communication, organization, attention to detail and relationship building skills will be necessary to succeed in this role.         

Essential Job Functions: 

  • Responsible for completing written responses to inquiries from prospective platforms and existing clients, including writing and editing content, working with cross-functional teams to obtain the necessary information, and ensuring that the final product is accurate and delivered on time.
  • Critically assess the accuracy and relevance of content provided by cross-functional team members in response to DDQs/RFPs.
  • Contribute to the refinement and implementation of internal AI initiatives designed to pre-populate and streamline questionnaire responses
  • Manage and maintain the private wealth central database of responses to ensure content is kept up to date.
  • Respond to ad hoc requests initiated by platforms seeking deeper analysis of performance metrics, portfolio construction, or underlying investments.
  • Collaborate with portfolio managers, BD leadership, investment, legal, compliance, fund accounting, and portfolio analytics teams to synthesize insights across performance drivers, capital deployment dynamics, fundraising trends, portfolio evolution, and competitive positioning.
  • Maintain a high standard of data integrity and presentation quality across all portfolio-related deliverables.
  • Contribute to the development of portfolio narratives and reusable content for quarterly updates and platform communications.
  • Assist with various projects to streamline internal processes.
  • Strong ability to focus on the task at hand with an attention of the final deadline associated with the project or deliverable.

Education and/or Work Experience Requirements:         

  • Undergraduate degree / higher education qualification in finance, business, data science, or liberal arts.
  • Experience with related financial services (private equity, investment banking, asset management, investor relations and/or consulting) is a plus.
  • Highly motivated self-starter, with demonstrated history of taking initiative, being adaptive in a fast-changing environment, and working independently.

Required Knowledge, Skills, and Abilities:          

  • Candidate should be an energetic, self-motivated, and team-oriented individual who thrives in high-performance environments and maintains composure and precision under tight deadlines.
  • Demonstrated intellectual curiosity and commitment to developing deep private markets expertise.
  • Demonstrated interest in financial technology, automation, and AI-enabled workflow optimization.
  • Excellent report writing and communication skills.
  •  High level of confidentiality and understanding regulatory boundaries with sharing information.
  • Strong proficiency with Microsoft Excel, Word, and PowerPoint.
  • Ability to be flexible with changing priorities and company growth.
  • Ability to take ownership and accountability.

jobs.description:

Position Overview:

StepStone is currently seeking candidates with excellent quantitative, qualitative and interpersonal skills for an Analyst position on StepStone’s Real Estate Advisory Team. The Analyst’s primary role is to engage in sector-focused research on various areas of the real estate private equity industry such as opportunistic, value-add, core and credit. The Analyst will work in a team environment with other Research team members to target and perform due diligence on selected real estate private equity fund managers; analyze PPMs, financial statements and investment returns; and conduct reference calls and personal interviews. In addition, the Analyst will work with the co-investment and secondary teams to source, diligence and execute transactions. The Analyst will have the opportunity to present recommendations, with the investment team, to the Real Estate Investment Committee.

StepStone prides itself on the Firm’s collegial atmosphere, encouraging team-building initiatives and a collaborative approach to accomplishing projects. The Analyst will have exposure to all levels of the Firm, from Partners to fellow Analysts, daily. The Analyst will often have opportunities to interact with many of the top fund managers in the real estate private equity industry.

The ideal candidate will possess a bachelor’s degree and backgrounds in real estate, private equity, investment banking, consulting or other relevant industries. The Analyst will adhere to the highest ethical standards, as well as demonstrate maturity, reliability and attentiveness to detail.

Essential Job Functions:

  • Perform market research using publicly available information and proprietary databases to identify, quantify and analyze macroeconomic trends across a variety of industry sectors
  • Interpret financial analyses prepared by target fund managers, including investment track records, financial statement analysis and fund models
  • Support senior Advisory staff on sourcing and managing the inflow of investment opportunities
  • Assess marketing and supporting materials of prospective fund managers to identify key value drivers and important areas for further investigation
  • Conduct phone interviews with senior executives to gather insights and information regarding the style and effectiveness of target fund managers
  • Form an independent opinion about the attractiveness of a sector or investment opportunity
  • Efficiently communicate a position verbally and in written materials, including defending a position in a team environment such as Investment Committee meetings
  • Use creativity to source and analyze data and to test hypotheses regarding attractiveness of specific sectors and investment opportunities
  • Prepare for and participate in meetings with managers and conduct on-going due diligence
  • Perform financial modeling and sensitivity analyses related to co-investment and secondary opportunities
  • Draft investment memorandums and presentations related to fund, co-investment and secondary diligences

Education and/or Work Experience Requirements:

  • BA/BS in in Real Estate, Private Equity, Finance, Economics, Accounting, Law or equivalent relevant experience
  • Demonstrated track record of academic and professional success
  • Prior internships or work experience in private equity real estate, investment banking, consulting or other relevant experience
  • Availability to travel, primarily domestically

Other Knowledge, Skills and Abilities:

  • Flexibility to work within StepStone’s dynamic and entrepreneurial organization
  • Developing investment judgment and general understanding of risk/reward trade-offs
  • Ability to represent the Firm credibly in written and interpersonal communications
  • Demonstrable analytical capabilities, including strong quantitative/modeling skills
  • High proficiency in Word, PowerPoint and Excel
  • Applied experience in valuation and investment analysis
  • Enrollment in CFA program a plus
  • Promote and demonstrate a positive, team-oriented attitude

Position Overview
The Associate / Senior Associate will be an integral member of StepStone Enterprise Services’ Product Management team. The role will support StepStone’s infrastructure and real assets platform investing in co-investment, secondary and primary infrastructure and real asset transactions globally. Responsibilities will consist of providing support to senior specialists for infrastructure and real asset products across the entire fund lifecycle from fund launches and capital formation to existing investor relations activities, including helping to create new content, responding to investor requests, and supporting investor roadshows/events. Periodic interactions with limited partners and potential investors may also be required.
Essential Job Functions:
  • Maintain and implement client service protocols to provide a high quality, consistent investor experience, including the application of standardized best practices gleaned from across the platform
  • Compile and write content for investor communications and drafting external and internal speaking points

  • Understand and analyze infrastructure industry trends and conduct asset industry research
  • Coordination and logistics of client events, onsite meetings, webinar events, investor conferences
  • Coordinate with technology to enforce internal efficiencies

jobs.description:

Real Estate Private Equity Associate / Sr. Associate Role Overview

StepStone is currently seeking candidates with excellent quantitative, qualitative, and interpersonal skills for an Associate / Senior Associate position on StepStone’s Real Estate Investment Team. The primary role is to assist in the identification, evaluation, due diligence, and execution of real estate investments across geographies and asset classes. The Associate/Sr. Associate will work in a team environment with other team members to source, underwrite, and diligence new investment opportunities, negotiate acquisition terms, manage existing assets, and conduct asset dispositions. The Associate/Sr. Associate will have the opportunity to present recommendations, with the investment team, to the Real Estate Investment Committee.

StepStone prides itself on the Firm’s collegial atmosphere, encouraging team-building initiatives and a collaborative approach to accomplishing projects. The Associate/Sr. Associate will have exposure to all levels of the Firm, from Partners to fellow Associates/Sr. Associates, on a daily basis.

The ideal candidate will possess a bachelor’s degree and a background in real estate private equity/credit, investment banking, or other real estate- or finance-related industries. The Associate/Sr. Associate will adhere to the highest ethical standards, as well as demonstrate maturity, reliability, and attention to detail.

Essential Job Functions:

  • Identify, source, and evaluate high-potential acquisition opportunities across various real estate asset classes (residential, commercial, industrial, mixed-use, etc.), geographies, and risk-spectrums
  • Participate and lead in the underwriting and due diligence process for potential acquisitions, including financial analysis, structuring, market research, site visits, and document review
  • Perform financial modeling and sensitivity analysis related to investment opportunities
  • Prepare for and participate in meetings with managers and investors and conduct on-going due diligence
  • Form an independent opinion about the attractiveness of a sector or investment opportunity
  • Construct an argument around an opinion and efficiently communicate that argument both verbally and in written materials; be able to defend position in a team environment, including Investment Committee meetings
  • Draft investment memorandums and presentations related to acquisition opportunities
  • Assist with asset management duties, including financial analysis and investor reporting for existing investments
  • Support the fundraising process, including creating fundraising presentation materials and coordinating historical fund- and asset-level performance analysis
  • Develop and maintain a network of key relationships, including brokers, developers, institutional investors, and financial partners
  • Bring investments through the investment committee process, presenting acquisition opportunities and providing insights and recommendations

Education and/or Work Experience Requirements:

  • 4+ years of experience in real estate private equity/credit, investment banking, or related real estate finance field
  • BA/BS in Business, Finance, Economics, Accounting, Real Estate, or related field
  • Extensive experience in real estate acquisitions, including a proven track record of successfully identifying and closing investment opportunities
  • Takes pride of ownership in work, and work ethic
  • Ability to represent the Firm credibly in written and interpersonal communications
  • Demonstrable analytical capabilities, including strong quantitative/modeling skills
  • High proficiency in Word, PowerPoint, and Excel
  • Developing investment judgment and general understanding of risk/reward trade-offs
  • Flexibility to work within StepStone’s dynamic and entrepreneurial organization
  • Based in Chicago, with availability to travel, primarily domestically

Knowledge, Skills, and Ability Requirements:

  • Highly analytical with exceptional problem-solving skills
  • ARGUS experience optional but preferred
  • Promote and demonstrate a positive, team-oriented attitude

POSITION OVERVIEW

As part of StepStone’s Portfolio Analytics and Reporting team (‘SPAR’), the Junior Analyst will play a major role in data collection and governance related to venture capital portfolio companies. This information is critical for reports that drive investment decisions and portfolio tracking. This role will also assist with various performance analyses, projects, and ad-hoc requests. This will be a fast-paced and dynamic environment that is ideal for those who want to continuously tackle challenging problems and learn new things.

ESSENTIAL JOB FUNCTIONS:

  • Supports StepStone VC Investment and Business Intelligence Teams by maintaining internal investment database while overseeing data collection process. This includes, but is not limited to:
    • Maintenance of data fields and records to power investment reporting
    • Creation of new dataflows (SQL querying, data transformations
    • Generating reports to satisfy the quarterly needs of internal and external stakeholders
  • Assisting with LP/Investor data requests
  • Work with StepStone VC Investment Team and related technology teams to develop new internal process efficiencies involving data storage, collection, and utilization
  • Gain knowledge and experience working with data from private markets general partners and companies, including limited partnership and direct security investments
  • Performs other duties as requested or as responsibilities dictate

EDUCATION AND/OR WORK EXPERIENCE REQUIREMENTS:

  • Bachelor's degree in Finance, Economics, Accounting, or Data Science related field preferred.
  • Demonstrated record of academic success
  • Experience in financial services preferred, but not required

REQUIRED KNOWLEDGE, SKILLS, AND ABILITIES

  • Strong Microsoft Excel skills (mandatory); this role involves working heavily with datasets in Excel, and being comfortable with basic functions (VLOOKUP, SUMIFS, etc.) is necessary
  • Familiarity with relational databases and experience with basic SQL
  • Strong interpersonal skills
  • Strong attention to detail – data is sensitive so operating with strong attention to detail is key
  • Critical thinking – Thinking strategically about internal processes and striving for efficiency
  • Ability to meet tight deadlines, work extra hours, when necessary, manage multiple ongoing projects, and work well within a team
  • Ability to communicate in a clear and concise manner both verbally and in writing with your team

Salary - $25

jobs.description:

POSITION OVERVIEW:   
             
The Private Equity Analyst will engage broadly in various areas of the private equity industry, including manager and fund diligence for StepStone’s advisory practice, as well as diligence responsibilities for StepStone’s asset management business lines (i.e., co-investment and secondaries practices). 

ESSENTIAL JOB FUNCTIONS:

  • Work in a team environment with other investment team members to source, screen, and conduct due diligence on select private equity fund managers, co-investments, and secondaries
  • Perform quantitative and qualitative research and financial modeling/analysis on companies and assets across industries and geographies
  • Interact with fund managers/portfolio company management teams to conduct in-depth portfolio and asset-level underwriting and analysis
  • Analyze PPMs, financial statements, and investment returns
  • Conduct reference calls as a part of manager and asset due diligence
  • Perform market research using publicly available information and proprietary databases to identify, quantify and analyze macroeconomic trends across a variety of industry sectors
  • Prepare due diligence materials and Investment Committee memos
  • Present recommendations to the investment team and the Firm’s Investment Committee
  • Monitor the performance of the Firm’s existing investments

EDUCATION AND/OR WORK EXPERIENCE REQUIREMENTS:

  • Must be able to work legally in the U.S. without requiring visa sponsorship now or in the future
  • 1 year of investment banking, consulting, and/or private equity experience   
  • GPA of 3.5 or higher

REQUIRED KNOWLEDGE, SKILLS, AND ABILITIES: 

  • Excellent quantitative skills, including financial modeling, analytical and valuation skills for purpose of evaluating investment opportunities
  • Strong sense of risk/reward trade-offs, and an ability to display sound investment judgement
  • Self-starter, with strong ability to think creatively and conduct research to find solutions
  • Ability to communicate and present ideas effectively, both verbally and in writing
  • Capable of multi-tasking with strong attention to detail
  • Promote and demonstrate a positive, team-oriented attitude, with desire to work on various projects
  • High proficiency in Microsoft Word, Power Point and Excel
  • Fluent (written and spoken) English

jobs.title: Private Equity Analyst
jobs.description:
We are global private markets specialists delivering tailored investment solutions, advisory services, and impactful, data driven insights to the world’s investors. Leveraging the power of our platform and our peerless intelligence across sectors, strategies, and geographies, we help identify the advantages and the answers our clients need to succeed.

About StepStone:

StepStone (Nasdaq: STEP) is a global private markets specialist overseeing over US$723 billion of private capital allocations, including approximately US$199 billion of assets under management.

StepStone Private Equity provides its investors and clients with customized exposure to private equity through its global market relationships, deep research capabilities, granular due diligence, and structuring capabilities. StepStone builds this exposure through its commingled funds, customized funds, separately managed accounts, and investment advisory assignments.

Position Overview:

StepStone is currently seeking candidates with excellent quantitative, qualitative and interpersonal skills for an Analyst position on StepStone’s Private Equity Investment Team in the Firm’s Beijing or Singapore office. The Analyst’s primary role is to engage in sector-focused research on various areas of the private equity industry such as venture capital, growth, buyout and distressed strategies in Asia. In addition, the Analyst will work in a team environment with other investment team members to target and perform due diligence on selected private companies and private equity funds; analyze PPMs, financial statements and investment returns; and conduct reference calls and personal interviews. The Analyst will have the opportunity to present recommendations, with the investment team, to the Firm’s Investment Committee.

The ideal candidate will possess a bachelor’s degree and 1+ years of private equity, investment banking, consulting, or other relevant experience. The Analyst will adhere to the highest ethical standards, as well as demonstrate maturity, reliability, and attentiveness to detail.

Essential Job Functions:

· Perform market research using publicly available information and proprietary databases to identify, quantify and analyze macroeconomic trends across a variety of industry sectors

· Interpret financial analyses prepared by target fund managers, including investment track records and financial statement analysis; prepare valuation analyses on private companies using comparable public companies and precedent transactions

· Support senior research staff on sourcing and managing the inflow of investment opportunities

· Assess marketing and supporting materials of prospective fund managers to identify key value drivers and important areas for further investigation

· Form an independent opinion about the attractiveness of a sector or investment opportunity

· Construct an argument around an opinion and efficiently communicate that argument both verbally and in written materials; be able to defend position in a team environment, including Investment Committee meetings

· Use creativity to source and analyze data and to test hypotheses regarding attractiveness of specific sectors and investment opportunities

· Analyze and monitor the performance of the Firm's existing fund managers and underlying portfolio companies; prepare for and participate in meetings with assigned managers, and conduct on-going due diligence

Other Requirements:

· BA/BS from a leading university; top quartile academic background; demonstrated track record of academic and professional success

· Ability to represent the Firm credibly in written and interpersonal communications

· Demonstrable analytical capabilities, including strong quantitative/modeling skills

· Leadership potential

· High proficiency in Word, Power Point and Excel

· Flexibility—StepStone is a dynamic and entrepreneurial organization; all professionals are expected to perform multiple functions as the firm continues to grow and develop

· Travel around 5 days per month to regions throughout Asia (Greater China, Korea, Japan, India, Australia, SE Asia, etc.

jobs.title: Senior Analyst, Investor Relations & Reporting
jobs.description:
We are global private markets specialists delivering tailored investment solutions, advisory services, and impactful, data driven insights to the world’s investors. Leveraging the power of our platform and our peerless intelligence across sectors, strategies, and geographies, we help identify the advantages and the answers our clients need to succeed.

Position Overview

StepStone Mexico is looking for a detailed-orientated individual who will work for a team that supports quarterly/annual reporting, and operations for numerous vehicles investing in Private Markets that are listed in the Mexican stock exchanges. The person should be fluent in Spanish and have a solid understanding of investment and private equity markets, as well as strong writing and translation skills.

Primary Responsibilities Include:

  • Prepare quarterly/annual investor reports, cash flow notices and any other deliverables, including common representative reports or reports to meet regulatory requirements, related to vehicles listed in the Mexican stock exchanges
  • Support all internal/external communication efforts related to vehicles listed in the Mexican stock exchanges to ensure on time reporting, including communications with the trustee, common representative, independent valuators, auditors, passive administrators, trust accountants, price providers, and any other service providers
  • Work with trustee to update firm records for all vehicles listed in the Mexican stock exchanges and manage operations of the trust which includes requesting signatures from executives for trust operations (cash flow notices, payment of services, among others)
  • Monitoring and reporting of the investment activity and performance of vehicles listed in the Mexican stock exchanges and assisting in fee/cash flow analyses and coupon payment calculations
  • Assist with meticulous review of client deliverables provided by internal teams, including the position reports provided by the private debt team related to the Rated Notes listed in the Mexican stock exchanges
  • Assist with resolving inquiries from institutional investors, high net worth clients, Private Bankers and/or Financial Advisors
  • Data entry and/or reconcile data into central database
  • Translate financial information and investment summaries from English to Spanish or vice versa
  • Coordinate compliance approvals of Fund reports, presentations, cash flow notices and file management for team
  • Complete RFPs/RFIs, surveys/questionnaires and client requests, including writing and editing content, working with cross-functional teams to obtain all the necessary information, and ensuring that the final product is accurate and delivered on time

Qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s degree in finance, accounting, economics or business preferred
  • Two or more years of related financial services experience required (understanding of CERPIs/CKDs/publicly listed trusts or working as a trustee)
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills in English and Spanish (writing sample required)
  • General familiarity with Mexican financial services industry and reporting requirements of private equity publicly listed securities in Mexico
  • Significant experience with Microsoft Excel
  • Highly motivated individual with demonstrated initiative, independence and adaptability and teamwork skills that prefers to work in a collegial environment.
  • Strong ability to multi-task and manage time in a detail-oriented environment
  • Follow up skills and acute attention to accuracy and detail are essential
  • Strong financial accounting skills

  • Prepare for and participate in meetings with managers and conduct on-going due diligence
  • Form an independent opinion about the attractiveness of a sector or investment opportunity
  • Construct an argument around an opinion and efficiently communicate that argument both verbally and in written materials; be able to defend position in a team environment, including Investment Committee meetings
  • Draft investment memorandums and presentations related to acquisition opportunities
  • Assist with asset management duties, including financial analysis and investor reporting for existing investments
  • Support the fundraising process, including creating fundraising presentation materials and coordinating historical fund- and asset-level performance analysis

Education and/or Work Experience Requirements:

  • 1-3 years of experience in real estate private equity/credit, investment banking, or related real estate finance field
  • BA/BS in Business, Finance, Economics, Accounting, Real Estate, or related field
  • Demonstrated track record of academic and professional success
  • Takes pride of ownership in work, and work ethic
  • Ability to represent the Firm credibly in written and interpersonal communications
  • Demonstrable analytical capabilities, including strong quantitative/modeling skills
  • High proficiency in Word, PowerPoint, and Excel
  • Developing investment judgment and general understanding of risk/reward trade-offs
  • Flexibility to work within StepStone’s dynamic and entrepreneurial organization
  • Based in Chicago, with availability to travel, primarily domestically

Required Knowledge, Skills, and Abilities:

  • ARGUS experience optional but preferred
  • Promote and demonstrate a positive, team-oriented attitude

At StepStone, we believe that our people are our most important asset and crucial to our success.  We are an Equal Opportunity Employer that strives to create an environment that empowers our employees and allows them to be heard, regardless of title or tenure.  Our organizational community features multiple Employment Resource Groups as well as mentorship programs to enhance the employee experience for all.  

As an Equal Opportunity Employer, StepStone does not discriminate on the basis of race, creed, color, religion, sex, national origin, citizenship status, age, disability, marital status, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, genetic information or any other characteristic protected by law.

Candidates must be at least 18 years old to apply.

Developing People at StepStone

jobs.title: Private Equity Internship - London - 1st Oct to Dec 2026
jobs.description:
Working in partnership to build better businesses.


Triton is an investment firm that believes in engaging with boards, managers and employees to build better businesses.  We are entrepreneurs who invest capital where we can see an opportunity to create long-term value.   
 
We believe Triton is a great place to work and we want our team to be entrepreneurial, enjoy being part of a collective effort, inspire and challenge each other, and to bring an international mindset.

  • Be part of our team of hands-on investment professionals

  • Be a collaborative team player
  • Have excellent attention to detail

  • Currently enrolled as a bachelor or masters student or recent graduate
  • Excellent academic track record
  • Relevant prior internship or work experience - e.g. investment banking, private equity or consulting
  • Fluency in Dutch/Flemish is a plus

Triton is an equal opportunity employer.  We recognise that diversity is key to our continued success, and strive to maintain a fair and equitable recruitment process for all applicants.

jobs.title: Sr. Analyst/Associate, Capital Markets (SBA 7(a))
jobs.description:
Interested in joining one of the fastest growing commercial real estate firms and the #4 commercial mortgage REIT in the country?


Ready Capital is searching for experienced and driven professionals to expand our team and grow with our company. We offer a work environment that thrives on collaboration and a corporate culture of employees who are passionate and excited about what they do. Come work with us!

Job Title: Sr. Analyst/Associate, Capital Markets (SBA 7(a))

Location: Hybrid / Remote


Job Summary:
Ready Capital (NYSE: RC) is a multi-strategy real estate finance company that originates, acquires, finances and services small- to medium-sized balance commercial loans. We specialize in loans backed by commercial real estate, including agency, multifamily, investor and bridge as well as SBA 7(a) business loans. Headquartered in New York City, Ready Capital employs over 400 lending professionals nationwide.


We are seeking a Sr. Analyst, Capital Markets (SBA 7(a)) to join the Treasury/Capital Markets team. This role will support the execution and ongoing management of SBA 7(a) loan financing activities, including loan pledging, collateral reporting, and coordination with internal stakeholders and external financing counterparties. The ideal candidate is analytically strong, highly detail-oriented, and comfortable operating in a fast-paced, execution-focused environment. Open to employees in the NY/NJ/CT area as a hybrid role, reporting into our mid-town Manhattan office, or remote for qualified candidates in other states. 
 

Key Responsibilities

  • Loan Pledging & Facility Management
    • Manage the end-to-end process for pledging SBA 7(a) loans into financing facilities
    • Ensure compliance with borrowing base requirements, eligibility criteria, and lender reporting obligations
    • Maintain accurate collateral tracking and resolve any diligence or eligibility issues with internal teams and lenders
    • Support ongoing facility maintenance, including reporting, covenant tracking, and audit/diligence requests
  • Capital Markets Execution
    • Support the execution of SBA 7(a) securitizations, including pool assembly, data tape validation, and transaction coordination
    • Assist in structuring, pricing support, and execution logistics
    • Work closely with internal stakeholders to ensure timely execution and accurate reporting of securitization activity
  • Cross-Functional Coordination
    • Partner with the SBA originations team to track pipeline and ensure loans are financeable and meet facility requirements
    • Act as a key liaison between originations, asset management, and financing counterparties
    • Coordinate with legal, compliance, and external counterparties as needed
  • Lender & Counterparty Interaction
    • Serve as a point of contact for warehouse lenders on collateral, reporting, and ongoing facility matters
    • Respond to lender diligence requests in a timely and organized manner
    • Support facility amendments, renewals, and onboarding/offboarding of collateral

Qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s degree (Finance, Accounting, Economics, Real Estate, or related field preferred).
  • 1–3 years of experience in capital markets, structured finance, warehouse lending, SBA lending, or related financial services roles
  • Familiarity with SBA 7(a) loans, securitizations, or loan financing structures is strongly preferred
  • Strong understanding of loan data, collateral eligibility, and financing mechanics
  • Advanced Excel skills and ability to manage large data sets with a high degree of accuracy
  • Highly organized with strong attention to detail and the ability to manage multiple processes simultaneously
  • Strong communication skills and ability to work cross-functionally in a team-oriented environment



Note: This job description is not intended to be all inclusive or exclusive. At any time, employees may perform other related duties as required to meet the ongoing needs of the organization and participate in additional trainings.

Ready Capital offers a generous PTO policy and paid holidays, the terms of which are set forth in the program policies. All full-time employees also are eligible to participate in various benefit plans, including medical, dental, vision, life, disability insurance and 401K; in each case in accordance with the terms of the applicable plans.

Physical Demands:
The physical demands described here are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential functions of this job. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.
While performing the duties of this Job, the employee is regularly required to sit; use hands to finger, handle, or feel and talk or hear. The employee is frequently required to stand and walk. The employee is occasionally required to reach with hands and arms; climb or balance and stoop, kneel, crouch, or crawl. The employee must frequently lift and/or move up to 10 pounds. Specific vision abilities required by this job include close vision, distance vision, color vision, peripheral vision, depth perception and ability to adjust focus.

Ready Capital is an equal opportunity employer (EOE)

Pay Range:

The annual full-time salary range for this role is:

$75,000 - $90,000 

Final compensation will be determined based on experience, qualifications, and performance. Additionally, certain positions may be eligible to receive a discretionary bonus as determined by bonus program guidelines, performance and approval.

jobs.title: Associate, Multifamily Originations
jobs.description:
Interested in joining one of the fastest growing commercial real estate firms and the #4 commercial mortgage REIT in the country?


Ready Capital is searching for experienced and driven professionals to expand our team and grow with our company. We offer a work environment that thrives on collaboration and a corporate culture of employees who are passionate and excited about what they do. Come work with us!

Job Title: Associate, Multifamily Originations 

Location: New York City (Hybrid – 3 days on-site)
 

Job Summary: 
Ready Capital Affordable Multi-Family is a dynamic and growing niche business dedicated exclusively to Affordable Housing Finance. With a team of 25 professionals, the firm has recently closed its own investor fund and currently services a loan portfolio of $4.3 billion. 

We are currently seeking an Associate, Originations, to join the Affordable Multifamily Originations team in our Midtown office. This role is an early-career to mid-career position designed to support senior originators and investment professionals across underwriting, transaction execution, and market analysis for affordable multifamily transactions, including tax-exempt bond and LIHTC financing. The ideal candidate is analytically strong, detail-oriented, and motivated to build a career in affordable housing finance with exposure to the full life cycle of originations and closings.

Key Responsibilities:
•    Support originations team in sourcing, underwriting, and execution of affordable multifamily loan transactions.
•    Complete financial modeling and credit analysis for multifamily assets, including review of Loan-to-Value (LTV), Debt Service Coverage Ratio (DSCR), sources and uses, and cash flow projections.
•    Prepare and maintain underwriting materials, internal credit memos, and investment committee packages.
•    Prepare marketing and investor materials related to tax-exempt bond and LIHTC transactions.
•    Coordinate due diligence materials and support the closing process for bond and loan transactions.
•    Lead group calls to ensure loan closing deadlines are met. 
•    Track transaction pipelines, maintain internal databases, and support reporting requirements.
•    Review legal documentation to ensure alignment with investor requirements and interests
•    Conduct market research on affordable housing trends, capital markets, and comparable transactions.
•    Travel nationally to attend industry conferences, participate in networking opportunities, and visit project properties.
•    Collaborate with internal teams, including capital markets, credit, legal, and servicing, as needed.


Qualifications:
•    Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Accounting, or Real Estate.
•    Strong interest in affordable housing, multifamily finance, and real estate credit.
•    Solid analytical and quantitative skills with high attention to detail.
•    Strong written and verbal communication skills.
•    Strong problem-solving skills with the ability to quickly identify and deliver answers to ad hoc questions from clients.
•    Ability to manage multiple priorities and deadlines in a fast-paced environment.
•    Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and PowerPoint; experience with financial models preferred.

Note: This job description is not intended to be all inclusive or exclusive. At any time, employees may perform other related duties as required to meet the ongoing needs of the organization and participate in additional trainings.

Ready Capital offers a generous PTO policy and paid holidays, the terms of which are set forth in the program policies. All full-time employees also are eligible to participate in various benefit plans, including medical, dental, vision, life, disability insurance and 401K; in each case in accordance with the terms of the applicable plans.

Physical Demands:
The physical demands described here are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential functions of this job.  Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.
While performing the duties of this Job, the employee is regularly required to sit; use hands to finger, handle, or feel and talk or hear.  The employee is frequently required to stand and walk.  The employee is occasionally required to reach with hands and arms; climb or balance and stoop, kneel, crouch, or crawl.  The employee must frequently lift and/or move up to 10 pounds.   Specific vision abilities required by this job include close vision, distance vision, color vision, peripheral vision, depth perception and ability to adjust focus.
 

Ready Capital is an equal opportunity employer (EOE)

Pay Range:

The annual full-time salary range for this role is:

$95,000 - $110,000

Final compensation will be determined based on experience, qualifications, and performance. Additionally, certain positions may be eligible to receive a discretionary bonus as determined by bonus program guidelines, performance and approval.

jobs.title: Product Owner Murex - Commodities & Carbon
jobs.description:
Senior Product Owner – Commodities

  • You are a Markets expert with strong Commodities product knowledge, keen to join a high-performing team in Institutional Banking and Markets.
  • This specialist role will focus on Commodities, Carbon and related Global Markets platforms, with knowledge of electricity markets advantageous.
  • Together we will deliver strategic technology change and resilient product outcomes for our customers.

See yourself in our Team

In Institutional Banking and Markets (IB&M), our purpose is to combine global connectivity and capability to build a better Australia. What we do as a business matters to our clients, the Group and the country. Global Markets is a department of IB&M and is responsible for activities across interest rates, bonds, foreign exchange, credit, debt, commodities and carbon products. Markets has offices across Australia, New Zealand, Hong Kong, Singapore, London and New York. The Global Markets Domain crews are responsible for the execution of technology change initiatives in IB&M. The crews also undertake initiatives that cut across the CBA Group to enable wider strategic priorities. This team works closely with Group and IB&M stakeholders to enable Global Markets and Commodities & Carbon businesses. This includes risk and controls management, business change, prioritisation and delivery of initiatives, and ongoing business improvement.

Do work that matters

Reporting to the Crew Lead for FICC & Carbon crew, this role will see you define and lead the product vision, strategy and roadmap for Commodities platforms across Global Markets. You will work with senior stakeholders across the Group to deliver business and customer value, whilst maintaining strong focus on risk, valuation, governance, controls, regulatory obligations and operational resilience.

Your responsibilities

  • Lead the product strategy, vision and roadmap for Commodities, Carbon and related Global Markets platforms, primarily across Murex and associated technology services.
  • Coordinate end-to-end delivery of the technology roadmap for the respective stream, including discovery, requirements, prioritisation, delivery planning, release coordination and sign-off.
  • On a day-to-day basis lead the delivery squad by providing business context, clarity, prioritisation and product vision.
  • Work with sales, trading, risk, operations, finance and technology stakeholders to capture business requirements and prioritise outcomes for technology squads.
  • Identify and manage risks, dependencies and issues for the squad in line with excellent operational risk, governance and compliance outcomes.
  • Stay abreast of external market trends, including commodities, carbon, electricity, regulatory change, economic developments and innovation, including relevant Artificial Intelligence use cases.
  • Build and foster strong relationships across the product value chain, including frontline teams, compliance, risk, operations and technology, to ensure efficient delivery of strategic initiatives and resolution of issues.
  • Support the Crew Lead to ensure overall crew objectives and business OKRs are met.
  • Proactively identify, articulate and manage risks, controls, governance obligations and compliance across your product.
  • Work with GM COO, Line 1 and Line 2 Risk teams and Compliance to ensure adherence to Global Markets policies across the platforms you are responsible for.

We’re interested in hearing from people who:

  • Have strong hands-on Product Owner or Product Manager experience, including backlog management, roadmap development, product discovery and end-to-end product lifecycle delivery.
  • Have a degree in Business, Engineering, Commerce, Technology or a related discipline.
  • Have excellent knowledge of Financial Markets, with deep understanding of Commodities products, risk, valuation, governance, trade lifecycle, operational processes and controls.
  • Possess sound knowledge of trading platforms such as Murex or Calypso, with knowledge of Carbon, Commodities and Electricity markets advantageous.
  • Have a proven track record delivering large and complex initiatives in fast-paced, risk-aware environments.
  • Are adept at managing senior stakeholders, influencing priorities, resolving ambiguity and aligning business and technology teams.
  • Bring advanced problem-solving, decision-making, data-driven prioritisation, stakeholder influencing and product roadmapping capability, consistent with Senior Product Owner expectations.
  • Can balance customer outcomes, commercial value, risk, technology trade-offs and delivery pace, with awareness of Artificial Intelligence concepts and practical use cases.

If you're already part of the Commonwealth Bank Group (including Bankwest, x15ventures), you'll need to apply through Sidekick to submit a valid application. We’re keen to support you with the next step in your career.

We're aware of some accessibility issues on this site, particularly for screen reader users. We want to make finding your dream job as easy as possible, so if you require additional support please contact HR Direct on 1800 989 696.

Advertising End Date: 05/07/2026

jobs.title: Senior Associate Financial Institutions & Government - 12 month contract
jobs.description:
• Support senior bankers covering key NSW Government and Higher Education clients 

  • Build your institutional banking expertise within a leading Coverage team

  • Join a progressive and innovative environment in Institutional Banking & Markets

See yourself in our team

Global Institutional Banking Coverage (GIBC) leads client origination and relationship management for CBA's Institutional Banking & Markets (IB&M) division. Our purpose is to help clients achieve their strategic objectives through differentiated insight, trusted advice and seamless delivery across the financing and risk management spectrum.

Within GIBC's Financial Institutions and Government (FIG), the Government and Higher Education team manages relationships and originates opportunities across the NSW Government and Higher Education sectors. The team works closely with clients to deliver tailored capital, transaction banking and risk management solutions that support strategic priorities and long-term outcomes.

Do work that matters

As a Senior Associate, you'll play an important role supporting the management of key client relationships across the NSW Government and Higher Education portfolio. Working closely with senior bankers, you'll help identify opportunities, coordinate client engagement activities and contribute to the delivery of tailored banking solutions.

You'll gain exposure to a broad range of products, clients and stakeholders while building deep sector expertise and developing your institutional banking career within a highly collaborative Coverage team.

More specifically, you will:

  • Support senior bankers in managing relationships across a portfolio of NSW Government and Higher Education clients

  • Assist in identifying and originating opportunities across capital, transaction banking and risk management solutions

  • Develop insights into the government sector, market trends and industry developments to support client engagement and business development activities

  • Prepare high-quality client presentations, briefing materials, transaction analysis and strategic insights

  • Support transaction execution including coordinating deal teams, product partners, credit risk, operations and other internal stakeholders

  • Assist in managing client, credit and transaction data to ensure accuracy and quality across systems

  • Work closely with product partners across IB&M to develop and implement client engagement strategies

  • Assist in managing client risk and ensuring compliance with governance and probity requirements, including government tender processes

  • Build strong internal and external stakeholder relationships to support business development and client service outcomes

  • Contribute to the broader team's objectives through collaboration, market intelligence and client-focused initiatives

We're interested in hearing from people who have:

  • Experience in Institutional Banking, Corporate Banking, Financial Services or a related client-facing environment

  • Strong relationship-building skills and the ability to establish credibility with clients and internal stakeholders

  • An interest in government, public sector or institutional clients (prior sector experience is advantageous but not essential)

  • Strong analytical, financial analysis and modelling, writing and presentation skills

  • The ability to manage multiple priorities and contribute effectively in a fast-paced environment

  • Strong commercial acumen and a genuine interest in financial markets and banking solutions

  • A collaborative mindset and a willingness to learn from experienced bankers and product specialists

What's in it for you?

This role offers a unique opportunity to build your career within one of Australia's leading Institutional Banking teams while gaining exposure to some of the state's most significant government and higher education organisations. You'll work alongside experienced relationship managers and product specialists, develop valuable sector expertise and gain broad exposure to transaction banking, capital markets and risk management solutions in a supportive and high-performing environment.

If you're passionate about building client relationships, developing your banking expertise and want to contribute to meaningful opportunities supporting some of Australia’s most important public institutions, we'd love to hear from you.

If you're already part of the Commonwealth Bank Group (including Bankwest, x15ventures), you'll need to apply through Sidekick to submit a valid application. We’re keen to support you with the next step in your career.

We're aware of some accessibility issues on this site, particularly for screen reader users. We want to make finding your dream job as easy as possible, so if you require additional support please contact HR Direct on 1800 989 696.

Advertising End Date: 06/07/2026

jobs.title: Manager - Transaction Advisory Services
jobs.description:
Description

Manager- Transaction Advisory Services (US - Remote)

What to expect when you join Sikich

Team members at Sikich have a lot in common while also being part of a rich and varied group of contributors, creating a distinct and thriving culture. Chief among our commonalities is a desire for growth and a shared unity of purpose in our professional lives. We believe that through diverse perspectives, challenging the status quo and rewarding action, we accelerate innovation and drive growth – for our clients, for ourselves and for our communities.

The professional services landscape continues to evolve. For Sikich, this means we have an opportunity to further cement our leadership position in this industry and continue to grow our organization in increasingly exciting ways. This growth is meaningful for every team member at our company because larger companies simply see more interesting client opportunities and can attract impressively talented individuals like you. Through a dedicated focus on key business priorities and intentionally creating a rewarding employee experience, Sikich has developed into a highly regarded provider of professional services and a sought-after employer of choice.

Do you want to work with other skilled practitioners and serve clients in a way that makes a difference? Are you seeking a supportive environment backed by a deep and extensive set of skillsets? Are you ready to make an impact and be acknowledged for your contributions? If you answered yes to these questions, we see a mutually beneficial and gratifying relationship on the horizon!

Are you ready to grow with us?

Position Summary

Reporting to the Director, the Manager will ….

What will you do in this role?

  • Lead day-to-day operations including managing a team through:
  • Drafting request lists and managing client communications regarding requested items
  • Managing the creation of databooks
  • Preparing materials for management meetings
  • Drafting adjusted EBITDA, adjusted net working capital, and debt and debt-like tables
  • Communicate with senior managers, directors, and principals on a regular basis
  • Develop associates and senior associates by coaching and providing real-time feedback
  • Comfortable prioritizing and working on multiple tasks including client work and internal initiatives

What do you need to succeed in this role?

  • 5+ years of experience in transaction advisory or audit
  • Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in accounting
  • CPA certification or strong knowledge of US GAAP
  • Project management experience
  • Calibrate style to communicate clearly and concisely
  • Ability and willingness to travel as needed

In compliance with this state’s pay transparency laws, the midpoint of the salary range for this role is $147,900. This is not a guarantee of compensation or salary, as final offer amount may vary based on factors including but not limited to experience and geographic location.

In addition, specific skills/experience required are as follows:

  • Servant Leader – You are hyper focused on engaging employees, fostering their development, and building a positive culture.
  • Solutions Focused – You see opportunities in every business problem and can develop, articulate, and implement solutions.
  • Collaboration – You are a relationship builder across all levels of the organization and across all business units.
  • Instills Trust - You do what you say, and you follow through on commitments, you act with integrity, you are consistent and are perceived as credible.
  • Impact & Influence Thinking – You gain support for ideas, proposals, and solutions, and get others to act, with or without formal authority, to advance initiatives/objectives.

About Sikich

Sikich offers the public and private sectors a diverse platform of professional services across consulting, technology and compliance. Highly specialized and hands-on teams deliver integrated solutions rooted in deep industry experience. Our approach is strategically and thoughtfully designed to help our clients, teams, and communities accelerate success.

Sikich Total Rewards

Our team members enjoy expansive benefits ranging from competitive compensation and insurance options to wellness programs and a flexible time off policy, to name only a few. Sikich also takes pride in prioritizing team members’ health, total wellbeing and time spent with family, friends and in the pursuit of personal goals, hobbies, and endeavors.

Some examples of our many benefits:

  • Sikich maintains a Flexible Time Off (FTO) Policy. We encourage every full-time employee, as your role permits, to utilize paid time off (personal time, mental/physical health care, vacation, sick leave, etc.). Waiting for time off to accrue is common at other companies. At Sikich, you do not have to wait for this benefit to kick in. FTO is activated on your first day with our organization.
  • Sikich will also recognize paid holidays during the year and strives to permit employees to have time off the last week of the calendar year when client and project work permits.
  • Sikich offers a comprehensive wellness program to engage, challenge and empower team members to take responsibility for their wellbeing. Activities can be tracked through our wellness provider to obtain gift cards and other rewards.



We also offer:

  • Flexible work arrangements
  • Health, dental, vision, life, and accident/death/disability insurance options
  • HSA employer contribution
  • Nine (9) paid holidays annually.
  • A robust paid Parental Bonding Leave program covering birth, adoption, and foster      children.
  • 401(k) with employer contributions
  • CPA bonus with four (4) paid exam days & four (4) paid study days.
  • Tuition reimbursement
  • Generous employee referral bonus program
  • Client referral bonus program
  • Pet insurance
  • FORCE – Sikich community volunteer program enabling each team member to use up to four hours of paid time annually to volunteer and make a difference in their localcommunities.

Want to learn more? Visit our Careers website or .

Sikich is an Equal Opportunity Employer M/F/D/V

Sikich practices in an alternative practice structure in accordance with the AICPA Professional Code of Conduct and applicable law, regulations, and professional standards. Sikich CPA LLC is a licensed CPA firm that provides audit and attest services to its clients, and Sikich LLC and its subsidiaries provide tax and business advisory services to its clients. Sikich CPA LLC has a contractual arrangement with Sikich LLC under which Sikich LLC supports Sikich CPA LLC’s performance of its professional services. Sikich LLC and its subsidiaries are not licensed CPA firms.

“Sikich” is the brand name under which Sikich CPA LLC and Sikich LLC provide professional services.  The entities under the Sikich brand are independently owned and are not liable for the services provided by any other entity providing services under the Sikich brand. The use of the terms “our company”, “we” and “us” and other similar terms denote the alternative practice structure of Sikich CPA LLC and Sikich LLC.
