jobs.title: Sr. Business Analyst, Asset Management IT, IT
jobs.description:
Position Description

We are seeking a highly motivated and experienced BA/PM with a background in Asset Management operations and systems to join the team. The ideal candidate will have a strong background in Asset Management workflows (front-to-back), product management, and knowledge of the full lifecycle of investment operations — from order management through settlement and NAV reporting.

Key Areas of Responsibility

  • Work with Asset Management business stakeholders to identify product requirements and create and own the product roadmap for AM platforms (e.g., OMS, PMS, operations, reporting).

  • Ensure that Asset Management platforms meet business needs, support regulatory requirements, and align with the firm's growth and entity consolidation objectives.

  • Ensure the platforms are properly integrated with upstream/downstream systems (e.g., market data, trading, settlement, accounting, risk)

  • Work with development teams to ensure features are delivered on time and meet business stakeholder expectations.

  • Own and coordinate system testing and UAT cycles — including test planning, test case preparation, defect tracking, and sign-off with business users.

  • Conduct process analysis and monitor key performance indicators to identify areas for improvement and operational efficiency gains

  • Partner with Compliance and Risk teams to meet regulatory requirements (e.g., SFC, CSRC, cross-border data regulations).

  • Work with business to define data administration, document governance, and entitlement controls

Requirements

  • Bachelor Degree or higher in IT or Business disciplines

  • Around 5-8 years experience as a BA/PM or similar role, with a focus on Asset Management systems or operations.
    Candidates with more experience can be considered as Lead BA

  • Strong understanding of Asset Management workflows: order management, portfolio management, trade lifecycle, reconciliation, NAV calculation, and client reporting

  • Familiarity with data governance, regulatory, and compliance requirements relevant to asset management (SFC, CSRC, cross-border data transfer)

  • Excellent communication skills in English and Mandarin, ability to work effectively with stakeholders at all levels

  • Hands-on experience in system testing and UAT — including writing test scripts, coordinating test cycles, and managing defect resolution.

  • Strong troubleshooting skills — ability to diagnose system issues, perform root cause analysis, read application logs, and work with technical teams to drive resolution under pressure.

  • Software development skills is a strong advantage (e.g., Python, Java, C#, JavaScript) and familiarity with development practices (version control, CI/CD, code review)

  • Familiarity with databases (SQL), APIs, system architecture, scripting, and ability to independently investigate data issues or write ad-hoc queries

  • Self-motivated and able to thrive in a fast-paced and dynamic environment.

jobs.title: Associate, Equity Derivatives Trade Services, Equity Derivatives
jobs.description:
Position Description

The position is within Front Office, reporting to Equity Derivatives Department. The role will work directly with the Front Office Trading, Sales, Structurers and Quantitative desks covering trade booking for the Equity Derivatives and Delta 1 products.

Key Areas of Responsibilities

  • Check automated bookings for Delta One, Flow and Exotic Derivatives Products
  • Trade booking of vanilla instruments, including vanilla equity options, stocks, futures, TRS, IRS, FX (swap/ spot/ NDF) across A shares, Hong Kong, ASEAN, US and European underlying
  • Trade booking of Structured Products, including Autocallables, Sharkfin, Boosters, BEN, FCN, ELN etc
  • Process life cycle events, including expiries, early termination, redemption by cash/ physical delivery, coupon pay-outs, corporate action adjustments, etc.
  • Process trade confirmations and term sheets
  • Setup and send valuation reports to clients
  • Prepare periodic/ ad-hoc trade summaries for internal reporting purposes
  • Handle other daily tasks such as PnL reconciliations liaising with Product Control, Operations, Finance

Requirements

  • Degree holder in Mathematics, Statistics, Finance or related discipline(s)
  • Minimum 4 years of Trade Support experience either working with a Trading Desk or in a Middle Office function on the trading desk
  • Basic understanding of vanilla and exotic products for equities
  • Detail oriented, focus on accuracy and time management
  • Strong communication and interpersonal skills
  • Ability to work in a fast-paced team environment
  • Willingness to learn and not afraid to take on new tasks
  • Ability to quickly learn new payoffs, booking models and concepts
  • Any experience in using Calypso is a plus
  • Fluent in English and Chinese (including Putonghua)

jobs.title: Materials and Energy Research Associate, Research
jobs.description:
Position Description

We are looking for a Materials and Energy Research Associate, responsible for providing support to the Lead Analyst. You would be working the Senior Analyst on research projects and client requests. You would be primarily responsible for coordinating research activities, e.g. following news, compiling data, updating financial models, writing parts of investment reports and fundamental research through deep dives on industry thematics.

Key Areas of Responsibilities

  • Assist Senior Analysts on specific research projects.

  • Collect data and compile answers for clients requests.

  • Help build and maintain commodity and financial models.

  • Maintain databases of economic and industry specific data.

  • Accompany clients on visits and produce notes.

  • Maintain sound working relationships with corporates, clients, the government, and industry organizations.

  • Develop and maintain key client relationships

  • Actively engage with clients and the relevant sales team members to communicate ideas to ensure maximum exposure/return from the time invested in producing research reports

Requirements

  • Bachelor degree or above in mining, petroleum/chemical engineering or similar technical degree, or a business-related discipline (preferably finance or accounting)

  • 2-7 years of experience as a research associate or corporate finance/valuations

  • (mining or energy background preferred), or specific industry experience in mining/energy-related fields

  • Strong analytical skills with quant skills highly desirable

  • Strong understanding of financial modelling

  • Strong excel/ spreadsheet skills

  • Attention to detail

  • Ability to work fast and accurately under extreme time pressure when required

  • Good interpersonal sensitivity

  • High commercial awareness

  • Excellent communication, relationship and management skills to both internal and external

  • Excellent command in both spoken and written English

jobs.title: Senior Associate, Product Launch
jobs.description:
Job Description Summary

For over forty years, HarbourVest has been home to a committed team of professionals with an entrepreneurial spirit and a desire to deliver impactful solutions to our clients and investing partners. As our global firm grows, we continue to add individuals who seek a collaborative, open-door culture that values diversity and innovative thinking.

In our collegial environment that’s marked by low turnover and high energy, you’ll be inspired to grow and thrive. Here, you will be encouraged to build on your strengths and acquire new skills and experiences.
We are committed to fostering an environment of inclusion that promotes mutual respect among all employees. Understanding and valuing these differences optimizes the potential of both the individual and the firm.


HarbourVest is an equal opportunity employer.

This position will be a hybrid work arrangement. You will receive 18 remote workdays per quarter to use at your discretion, subject to manager approval. For example, you may choose to work in the office 4 days per week and take one remote day weekly (typically 13 weeks per quarter), leaving 5 additional remote days to be used as needed.

The Product Launch Senior Associate will assist with leading the launch of innovative new investment solutions for our clients, including those utilising an existing operating model and those adopting a new operating model. This is an exciting new role directly involved in the firm's expansion. The Senior Associate will be part of the Strategy, Innovation, and Execution function, which is collectively responsible for building the firm in new areas.

The ideal candidate is someone who is:
•    Entrepreneurial, creative, thoughtful, and flexible, with professional experience assisting in developing, fleshing out, and launching new investment products or solutions, preferably within private markets
•    Outstanding attention to detail and ability to assist with pushing for decisions on a project while seeking input from different perspectives, without existing precedents
•    Excellent at communicating complex concepts in simple terms, in writing and in person
•    Experience working in team environments, with the ability to help with leading or facilitating group efforts, and to help influence colleagues and counterparties using a range of techniques
•    Organized and effective in supporting key initiatives, with the willingness and capability to assume responsibility and ownership of outcomes
•    Focused on having a significant impact on the business, while considering short- and long-term opportunities and risks

What you will do:
This role will help with to lead the development of specific new products (including those that are following existing or new operating models) that align with the firm's strategy. The role will include:
•    Assisting on all aspects of the launch of a new product from the point of concept approval to the first subscription
•    Supporting plans to launch a new product
•    Assisting with establishing key dependencies and goals in order to meet launch targets
•    Carefully reviewing and opining on legal, marketing and other product documentation from a commercial perspective
•    Helping to gather feedback and input from a large cross-section of the organization and evaluating different inputs to inform decisions around product terms and related sales and operating models
•    Coordinating with operational leads to ensure that the firm will be prepared to support the operational needs of a new product
•    Sharing experience and insights to influence the firm's product strategy and planning and prioritization process
•    Contributing to research and development before a product concept reaches the launch stage
•    Assisting with preparing and maintaining the firm’s overall product development and launch pipeline
•    Updating and maintaining an array of tools, templates, references, and resources to assist in the development and launch of products
•    Other responsibilities as required

The role will include close collaboration with other members of the Product Launch team, leaders of the Strategy, Innovation, and Execution function, and ongoing partnership with a diverse set of colleagues across the firm, including Legal, Compliance, Marketing, Investor Relations, Private Wealth, Tax, Investments, Client Operations New Business Implementation, and Financial Planning & Analysis, as well as external providers and advisors.

What you bring:
•    3-6 years of professional experience in private markets or asset management space, focused on structuring / product development / product launch and related topics, from a commercial or legal perspective
•    Demonstrated excellence in implementing multi-dimensional projects
•    Strong familiarity with and curiosity about the private equity or broader private markets landscape
•    Strong understanding of the mechanics and inter-dependencies of alternative investment funds
•    Strong project management experience with an ability to lead and manage large cross-functional teams
•    Familiarity with different legal structures used for investors to access private market strategies
•    Familiarity with business models

Education Preferred:
•    Bachelors degree
•    Nice to have: JD or MBA or equivalent experience

Base Salary Range 

$120,000.00 - $130,000.00

This USD base salary range represents only one component of total compensation for this role and is provided in accordance with local requirements. This role is eligible for a discretionary annual bonus, which is determined based on individual and overall firm performance. In addition to salary and bonus, total compensation may include eligibility for long-term reward programs and a comprehensive total rewards package that may include retirement, health, insurance, paid time off, and wellness programs. Our total rewards offerings are influenced by several business factors, and eligibility for certain components will vary by position and geography. Please note the posted ranges do not apply outside the U.S. and should not be converted to other currencies as a proxy for compensation in other countries.

jobs.title: Senior Vice President, Head of Valuations
jobs.description:
Job Description Summary

For over forty years, HarbourVest has been home to a committed team of professionals with an entrepreneurial spirit and a desire to deliver impactful solutions to our clients and investing partners. As our global firm grows, we continue to add individuals who seek a collaborative, open-door culture that values diversity and innovative thinking.
 

In our collegial environment that’s marked by low turnover and high energy, you’ll be inspired to grow and thrive. Here, you will be encouraged to build on your strengths and acquire new skills and experiences.
 

We are committed to fostering an environment of inclusion that promotes mutual respect among all employees. Understanding and valuing these differences optimizes the potential of both the individual and the firm.
 

HarbourVest is an equal opportunity employer.
 

This position will be a hybrid work arrangement. You will receive 18 remote workdays per quarter to use at your discretion, subject to manager approval. For example, you may choose to work in the office 4 days per week and take one remote day weekly (typically 13 weeks per quarter), leaving 5 additional remote days to be used as needed.
 

The Head of Valuation is a highly visible leadership role at the center of HarbourVest’s continued growth throughout the private investment landscape! This individual will develop and be responsible for the firm’s valuation strategy across its alternative assets. They will focus on governance, fair value methodology, and operating scalability in investment sectors such as private equity, debt financing, and infrastructure assets. The role also serves as a trusted senior representative of the firm in engagements with auditors, regulators, investors, and fund boards, helping to reinforce HarbourVest’s reputation for rigor, integrity, and innovation!
 

The ideal candidate is an accomplished valuation leader with deep experience across Evergreen and Closed-end fund structures and a strong command of SEC and comparable regulatory frameworks. This person combines technical credibility with executive presence, bringing the judgment, influence, and strategic perspective required to lead through complexity, build institutional-grade processes, and partner effectively with senior team members across Investment Management, Enterprise Risk Management, Fund Accounting, Product Operations, Performance Management, auditors, and third-party providers.
 

What you will do

  • Set the vision for HarbourVest’s valuation function. Continue to develop the function into a world-class capability with scalable governance, policies, and operating models supporting business growth, product innovation, and more frequent valuation cycles.

  • Own and continuously evolve HarbourVest’s fair valuation framework across all investments, crafting methodology standards, governance, documentation, testing, and ongoing process enhancements.

  • Coordinate and manage third-party valuation providers, including onboarding, performance oversight, challenge processes, and long-term vendor strategy.

  • Act as HarbourVest’s senior valuation voice in client communications, operational due diligence processes, and auditor or regulatory engagements, bringing confidence, clarity, and credibility to highly visible conversations.

  • Partner across Fund Accounting, Reporting, Legal, Compliance, and Investment teams to embed valuation insight into NAV processes, financial reporting, investor communications, and board materials across a diverse range of fund structures.

  • Lead valuation governance forums and support fund board reporting, ensuring clarity, rigor, and defensibility of valuation decisions involving all HarbourVest products.

  • Build, lead, and encourage a high-performing valuation team, strengthening the platform for future growth while developing outstanding talent and fostering a culture of excellence, accountability, and continuous improvement.

  • Bring forward-looking, industry-informed leadership to valuation across private markets. Serve as an industry leader and orchestrate HarbourVest’s continued evolution of valuation practices.
     

What you bring

  • Deep and credible expertise related to valuation, accounting, audit, or asset management, with important leadership exposure across illiquid and alternative assets including private equity, private credit, and infrastructure.

  • Shown technical grounding in US GAAP or equivalent (and familiarity with IFRS is an advantage) and fair value accounting for Level 3 assets.

  • Proven record with valuation and governance across registered investment companies, evergreen vehicles, and closed-end private fund structures, including strong understanding of SEC Rule 2a-5 and related valuation frameworks.

  • Consistent track record to review sophisticated valuation models and methodologies (e.g., DCF, market approach) and assess valuation risk.

  • Experience handling and challenging third-party valuation agents and fund administrators.

  • Proven ability in leading teams, influencing senior decision-makers, and operating with a high degree of independence, judgment, and accountability.

  • Excellent communication skills, with the ability to translate technical valuation concepts for executive, client, and board-level audiences.
     

Education Preferred

  • Bachelor’s degree or equivalent professional experience in Accounting, Finance, or a related field

  • MBA, CPA, and/or CFA or equivalent experience preferred
     

Experience

15+ years of experience in valuation, audit, or fund accounting within alternative investments, asset management, or a top valuation firm, with relevant leadership experience spanning private equity, private credit, infrastructure instruments across open-ended and closed-ended products within a diverse set of fund structures.

​#LI-Hybrid
 

Base Salary Range

$285,000.00 - $310,000.00

This USD base salary range represents only one component of total compensation for this role and is provided in accordance with local requirements. This role is eligible for a discretionary annual bonus, which is determined based on individual and overall firm performance. In addition to salary and bonus, total compensation may include eligibility for long-term reward programs and a comprehensive total rewards package that may include retirement, health, insurance, paid time off, and wellness programs. Our total rewards offerings are influenced by several business factors, and eligibility for certain components will vary by position and geography. Please note the posted ranges do not apply outside the U.S. and should not be converted to other currencies as a proxy for compensation in other countries.

jobs.title: Vice President, Evergreen Credit Investment Specialist
jobs.description:
Job Description Summary

For over forty years, HarbourVest has been home to a committed team of professionals with an entrepreneurial spirit and a desire to deliver impactful solutions to our clients and investing partners. As our global firm grows, we continue to add individuals who seek a collaborative, open-door culture that values diversity and innovative thinking.

In our collegial environment that’s marked by low turnover and high energy, you’ll be inspired to grow and thrive. Here, you will be encouraged to build on your strengths and acquire new skills and experiences.

We are committed to fostering an environment of inclusion that promotes mutual respect among all employees. Understanding and valuing these differences optimizes the potential of both the individual and the firm.

HarbourVest is an equal opportunity employer.

You will receive 18 remote workdays per quarter to use at your discretion, subject to manager approval. For example, you may choose to work in the office 4 days per week and take one remote day weekly (typically 13 weeks per quarter), leaving 5 additional remote days to be used as needed. There will also be the need for travel.

About the role:

Vice President, Investment Specialist will work to drive the growth of HarbourVest’s private credit evergreen solutions in EMEA and APAC. HarbourVest’s evergreen solutions include private markets comingled vehicles and separate account opportunities, targeted at private wealth and institutional investors. Responsibilities include working alongside Investor Relations and the Private Wealth team to facilitate new business wins, identifying capability builds that cross-functionally improve the product and identifying adjacencies for new product development. This individual will be responsible for educating and engaging with the global Private Wealth and Investor Relations teams and other internal partners; making pitches to clients and prospects; conducting portfolio reviews with clients and supervising important client responses to ensure quality of submissions.

The ideal candidate is someone who has:

  • A strong understanding of private market evergreen vehicles and a deep knowledge of private credit and the private wealth market.
  • Excellent presentation and client engagement skills with the ability to tailor information based on the audience.
  • Strong relationship management skills to partner closely with fellow HarbourVest Investment and Investor Relations professionals, as well as external prospects and clients.
  • Strong investment and analytical competence.
  • Strong written skills.
  • A high level of maturity and ability to multi-task with little direct supervision.
  • An entrepreneurial approach with an ability to be forward-thinking and work without a playbook.
  • A desire for travel.
  • An understanding of the investment process.
  • A strong understanding of quantitative investment measurements and statistical tools.

What you will do:

External responsibilities:

  • Support sales, by bringing in-depth vehicle and market expertise to prospect and client meetings.
  • Support the Private Wealth and Investor Relations teams in their discussions with private wealth prospects, clients, distribution partners and institutional investors. Demonstrate deep knowledge of the market, product, HarbourVest’s capabilities, solution and differentiation.
  • Take into account the broader picture; engage externally around a broad range of business and investment topics. Speak with nuance around the investment landscape, client portfolio and market outlook.
  • Display creativity and problem solving in proposing solutions to client challenges. Consider the wider business implications and context (complexity, operational impact) in discussions that will result in the optimal outcome for HarbourVest.
  • Deep understanding of key competitors, fundraising and industry trends.


Internal product specialist responsibilities:

  • Engage with the Private Client and Investor Relations teams to collect feedback from the market. Synthesize market feedback and competitive intelligence and apply it to product evolution and content development.
  • Project manage the sales opportunity set and go-to-market strategy for non-US private credit evergreen products. Act as liaison point for all evergreen collective sales efforts, organized across various teams for the product.
  • Partner with the Investor Relations team to serve as the main point of contact to arrange calls/ meetings, follow-ups, comprehensive investment knowledge, identity and address concerns and provide guidance through diligence.
  • Educate the Private Wealth and Investor Relations teams on the product and strategy.
  • Oversee important RFPs, written collateral, portfolio updates and client responses to ensure the quality of submissions.

Education Preferred:

Strong academic and professional credentials. MBA and / or CFA preferred but not required.

Experience:

10+ years of investment or product roles with a client service orientation. An investment research background is a plus. Must have a blend of product leadership, investment capabilities and commercial sense. Relevant backgrounds include product specialist for liquid products, investment banking, equity analyst, consulting or transaction services experience.

jobs.title: Senior Associate, Portfolio Analytics
jobs.description:
Job Description Summary

For over forty years, HarbourVest has been home to a committed team of professionals with an entrepreneurial spirit and a desire to deliver impactful solutions to our clients and investing partners. As our global firm grows, we continue to add individuals who seek a collaborative, open-door culture that values diversity and innovative thinking.

In our collegial environment that’s marked by low turnover and high energy, you’ll be inspired to grow and thrive. Here, you will be encouraged to build on your strengths and acquire new skills and experiences.

We are committed to fostering an environment of inclusion that promotes mutual respect among all employees. Understanding and valuing these differences optimizes the potential of both the individual and the firm.

HarbourVest is an equal opportunity employer.

This position will be a hybrid work arrangement. You will receive 18 remote workdays per quarter to use at your discretion, subject to manager approval. For example, you may choose to work in the office 4 days per week and take one remote day weekly (typically 13 weeks per quarter), leaving 5 additional remote days to be used as needed.

The Portfolio Analytics team is seeking a Senior Associate to help lead and evolve our third-party vendor partnerships, with a primary focus on Acuity Analytics. This role will partner closely with internal teams, including QIS and Portfolio Analytics, to strengthen delivery, improve processes, and support the continued growth of our data management and due diligence capabilities.

This role gives you the chance to build a new and evolving operating model. You will influence how work is scaled globally and drive meaningful improvements across teams.

The ideal candidate is someone who has:

  • Proficiency in reviewing financial documents including balance sheets, income statements, cash flow reports, and investment schedules, with preference for experience in private equity or alternative investment contexts.
  • Background in private markets, portfolio analytics, or due diligence processes
  • Knowledge of critical performance indicators, data governance concepts, and data quality control frameworks
  • Experience partnering with quantitative or data-driven teams in fluid or developing settings
  • Experience assisting operational improvement or transformation initiatives
  • Experience working with platforms like SharePoint, eFront, S&P Capital IQ, Pitchbook, or comparable systems
  • Experience with data governance or frameworks aiding quality oversight
  • Familiarity with workflow automation platforms (i.e. Power Automate)

What you will do:

Lead Vendor Oversight and Governance

  • Facilitate regular governance meetings with third-party, driving discussions, and tracking outcomes
  • Review and analyze engagement data to identify trends, risks, and opportunities to improve efficiency
  • Serve as a primary point of contact for third-party queries, ensuring timely and accurate resolution
  • Proactively identify and raise issues, while working collaboratively toward resolution

Drive Improvement In Execution and Scale

  • Operate effectively within unstructured processes, bringing clarity and organization to evolving workflows
  • Build and maintain clear documentation to support consistency and knowledge sharing
  • Identify gaps in current processes and recommend practical, data-driven improvements
  • Partner with technology and data teams to enhance workflows, automation, and system integration

Collaborate Across Teams

  • Build cross-functional expertise across key workflows (cash flows, operating metrics, company performance data, and internal/external requests) to support team flexibility and operational resiliency
  • Step in to support critical processes as needed, ensuring continuity during periods of transition, volume spikes, or resource constraints
  • Contribute ideas and recommendations to support ongoing operational improvements
  • Build strong working relationships across global teams to promote transparency and alignment

Strengthen Risk and Data Practices

  • Contribute to a structured approach for managing vendor-related risks and controls
  • Support data quality efforts through validation, reconciliation, and issue resolution processes
  • Perform targeted quality reviews, including random sampling of vendor output, to confirm adherence to defined processes and standards

What you bring:

  • Background in handling unstructured data and evolving processes, with the ability to build structure and clarity
  • Clear and effective communication skills, with confidence addressing senior team members
  • Experience collaborating with third-party vendors or global teams is a plus
  • Demonstrate curiosity and ownership in improving processes and workflows
  • Ability to prioritize competing demands and work jointly with different teams
  • Attention to detail and a focus on delivering high-quality, accurate work
  • Strong problem-solving skills and a proactive, can-do attitude

Education

  • Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Accounting, Business, or related field, or equivalent experience.
  • MBA or other advanced credentials are a plus but not required.

Experience

  • 5-8 years experience within the financial services sector, operational functions, data management, or a related field

#LI-Hybrid

Base Salary Range

$90,000.00 - $110,000.00

This USD base salary range represents only one component of total compensation for this role and is provided in accordance with local requirements. This role is eligible for a discretionary annual bonus, which is determined based on individual and overall firm performance. In addition to salary and bonus, total compensation may include eligibility for long-term reward programs and a comprehensive total rewards package that may include retirement, health, insurance, paid time off, and wellness programs. Our total rewards offerings are influenced by several business factors, and eligibility for certain components will vary by position and geography. Please note the posted ranges do not apply outside the U.S. and should not be converted to other currencies as a proxy for compensation in other countries.

jobs.title: Senior Associate, Global Private Wealth Operations
jobs.description:
Job Description Summary

For over forty years, HarbourVest has been home to a committed team of professionals with an entrepreneurial spirit and a desire to deliver impactful solutions to our clients and investing partners. As our global firm grows, we continue to add individuals who seek a collaborative, open-door culture that values diversity and innovative thinking.

In our collegial environment that’s marked by low turnover and high energy, you’ll be inspired to grow and thrive. Here, you will be encouraged to build on your strengths and acquire new skills and experiences.

We are committed to fostering an environment of inclusion that promotes mutual respect among all employees. Understanding and valuing these differences optimizes the potential of both the individual and the firm.

HarbourVest is an equal opportunity employer.

This position will be a hybrid work arrangement. You will receive 18 remote workdays per quarter to use at your discretion, subject to manager approval. For example, you may choose to work in the office 4 days per week and take one remote day weekly (typically 13 weeks per quarter), leaving 5 additional remote days to be used as needed.

The Senior Associate, Global Private Wealth Operations will serve as support function to the Head of Global Private Wealth and the Chief Operating Officer, Global Private Wealth—providing leverage, throughput, and organizational lift for both C-suite executives. This position is responsible for providing dependable organizational and project support, providing executive readiness for meetings and client interactions, and ensuring key initiatives are organized and progress smoothly throughout the team providing follow up, as necessary.

The ideal candidate is someone who:

  • Builds simple systems to track decisions, actions, and deadlines.
  • Is a self-starter, able to work independently and manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment.
  • Maintains composure under pressure; and is comfortable with intensity, ambiguity, and fast-changing priorities—able to push back respectfully and follow up persistently to drive closure.

What you will do:

  • Leadership Leverage & Execution Support: Support leaders to clarify priorities, translate them into actionable plans, and drive progress on operational improvements and special projects through strong coordination, tracking, and follow-through.
  • Executive Communications & Materials: Draft and coordinate materials for leaders (e.g., memos, presentations, dashboards) and ensure input, reviews, and deadlines are managed across internal stakeholders.
  • Meeting & Agenda Management: Work with EA’s to coordinate and facilitate leadership meetings, prepare agendas, ensure follow-ups, and track key action items.
  • Operational Coordination: Partner with operations, finance, and investment teams to support reporting rhythms, surface dependencies/risks, and help drive process improvements.
  • Issue Identification & Follow-Through: Spot operational bottlenecks, track down potential solution resources, and coordinate owners to implement fixes and track outcomes.
  • Confidentiality: Handle sensitive information with the utmost integrity and discretion during leadership meetings, maintaining key details for governance purposes as necessary.
  • And other responsibilities as required

What you bring :

  • Exceptional organizational, analytical, and problem-solving skills.
  • Strong written and verbal communication abilities (especially the ability to draft clear, concise leader-ready materials). The ability to turn rough inputs into clear, concise written output.
  • Highly proficient in Microsoft Office Suite (PowerPoint, Excel, Word)
  • Strong interpersonal skills with the ability to work cross-functionally and at all organizational levels

Education Preferred

  • Bachelor of Arts (B.A), Bachelor of Science (B.S), or equivalent experience

Experience

  • 5+ years in management consulting, business operations, investment operations, finance, program/project management, or a similar execution-oriented role. Private equity or fund of funds experience highly preferred.

#LI-Hybrid

Base Salary Range

$130,000.00 - $145,000.00

This USD base salary range represents only one component of total compensation for this role and is provided in accordance with local requirements. This role is eligible for a discretionary annual bonus, which is determined based on individual and overall firm performance. In addition to salary and bonus, total compensation may include eligibility for long-term reward programs and a comprehensive total rewards package that may include retirement, health, insurance, paid time off, and wellness programs. Our total rewards offerings are influenced by several business factors, and eligibility for certain components will vary by position and geography. Please note the posted ranges do not apply outside the U.S. and should not be converted to other currencies as a proxy for compensation in other countries.

jobs.title: M&A Associate Director
jobs.description:
Job Title: M&A Associate Director

Contract Type: Permanent, full-time

Location: London, St Pauls

Department: Investment Office

What the Hiring Manager says….

“As an Associate Director with an M&A background in the Investment office, you will play a pivotal role in ensuring our portfolio companies have the right processes, governance and teams in place to originate and execute acquisitions. You will push and challenge these companies, help with origination where possible, and monitor progress to ensure sufficient focus on M&A at the company level. Your extensive network across Europe will be highly relevant in this role.”

Summary of our opportunity

You will be responsible for supporting portfolio companies in their inorganic growth strategies. This role involves ensuring that these companies have efficient origination processes and teams to identify and execute high-value acquisition opportunities. You will work closely with senior management and investment teams. Impact will be measured through value driven for the various stakeholders.

The position will focus on portfolio companies from Europe and Mid-Market Funds with a current portfolio of ~50 companies. The position will be based on London, reporting to the Head of the Investment Office.

Primary responsibilities for this role include (but are not limited to):

Strategic Leadership

  • Ensure portfolio companies have the right processes and teams in place for M&A activities.
  • Push and challenge companies to optimize their origination and execution strategies.

Transaction Support

  • Assist in the origination of acquisition opportunities where possible.
  • Monitor and report on the progress of M&A activities at the company level.

Stakeholder Engagement

  • Build and maintain strong relationships with internal and external stakeholders, including investors, advisors, and portfolio company management teams.
  • Leverage your network across Europe to identify and support acquisition opportunities.

Key Requirements/ Qualifications

You will have demonstrable experience of: 

  • Extensive experience in M&A with private equity funds but more importantly Corporates.
  • Proven track record of successful deal origination and execution.
  • Strong financial acumen and analytical skills.
  • Excellent negotiation and communication skills.
  • Ability to lead cross-functional teams and manage complex projects.
  • Nationality and fluency in two or three languages, English ideally combined with French, German, or Spanish.

Personal Attributes

Personal attributes that will make you successful in this role include but are not limited to: 

  • Ability to push things forward and drive progress
  • Good project manager given the multitude of projects across the portfolio
  • Proactive approach to problem-solving.
  • Strong leadership and interpersonal skills
  • Collaborative and team-oriented.

Our Global Benefits Framework is centred around these 3 key attributes:

  • Wellbeing - Supporting your mental, physical and financial wellbeing as well as your lifestyle needs.
  • Inclusivity – Fit for purpose and competitive, no matter who or where you are.
  • Longevity – Long Term and sustainable in design.

Inclusion is one of ICG’s key values and we are committed to creating an equitable and inclusive workplace for everyone. By welcoming different perspectives, we strengthen ICG’s performance, enrich decision-making and contribute to better outcomes for our clients, colleagues, and the markets we serve. Accordingly, our recruitment processes provide equal employment opportunities to all applicants and employees, regardless of age, ethnicity, disability, sex, gender, sexual orientation, religion, or any other characteristic protected by local law.

We are equally committed to delivering an inclusive and accessible candidate experience. If you require information in an alternative format, additional time for assessments, an adjusted interview schedule, or any other support during the recruitment process, please let us know.

jobs.title: Vice President, Private Credit
jobs.description:
As one of the world’s leading independent global investment firms, Invesco is dedicated to rethinking possibilities for our clients. By delivering the combined power of our distinctive investment management capabilities, we provide a wide range of investment strategies and vehicles to our clients around the world. If you're looking for challenging work, intelligent colleagues, and exposure across a global footprint, come explore your potential at Invesco.

Job Description

We have an outstanding benefits package which includes: 

  • Company-provided healthcare 

  • A competitive annual leave allowance 

  • Flexible working options, including hybrid working arrangements 

  • Generous pension provisions 

  • Income protection 

  • Health and wellness benefits 

  • Volunteering days 

  • Enhanced parental leave 

  • Life insurance 

Your job:

We’re looking for a Vice President , Private Credit to join our Invesco Senior Secured Management team in London!

This role offers the opportunity to work across the full credit spectrum — from senior secured loans to HY bonds and private credit — while taking ownership of deep sector coverage, investment analysis, and participation in complex credit situations.

If you enjoy fundamental credit work, thrive in a fast‑paced investment environment, and want to help shape portfolio decisions, we’d love to hear from you.

Among your responsibilities will be:

Investment Analysis & Credit Underwriting

  • Review and prepare credit theses for new senior secured loans, high yield bonds (fixed & floating), and middle‑market private investments.

  • Produce detailed underwriting memos, covering company/industry analysis, capital structure, cash‑flow projections, collateral assessment, sponsor quality, and restructuring considerations.

  • Present comprehensive investment memos to the Investment Committee with clear theses and risk mitigants.

Sector Coverage & Monitoring

  • Build in‑depth knowledge across 2–3 industries and maintain coverage of 25–30 issuers.

  • Conduct ongoing monitoring, including financial analysis, cash‑flow modelling, industry deep‑dives, rating reviews, and performance assessments.

  • Actively generate new investment ideas within your coverage universe.

Distressed & Secondary Opportunities

  • Identify secondary market opportunities.

  • Support the team lead in workout and distressed situations.

Market Interaction & Due Diligence

  • Build a strong network across the market to understand sentiment on amendments and new deals.

  • Engage with syndicate desks, sell‑side analysts, and private equity sponsors on both primary and secondary opportunities.

  • Participate in management meetings, site visits, plant tours, and expert calls.

Risk Management & Administration

  • Maintain electronic credit files, compliance reporting, collateral records, and exposure tracking.

What you bring

  • Solid experience as a credit analyst covering credits and underwriting investments in leveraged loans / high yield bonds / direct lending.

  • Strong proficiency in Microsoft Office, particularly Excel and Word.

  • Bachelor’s degree required; CFA preferred.

  • Additional European language(s) are a plus.

  • Logical thinker with the ability to “join the dots.”

  • Strong financial modelling, quantitative and qualitative analysis skills.

  • Excellent written and verbal communication; comfortable presenting to senior stakeholders.

  • Ability to distil key insights from large datasets.

  • Highly detail‑oriented with strong accuracy and follow‑through.

  • Self‑motivated, able to multi‑task and work independently.

  • Proactive, solution‑focused mindset with unquestionable integrity.

  • Strong interpersonal skills and a collaborative working style.

If you’re looking to take the next step in your credit investing career — with responsibility, autonomy, and exposure across the capital structure — we’d be excited to speak with you.

Disability Confident Scheme: 

Applicants who opt in to the Disability Confident Scheme and meet the criteria for the role will be offered an interview. We are committed to providing an inclusive recruitment process for all candidates who make an application. By opting-in to this scheme, applicants will be disclosing that they have a disability solely for the purpose of the Disability Confident Scheme.   

The Disability Confident Scheme only guarantees an interview – it does not automatically mean that applicants interviewed will gain employment with Invesco at that time.  

To apply through the Disability Confident Scheme, you’ll need to firstly ensure you have applied for the role via our external careers page. Following this, you’ll need to email us at emea-talentacquisition@invesco.comconfirming your wish to opt-in, alongside your contact details and the title of the role you wish to apply for.

Full Time / Part Time

Full time

Worker Type

Employee

Job Exempt (Yes / No)

Yes

Workplace Model


Pursuant to Invesco’s Workplace Policy, employees are expected to comply with the firm’s most current workplace model, which as of October 1, 2025, includes spending at least four full days each week working in the role's advertised office. This reflects our belief that spending time together in the office helps us build stronger relationships, collaborate more easily, and support each other’s growth and development.

If this sounds like you, we’d love to hear from you! We want all of our candidates to shine during the application and selection process, so if you need any adjustments to be made, please send an e-mail to emea-talentacquisition@invesco.com. Please include your name, the job you are interested in, and the type of adjustment you need (for example; breaks during your interview, remote interviews, additional time for assessments or other required adjustments)

We promote a working environment that welcomes everyone and creates inclusive teams, celebrates difference and encourages everyone to be themselves at work.

Our commitment to the community and environmental, social and governance investing:
We partner with charitable organisations globally to make an impact in the communities where we live and work. Our people are encouraged to support the charities they feel most passionate about.  We are also committed to environmental, social and governance (ESG) investing. We serve our clients in this space as a trusted partner both on specific responsible investment product strategies as well as part of our commitment to deliver a superior investment experience. 

Recruitment Agencies: 
Invesco has an in-house recruitment team, which focuses on sourcing great candidates directly. Invesco will not accept unsolicited resumes from agency or search firm recruiters. Fees will not be paid in the event a candidate submitted by a recruiter without an agreement in place is hired. When we do use agencies, we have a PSL in place, so please do not contact hiring managers directly.

Regulatory:
This position may fall in-scope of one or multiple regimes/directives.

jobs.title: Senior Risk Analyst, Private Credit Investment Risk
jobs.description:
As one of the world’s leading independent global investment firms, Invesco is dedicated to rethinking possibilities for our clients. By delivering the combined power of our distinctive investment management capabilities, we provide a wide range of investment strategies and vehicles to our clients around the world. If you're looking for challenging work, intelligent colleagues, and exposure across a global footprint, come explore your potential at Invesco.

Job Description

We have an outstanding benefits package which includes: 

  • Company-provided healthcare 

  • A competitive annual leave allowance 

  • Flexible working options, including hybrid working arrangements 

  • Generous pension provisions 

  • Income protection 

  • Health and wellness benefits 

  • Volunteering days 

  • Enhanced parental leave 

  • Life insurance 

Your role:

You will support Invesco’s risk management capabilities by providing transparency to the senior team on all investment related risks for Private Credit (CLO, Broadly Syndicated Loans, Direct Lending, Special Situations/Distressed and other) portfolios. You will play a key role in the development and implementation of best practices to ensure effective management of market, counterparty, and liquidity risk on behalf of Invesco’s clients.

Key Responsibilities:

  • Work with the Global Head of Alternatives Risk and the Risk Director in formulating direction around Investment Risk strategy and objectives in alignment with Invesco’s mission and investment philosophy.

  • Facilitate investment performance and positive client outcomes through the delivery of commercially aware value-added insights along with effective collaboration with the Investment team.

  • Drive delivery of strategic projects related to technology, data, and human capital to ensure Private Credit Investment Risk has the necessary tools and resources to meet stated objectives.

  • Advance the ongoing buildout of data analytics and data libraries to support deep dive analysis across Private Credit portfolios.

  • Identify key risk exposures and performance drivers; define and monitor risk guidelines and develop quantitative analytics to support risk estimation and management.

  • Maintain an active dialogue with the Private Credit investment teams and facilitate discussion between investment team members around risk profiles, changes to risk composition and firmwide risk appetite.

  • Gather necessary data and develop presentation materials for periodic board updates to ensure Invesco leadership is aware of the performance and state of risk across all Private Credit funds.

  • Continually develop and refine Private Credit Investment Risk’s capabilities to understand the drivers of portfolio performance and alpha generation beyond benchmarking.

  • Lead the ongoing review of risk limits and effective challenge process in consultation with cross-functional teams.

  • Ensure the Private Credit Investment Risk strategy continuously reflects current business environment, market conditions, and legal/regulatory requirements.

  • Anticipate new and emerging risks and play a critical role in providing insights and guidance on the positioning of portfolios to be successful across a wide range of market conditions.

  • Provide guidance for Private Credit risk governance policies and procedures to ensure Private Credit risk meets business/regulatory requirements and function as designed/planned.

What you can bring:

  • Proven experience in relevant financial markets, including at an Asset Manager, Hedge Fund, or Investment Bank.

  • Experience with financial or risk modeling, data analysis of large data sets, dashboard/visualization creation, leveraged loan markets and/or credit analysis.

  • Demonstrated track record building and fostering a wide range of stakeholder relationships and needs.

  • Collaborative and motivated by working in close partnership with colleagues as part of Investment Risk team.

  • Bachelor’s Degree in relevant financial field a plus (Finance, Business, Risk Management, Financial Engineering, etc.).

  • CFA, CAIA, or FRM designation(s) a plus.

Following this, you’ll need to email us at emea-talentacquisition@invesco.comconfirming your wish to opt-in, alongside your contact details and the title of the role you wish to apply for.  

Full Time / Part Time

Full time

Worker Type

Employee

Job Exempt (Yes / No)

Yes

Workplace Model


Pursuant to Invesco’s Workplace Policy, employees are expected to comply with the firm’s most current workplace model, which as of October 1, 2025, includes spending at least four full days each week working in the role's advertised office. This reflects our belief that spending time together in the office helps us build stronger relationships, collaborate more easily, and support each other’s growth and development.

If this sounds like you, we’d love to hear from you! We want all of our candidates to shine during the application and selection process, so if you need any adjustments to be made, please send an e-mail to emea-talentacquisition@invesco.com. Please include your name, the job you are interested in, and the type of adjustment you need (for example; breaks during your interview, remote interviews, additional time for assessments or other required adjustments)

jobs.title: Intern - Client Portfolio Management / Fixed Income
jobs.description:
About Invesco

As one of the world’s leading independent global investment firms, Invesco is dedicated to rethinking possibilities for ourclients. By delivering the combined power of our distinctive investment management capabilities, we provide a wide range of investment strategies and vehicles to our clients around the world. If you're looking for challenging work, intelligent colleagues, and exposure across a global footprint, come explore your potential at Invesco.

What's in it for you?

Our people are at the very core of our success. Invesco employees get more out of life through our comprehensive compensation and benefit offerings including: 

  • Generous annual leave plus flexible paid time off

  • Hybrid work schedule 

  • Learning and development programs

  • Health & wellbeing benefits 

  • Parental Leave benefits 

  • Employee stock purchase plan (if applicable in your region)

Job Description

Responsibilities

  • Support CPM team in client portfolio analysis and investment materials preparation

  • Conduct market and fixed income research, including macro and product-level insights

  • Assist in preparing client presentations, pitch materials and performance analysis reports

  • Help maintain internal data, trackers, and reporting materials

  • Provide general support to the investment / CPM team on ongoing projects

Requirements

  • Undergraduate or postgraduate student (Finance / Economics preferred)

  • Strong interest in asset management / fixed income markets

  • Prior internship experience in asset management / banking / research (preferred)

  • Good communication and language skills in English and Chinese

  • Proficient in Excel & PowerPoint

  • Able to commit 6 months full time

Full Time / Part Time

Full time

Worker Type

Student/Intern (Fixed Term)

Job Exempt (Yes / No)

No

Workplace Model


At Invesco, our workplace model supports our culture and meets the needs of our clients while providing flexibility our employees value. As a full-time employee, compliance with the workplace policy means working with your direct manager to create a schedule where you will work in your designated office at least four days a week, with one day working outside an Invesco office.  

Invesco's culture of inclusivity and its commitment to diversity in the workplace are demonstrated through our people practices. We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, creed, color, religion, sex, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, marital status, national origin, citizenship status, disability, age, or veteran status. Our equal opportunity employment efforts comply with all applicable local laws governing non-discrimination in employment.

Personal data provided by job applicants will be used strictly in accordance with our personal data policy and for recruitment purposes only. Candidates not notified within twelve weeks may consider their application unsuccessful. All related information will be kept in our file for future recruitment purposes. When there are vacancies in our subsidiary or associate companies, we may transfer the application to them for consideration of employment. Under the Personal Data (Privacy) Ordinance, job applicants have a right to request access to, and to request correction of, Personal Data in relation to the application. If job applicants wish to exercise these rights, they should contact the Human Resources department.

jobs.title: Sr. Equity ETF Strategist
jobs.description:
About Invesco

As one of the world’s leading independent global investment firms, Invesco is dedicated to rethinking possibilities for our clients. By delivering the combined power of our distinctive investment management capabilities, we provide a wide range of investment strategies and vehicles to our clients around the world. If you're looking for challenging work, intelligent colleagues, and exposure across a global footprint, come explore your potential at Invesco.

What’s in it for you?

Our people are at the very core of our success. Invesco employees get more out of life through our comprehensive compensation and benefit offerings including:

  • Flexible paid time off

  • Hybrid work schedule 

  • 401(K) matching of 100% up to the first 6% with a discretionary supplemental contribution 

  • Health & wellbeing benefits 

  • Parental Leave benefits 

  • Employee stock purchase plan

Job Description

The role:

The Sr. Equity ETF Strategist will leverage his/her capital markets, investment management, and product knowledge to deliver macro and equity market insights to help position products to institutions and financial advisors. The role will work collaboratively with Product Development, Product Management, Portfolio Management, Capital Markets, Sales and Marketing to position and grow the product line.


Responsibilities of the role:

• Drive growth of the equity ETF product line in partnership with cross-functional teams (sales, marketing, capital markets, product management, portfolio management and product development)

• Help build and reinforce a brand for Invesco ETF products by presenting at industry conferences and serving as the “voice” for the product line in media interviews, sponsored events, webcasts, etc.

• Lead efforts to develop and manage insights on the product line through asset class and product-specific communication ; product training and positioning ; product-specific competitive analysis ; market and product performance commentaries; and content development in partnership with Marketing

• Partner with Sales to execute product-specific distribution strategies and participate in client meetings

• Monitor macroeconomic conditions and equity market signals to formulate asset class views specific to product line, and translate them into actionable investment and product positioning ideas for clients

• Identify new product opportunities as well as assess existing product line to identify opportunities for enhancement

Requirements of the role:

• 10+ years of investment management or research experience, with strong understanding of equity markets.

jobs.title: Associate Director, Private Markets Product, Private Credit
jobs.description:
As one of the world’s leading independent global investment firms, Invesco is dedicated to rethinking possibilities for our clients. By delivering the combined power of our distinctive investment management capabilities, we provide a wide range of investment strategies and vehicles to our clients around the world. If you're looking for challenging work, intelligent colleagues, and exposure across a global footprint, come explore your potential at Invesco.

Job Description

We have an outstanding benefits package which includes:

  • Company-provided healthcare

  • A competitive annual leave allowance

  • Flexible working options, including hybrid working arrangements

  • Generous pension provisions

  • Income protection

  • Health and wellness benefits

  • Volunteering days

  • Enhanced parental leave

  • Life insurance

Your Role:

We are looking for an experienced Product Manager (Associate Director) to join our Private Markets Product team in London, focused on Private Credit!

This is a senior, high-impact role at the intersection of investment, distribution, and strategy. You will take end-to-end ownership of key product initiatives across our European private credit platform, including broadly syndicated loans, CLOs, direct lending, and special situations.

You will act as a core partner to Portfolio Managers and Client Portfolio Managers, shaping product strategy, driving commercial outcomes, and representing the platform internally and externally. This role requires a deep understanding of private credit markets, strong commercial judgment, and the ability to influence senior stakeholders across the business.

What you will be doing:

  • Own product strategy and positioning for key private credit capabilities across Europe, aligning with investment teams and distribution priorities

  • Act as a senior product lead for complex initiatives, including new product development, fund launches, and platform evolution

  • Partner closely with Portfolio Managers and CPMs to translate investment strategy into compelling client narratives and commercial solutions

  • Serve as a trusted advisor to Distribution, supporting fundraising strategies, investor engagement, and key client relationships

  • Represent product in senior client interactions, including due diligence meetings, roadshows, and strategic discussions

  • Lead high-impact deliverables, including thought leadership, market insights, and differentiated product messaging

  • Drive cross-functional execution across Legal, Compliance, Operations, and Marketing to ensure seamless product delivery

  • Contribute to the long-term strategic development of the private credit platform and broader private markets product function

  • Provide guidance and informal leadership to junior team members

What you will bring:

  • Strong experience in product management, investment, or client-facing roles within private credit / private markets

  • Deep expertise in private credit, including fund structures, strategies, portfolio construction, and performance drivers

  • Proven track record of owning complex initiatives and operating autonomously in a fast-paced, front-office environment

  • Strong experience partnering with senior stakeholders, including investment teams and distribution

  • Commercial mindset with the ability to translate investment content into client solutions and fundraising outcomes

  • Experience engaging with institutional investors and supporting fundraising efforts

  • Highly developed communication and influencing skills, with confidence in senior internal and external settings

  • Strong analytical capabilities and familiarity with tools such as Bloomberg, Preqin, eVestment, etc.

Disability Confident Scheme :

Applicants who opt in to the Disability Confident Scheme and meet the ‘minimum criteria’ for the role will be offered an interview. We are committed to providing an inclusive recruitment process for all candidates who make an application. By opting-in to this scheme, applicants will be disclosing that they have a disability solely for the purpose of the Disability Confident Scheme.

The Disability Confident Scheme only guarantees an interview – it does not automatically mean that applicants interviewed will gain employment with Invesco at that time.

To apply through the Disability Confident Scheme, you’ll need to firstly ensure you have applied for the role via our external careers page. Following this, you’ll need to email us at EMEA-TalentAcquisition@invesco.com confirming your wish to opt-in, alongside your contact details and the title of the role you wish to apply for.

Full Time / Part Time

Full time

Worker Type

Employee

Job Exempt (Yes / No)

Yes

Workplace Model


Pursuant to Invesco’s Workplace Policy, employees are expected to comply with the firm’s most current workplace model, which as of October 1, 2025, includes spending at least four full days each week working in an Invesco office. This reflects our belief that spending time together in the office helps us build stronger relationships, collaborate more easily, and support each other’s growth and development.

If this sounds like you, we’d love to hear from you! We want all of our candidates to shine during the application and selection process, so if you need any adjustments to be made, please send an e-mail to emea-talentacquisition@invesco.com. Please include your name, the job you are interested in, and the type of adjustment you need (for example; breaks during your interview, remote interviews, additional time for assessments or other required adjustments)

We promote a working environment that welcomes everyone and creates inclusive teams, celebrates difference and encourages everyone to be themselves at work.

Our commitment to the community and environmental, social and governance investing:
We partner with charitable organisations globally to make an impact in the communities where we live and work. Our people are encouraged to support the charities they feel most passionate about.  We are also committed to environmental, social and governance (ESG) investing. We serve our clients in this space as a trusted partner both on specific responsible investment product strategies as well as part of our commitment to deliver a superior investment experience. 

Recruitment Agencies: 
Invesco has an in-house recruitment team, which focuses on sourcing great candidates directly. Invesco will not accept unsolicited resumes from agency or search firm recruiters. Fees will not be paid in the event a candidate submitted by a recruiter without an agreement in place is hired. When we do use agencies, we have a PSL in place, so please do not contact hiring managers directly.

Regulatory:
This position may fall in-scope of one or multiple regimes/directives.

jobs.title: Associate Credit Analyst
jobs.description:
About Invesco

As one of the world’s leading independent global investment firms, Invesco is dedicated to rethinking possibilities for our clients. By delivering the combined power of our distinctive investment management capabilities, we provide a wide range of investment strategies and vehicles to our clients around the world. If you're looking for challenging work, intelligent colleagues, and exposure across a global footprint, come explore your potential at Invesco.

What’s in it for you?

Our people are at the very core of our success. Invesco employees get more out of life through our comprehensive compensation and benefit offerings including:

  • Flexible paid time off

  • Hybrid work schedule 

  • 401(K) matching of 100% up to the first 6% with a discretionary supplemental contribution 

  • Health & wellbeing benefits 

  • Parental Leave benefits 

  • Employee stock purchase plan

Job Description

Your Team

The Global Liquidity Research team supports the investment process for Invesco’s liquidity portfolios through fundamental credit research, issuer surveillance, and investment recommendation support. The team works closely with portfolio managers, the Global Liquidity Research Committee, risk partners, and other internal stakeholders to evaluate current and potential investments. The team is responsible for maintaining a disciplined credit framework consistent with portfolio objectives, regulatory requirements, rating agency considerations, and applicable fund guidelines. This role sits within a collaborative research environment where clear communication, sound judgment, intellectual curiosity, deep sector expertise, and attention to detail are critical.

Your Role

The Associate Credit Analyst will perform fundamental credit analysis of current and prospective issuers and securities for Global Liquidity portfolios. The role will assess issuer financial condition, legal structure, business profile, liquidity, credit quality, market developments, and investment suitability within applicable fund guidelines. The analyst will serve as a member of the Global Liquidity Research Committee and contribute to issuer and security approval decisions. The role also offers the opportunity to develop deep sector expertise, support client-facing discussions, contribute to thought leadership, and participate in broader Invesco Fixed Income sector teams alongside analysts from across fixed income. Through this work, the analyst will contribute to issuer approvals, portfolio risk management, client engagement, and the broader Invesco Fixed Income investment process. This role requires sound analytical judgment, clear communication, effective coordination with investment colleagues, and the ability to work independently within established policies, procedures, and regulatory requirements.

You Will Be Responsible For:

  • Perform fundamental credit analysis of current and potential holdings, including review of issuer financial condition, legal structure, business profile, liquidity profile, capital structure, investment suitability, and key credit risks.

  • Build and maintain deep sector expertise across assigned coverage areas, developing a strong command of issuer fundamentals, sector dynamics, market structure, regulatory developments, rating agency perspectives, and emerging risks or opportunities.

  • Develop and articulate defensible credit recommendations and issuer reviews supported by financial analysis, market data, rating agency materials, sell-side research, and internal research.

  • Serve as a member of the Global Liquidity Research Committee, contributing to the review and approval of issuers and securities for purchase by portfolio managers.

  • Collaborate with portfolio managers to evaluate securities within portfolio objectives, fund restrictions, regulatory requirements, liquidity needs, and approved credit parameters.

  • Actively participate in broader Invesco Fixed Income sector teams by sharing sector views, issuer updates, and market insights with analysts and investment colleagues across strategies.

  • Cultivate relationships with sell-side sales, origination, and research teams, as well as rating agency analysts and other external market participants, to support issuer surveillance, market awareness, and credit analysis.

  • Contribute to client-facing materials, client presentations, thought leadership, market updates, and internal presentations that communicate Global Liquidity credit views and relevant market themes.

The Experience You Bring:

  • 1–3 years of experience in financial services, credit analysis, accounting, investment research, portfolio management, or a related analytical role.

  • Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Accounting, Economics, Business, or a related field; progress toward CFA designation or an advanced degree is preferred.

  • Working knowledge of financial statement analysis, credit fundamentals, accounting concepts, market data, and investment research methods.

  • Familiarity with money market funds, short-duration fixed income, SEC Rule 2a-7, rating agency methodologies, or investment management regulation is preferred.

  • Strong analytical and quantitative skills, with the ability to interpret financial data, identify key risks, and develop well-reasoned conclusions.

  • Effective written, verbal, and interpersonal communication skills, including the ability to simplify complex information for portfolio managers, analysts, clients, and other stakeholders.

  • Proficiency with Bloomberg, Excel, PowerPoint, Word, and Microsoft Copilot or similar AI-enabled productivity tools, with the ability to use technology responsibly to improve research efficiency, drafting, organization, and workflow productivity while maintaining analytical judgment, confidentiality, and quality control.

  • Intellectual curiosity, commitment to developing sector expertise, high standards of accuracy and integrity, strong attention to detail, and the ability to manage priorities under time-sensitive conditions.

Full Time / Part Time

Full time

Worker Type

Employee

Job Exempt (Yes / No)

Yes

Workplace Model

Pursuant to Invesco’s Workplace Policy, employees are expected to comply with the firm’s most current workplace model, which as of October 1, 2025, includes spending at least four full days each week working in an Invesco office. This reflects our belief that spending time together in the office helps us build stronger relationships, collaborate more easily, and support each other’s growth and development.

It is not designed to contain or be interpreted as a comprehensive inventory of all duties, responsibilities and qualifications required of employees assigned to this job. The job holder may be required to perform other duties as deemed appropriate by their manager from time to time.

All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, creed, color, religion, sex, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, marital status, national origin, citizenship status, disability, age, or veteran status. Our equal opportunity employment efforts comply with all applicable U.S. state and federal laws governing non-discrimination in employment.

jobs.description:
About Invesco

As one of the world’s leading independent global investment firms, Invesco is dedicated to rethinking possibilities for ourclients. By delivering the combined power of our distinctive investment management capabilities, we provide a wide range of investment strategies and vehicles to our clients around the world. If you're looking for challenging work, intelligent colleagues, and exposure across a global footprint, come explore your potential at Invesco.

What's in it for you?

Our people are at the very core of our success. Invesco employees get more out of life through our comprehensive compensation and benefit offerings including: 

  • Generous annual leave plus flexible paid time off

  • Hybrid work schedule 

  • Learning and development programs

  • Health & wellbeing benefits 

  • Parental Leave benefits 

  • Employee stock purchase plan (if applicable in your region)

Job Description

About the Role:

Invesco’s Product Research & Insights team in EMEA and APAC is a dynamic group that drives deep analysis of products, markets, and asset flows to uncover insights that sharpen product positioning, strengthen client engagement, inform product development, and unlock growth opportunities across EMEA and APAC.

Responsibilities of the role:

  • Serve as the primary subject‑matter expert on Invesco’s product suite, competitor offerings, and local versus regional industry trends across APAC public and private markets

  • Conduct in‑depth product research to identify competitive advantages and develop differentiated value propositions for internal and external stakeholders

  • Lead the analysis of client demand trends, fund flows, and competitor positioning, synthesising insights into clear recommendations for product development, enhancement, or rationalisation

  • Partner closely with the regional product development team to assess Invesco’s product shelf, identify critical gaps, and support the origination and structuring of new products

  • Act as an APAC ambassador for the broader Product Research & Insights (PR&I) team, leading local initiatives, fostering engagement, and contributing to regional decision making through committee participation and presentations

  • Serve as a strategic partner to regional distribution teams, supporting client engagement through high‑quality product positioning and market‑driven insights

  • Contribute to the client‑led product prioritisation process for APAC, ensuring distribution efforts are aligned with areas of highest client demand and strongest product potential

  • Ensure all activities are in line with Invesco’s Conduct principles

Requirement for the Role:

  • University degree related to financial markets; CFA or similar qualification preferred

  • 8+ years’ asset management experience covering product research, strategy, positioning, and senior stakeholder engagement

  • Solid understanding of investment vehicles and fund structures (e.g. mutual funds, UCITS, ETFs, SMAs, alternatives), and how global financial markets and regulations operate.

  • Good knowledge of portfolio construction and risk/return trade-offs, with an understanding of product positioning and client evaluation

  • Familiarity with market trends, macro drivers, and evolving client needs

  • Proficiency in tools such as Morningstar, eVestment, Broadridge, Preqin, or Bloomberg; highly analytical with the ability to turn data into insights

  • Proven ability in relationship building, managing stakeholders, influencing decisions, and leading cross-functional initiatives

  • Excellent communication skills in English with the ability to simplify complex and technical concepts

  • Experience with Python, VBA, or other automation tools preferred
     

How to Apply:

  •  Apply for the role directly at Invesco Careers: Careers at Invesco | APAC.

To know more about us:

  • About Invesco: https://www.invesco.com/corporate/en/home.html

  • About our Culture: https://www.invesco.com/corporate/en/about-us/our-culture.html

  • About our CR program: https://www.invesco.com/corporate/en/our-commitments/corporate-responsibility.html
