Full time

Worker Type

Employee

Job Exempt (Yes / No)

Yes

Workplace Model


At Invesco, our workplace model supports our culture and meets the needs of our clients while providing flexibility our employees value. As a full-time employee, compliance with the workplace policy means working with your direct manager to create a schedule where you will work in your designated office at least four days a week, with one day working outside an Invesco office.  

Invesco's culture of inclusivity and its commitment to diversity in the workplace are demonstrated through our people practices. We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, creed, color, religion, sex, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, marital status, national origin, citizenship status, disability, age, or veteran status. Our equal opportunity employment efforts comply with all applicable local laws governing non-discrimination in employment.

Personal data provided by job applicants will be used strictly in accordance with our personal data policy and for recruitment purposes only. Candidates not notified within twelve weeks may consider their application unsuccessful. All related information will be kept in our file for future recruitment purposes. When there are vacancies in our subsidiary or associate companies, we may transfer the application to them for consideration of employment. Under the Personal Data (Privacy) Ordinance, job applicants have a right to request access to, and to request correction of, Personal Data in relation to the application. If job applicants wish to exercise these rights, they should contact the Human Resources department.

Job Description

The ETF Product Strategy Group within the Americas Product is committed to providing superior client support and actionable investment insights that drive our product line’s strategic agenda for growth. This individual will work closely with others on the team and help develop ETF research and analysis that positions our fixed income and alternatives ETFs in the current macro environment. The Sr. Analyst will work cross-functionally with Product Development, Portfolio Management, Capital Markets, Sales and Marketing to position and grow the product line.

Responsibilities of the Role:

  • Work alongside analysts and strategists on the team to develop product segment research and analysis on Invesco ETFs, the ETF industry, and broader financial markets, serving as a subject matter expert for the product line

  • Develop and update product materials that position Invesco’s ETFs

  • Project manage and lead research projects as assigned by senior leaders

  • Execute on actions needed to maintain and improve the product line

  • Partner closely with ETF strategists and specialists to support our clients and ensure product integrity, occasionally participating in client meetings

Requirements of the Role:

  • 3-5 years of investment management and/or research experience preferred

  • Skills and professional development in a senior analyst role with a desire to take on more responsibility and accountability

  • Previous experience or solid understanding of fixed-income and alternatives investing strongly preferred

  • ETF and index familiarity and industry experience required

  • Experience with Bloomberg, Morningstar Direct, FactSet, Python a plus

  • Strong research and analytical skills to analyze and interpret various market research reports and data and apply such information to daily activities

  • Strong subject matter acumen and written communication skills

  • Team player with excellent interpersonal skills; maintain effective, cooperative working relationships with all departments and organizational levels

  • Self-directed; must be comfortable with establishing objectives, then working independently to achieve them, seeking the team’s input as needed

  • Undergraduate degree in business, finance, mathematics or related discipline required

  • Series 7 & 63 (already licensed is a plus, can study for these exams after being hired)

  • CFA track or progress towards advanced strongly preferred

jobs.title: Client Portfolio Manager - Fixed Income
jobs.description:
About Invesco

As one of the world’s leading independent global investment firms, Invesco is dedicated to rethinking possibilities for our clients. By delivering the combined power of our distinctive investment management capabilities, we provide a wide range of investment strategies and vehicles to our clients around the world. If you're looking for challenging work, intelligent colleagues, and exposure across a global footprint, come explore your potential at Invesco.

What’s in it for you?

Our people are at the very core of our success. Invesco employees get more out of life through our comprehensive compensation and benefit offerings including:

  • Flexible paid time off

  • Hybrid work schedule 

  • 401(K) matching of 100% up to the first 6% with a discretionary supplemental contribution 

  • Health & wellbeing benefits 

  • Parental Leave benefits 

  • Employee stock purchase plan

Job Description

The Invesco Fixed Income (IFI) platform is built on a foundation of deep, proprietary research, robust macroeconomic views and a global network of investment professionals who bring regional expertise. IFI’s robust capabilities span taxable and municipal product offerings including multi-sector as well as sector specific solutions. Our platform’s strength lies in the consistent application of top-down relative value and economic views alongside proprietary fundamental research. We seek to deliver outperformance in a variety of markets within an established risk framework, moderating our risk as conditions and views warrant. The consistency of our process is predicated on stable teams who collaborate across sectors and regions. Ultimately, our client-first culture and disciplined active management empower investors to navigate complex markets with clarity and confidence.

Quick Facts:

●           $536 billion in assets under management as of December 31, 2025

●           175 investment professionals averaging 18 years in the industry and 13 years with Invesco

●           12 primary strategies with a presence in 12 key global markets

Job Purpose:

The IFI Client Portfolio Manager (CPM) acts as an integral part of the portfolio management team and possesses deep knowledge of capital markets and the team’s investment philosophy and process across specified strategies. The CPM is regarded as a credible investment professionals who acts as the primary interface between our client-facing resources and the investment team. The CPM interacts with the portfolio management team daily to understand historical and current portfolio positioning, market conditions and portfolio themes. The CPM is able to communicate performance attribution, the rationale for specific holdings and portfolio activity, risk attributes, and views of the team clearly and succinctly to a variety of institutional clients and institutional / retail stakeholders. The CPM will maintain detailed knowledge of the competitive landscape/peer group, stay current on market trends and developments in all relevant distribution channels, and be able to clearly articulate what differentiates our products from the competition. The CPM acts in partnership with the Client Portfolio Analyst and Product Managers on business plans and marketing content across the product suite they represent.

This is a multi-faceted, dynamic, and challenging role that demands outstanding skills in communications, keen attention to detail, a self-starting attitude, the ability to develop and cultivate trust, business acumen, and analytical prowess. Invesco seeks to hire highly engaged, confident individuals, who do not settle for mediocrity, continually strive for excellence with their firm and peers, and who retain a high degree of intellectual curiosity. 

Key Responsibilities / Duties: 

  • Grow and retain business by representing the investment team and key investment strategies to clients. 

  • Service existing clients via in-person meetings (on- and off-site), conference calls and board / investment committee meetings, as needed in place of Portfolio Managers, or in support of them.

  • Provide end-to-end client servicing such that institutional clients’ needs are met with the same high standards they come to expect with Invesco products.

  • Regularly reflect the investment team by communicating existing (and potential) investment strategies to clients. Provide portfolio updates and insights to drive client engagement and loyalty.

  • Drive the development of research, presentations and investment insights for clients. This includes monitoring key investment and industry trends, working with the thought leadership team to identifytopics that resonate with investors. This individual will leverage publications, speaking engagements, public relations and marketing channels to drive key messages into the market.

  • Attend and present at industry and client conferences including Invesco roundtables, industry events, and PR opportunities.

Secondary Responsibilities / Duties: 

  • Play a key role in developing institutional and wealth opportunities by working with the distribution teams to educate consultants, investment platform gatekeepers and prospects on investment grade and high yield investment strategies.

  • Develop and modify collateral including Finals presentations, DDQs, RFPs and due diligence meeting presentations as needed.

  • Build strong relationships with the Distribution teams supporting their efforts to execute an effective sales strategy.

​Skills / Personal Attributes Required:

  • 10+ years of investment-related client-facing experience preferably covering U.S. investment grade and/or high yield strategies.

  • Experience working with investment platform research analysts, field level financial advisors, retirement plan gatekeepers, investment consultants and/or institutional investment committees for corporations, public entities, and/or endowments and foundations.

  • Deep investment expertise including an understanding of portfolio construction, risk drivers, market dynamics, and product-specific nuances.

  • entrepreneurial spirit, with a focus on continuous improvement, willingness to take risks, embrace and lead change, determination to find creative solutions to problems. Strong work ethic and drive to succeed.

  • Technical skills, including strong investment acumen, understanding of financial markets and economics, proficiency with Bloomberg, Microsoft Office, Morningstar and other software products and comfortability adopting AI tools to enhance productivity.

  • Strong communication skills, both written and verbal.

  • Ability to collaborate and communicate well with others. Strong team player.

  • Expected at least 25-50% travel.

Formal Education:

  • Undergraduate degree required, preferably in business or related discipline.

  • MBA/CFA and/or direct money management experience preferred.

  • Series 7 and 63 required.

jobs.title: Client Portfolio Manager - Custom Equity Platform
jobs.description:
About Invesco

As one of the world’s leading independent global investment firms, Invesco is dedicated to rethinking possibilities for our clients. By delivering the combined power of our distinctive investment management capabilities, we provide a wide range of investment strategies and vehicles to our clients around the world. If you're looking for challenging work, intelligent colleagues, and exposure across a global footprint, come explore your potential at Invesco.

What’s in it for you?

Our people are at the very core of our success. Invesco employees get more out of life through our comprehensive compensation and benefit offerings including:

  • Flexible paid time off

  • Hybrid work schedule 

  • 401(K) matching of 100% up to the first 6% with a discretionary supplemental contribution 

  • Health & wellbeing benefits 

  • Parental Leave benefits 

  • Employee stock purchase plan

Job Description

About the Department / Team:

Invesco is a well-established provider of separately managed account solutions through our Custom SMA platform.  These solutions seek to provide investors with customizable and tax-efficient exposure to equity and fixed income asset classes. The rapidly growing Custom Equity SMA platform is focused on offering highly customized long-only and long/short equity portfolios that allow, among other things, a range of tax management techniques and funding options.

About the Role:

The Client Portfolio Manager (CPM) acts as an integral part of the portfolio management team and possesses deep knowledge of capital markets and the team’s investment philosophy and quantitative investment process. The CPM is regarded as a credible investor who acts as the primary interface between our client-facing teams and the investment team. The CPM interacts with the portfolio management team daily to understand past and current portfolio positioning, performance attribution, the rationale for specific holdings and portfolio activity, risk attributes, views of the team/asset class, and has primary responsibility for conveying this information effectively to retail and institutional clients including gatekeepers, research analysts, consultants, and internal colleagues. The CPM will maintain detailed knowledge of the competitive landscape/peer group, stay current on market trends and developments in all relevant distribution channels, and be able to clearly articulate what differentiates our products from the competition. The CPM acts in partnership with rest of the CPM team, the SMA team, marketing, and other relevant partners on business and marketing related projects. The result is a multi-faceted, dynamic, and challenging role that demands highly developed skills in communications, stage presence, relationship building, business acumen, and analytics. We hire highly engaged, confident individuals who are persistently curious, will not settle for average, and continually strive to improve themselves, their work, and their environment.

Responsibilities of the Role:

  • Grow and retain business by representing the investment team and key investment strategies to clients.
  • Play a lead role in managing new business opportunities by working with the distribution teams to educate advisors, prospects, and investment platform gatekeepers on Custom Equity strategies – this includes participation in the RFP/RFI process and addressing ad hoc prospect questions.
  • Participate in advisor pitches, due diligence meetings, and stand and deliver presentations as needed.
  • Service existing clients via in-person meetings (on- and off-site), conference calls and investment committee meetings, as needed in place of Portfolio Managers.
  • Provide portfolio updates and insights to drive client engagement and loyalty.
  • Understand distribution opportunities in each channel and help shape product strategy with the Senior CPM and the product manager.
  • Participate in the development of advisor-facing content, presentations, and research.
  • This includes monitoring key investment and industry trends, working with research and product development and identifying issues and topics that leverage insights into the marketplace.
  • Manage the ongoing internal and external experience for our products and work with Marketing to support product specific content.
  • Present at Invesco roundtables and industry conferences.
  • Build strong relationships with the Distribution teams supporting their efforts to execute and effective sales strategy.
  • Support Distribution with training, large conferences, meeting support.
  • Assist Sales with custom proposals for significant business opportunities and participate in client calls/meetings as needed.
  • Liaise with the firm’s various marketing and RFP teams to ensure all data and responses regarding a given product are accurate and appropriate.
  • Assist with quarterly investment commentaries and regulatory commentary content.

Requirements of the Role:

  • Undergraduate degree required, preferably in business or related discipline.
  • 5-8 years of investment-related client-facing experience.
  • Proven success engaging and building relationships with wealth management advisors, gatekeepers, and investment due diligence teams.
  • Experience working with advisors across all channels including wirehouses, RIAs, and independent advisory firms strongly preferred.
  • Entrepreneurial spirit. The Custom Equity team is building upon a newer capability and growing quickly, so a successful candidate must have a desire for team success that allows her or him to identify, own, and solve problems.
  • Technical skills, including strong understanding of quantitative investment processes, understanding of financial markets and economics, proficiency with Bloomberg, Microsoft Office, and other software products.
  • Highly developed communication skills, both written and verbal, including the ability to simplify complex topics into understandable and compelling messages.
  • Strong and influence management skills
  • MBA/CFA a plus
  • Series 7 and 63 or 66 required
  • Regular travel required
The salary range for this position in New York City and Seattle is $150,000 - $180,000 / year. The total compensation offered for this position includes salary and incentive pay and will vary based on skills, experience and location.

Full Time / Part Time

Full time

Worker Type

Employee

Job Exempt (Yes / No)

Yes

Workplace Model

Pursuant to Invesco’s Workplace Policy, employees are expected to comply with the firm’s most current workplace model, which as of October 1, 2025, includes spending at least four full days each week working in an Invesco office. This reflects our belief that spending time together in the office helps us build stronger relationships, collaborate more easily, and support each other’s growth and development.

Job Description

Summary:

The Associate will work across multiple disciplines, including Acquisitions and Asset Management, working closely with experienced investment professionals and receive on-the-job training in each of these fields. 

The Associate will provide support in financial modelling and investment analysis of existing assets in the portfolio, and real estate investment opportunities identified by the Australian Real Estate Team. This role will also assist in the preparation of investment recommendations, investment committee papers, asset business plans, and property and fund reporting.

Depending on the individual’s performance, the Associate may be offered a permanent position at the end or during the 22-month program, if they have displayed the required competencies and delivered high quality output. Performance will be assessed throughout the program.

Primary duties and responsibilities:

  • Assisting the Acquisitions team with deal screening and underwriting of investment opportunities across APAC, including cash flow modeling, return sensitivities, verifying assumptions and market research

  • Participate in preparation of Investment Committee materials

  • Assist in due diligence and research market conditions including location assessment, rent comparables, sales comparables and replacement cost analysis

  • Assisting the Asset Management team in day-to-day active management including capex planning, sell-hold analysis, lease approval memos, refinancing scenarios, preparing annual business plans, coordinating quarterly valuations, updating asset performance and regular reporting

  • Review, analyse and comment on investment recommendations made by other members of the team

  • Other duties and projects as assigned by the Line Manager or other key members of the IRE team.

Formal education:

  • Educated to at least degree level, ideally in property, engineering/science, finance, economics or business studies.


Work experience/knowledge/competencies:

  • 1 - 2 years’ professional experience in finance/investment banking, property brokerage, auditing, consulting, or related fields

  • Proficient in Excel and basic financial modelling

  • Familiarity with real estate investment fundamentals, would be advantageous

  • Superior quantitative analytical and numerical ability

  • Excellent problem-solving skills

  • Strong interpersonal and communication skills (oral and written).


Abilities/personal attributes:

  • Highly numerate

  • Strong time management ability, able to prioritize and manage multiple priorities

  • Ability and willingness to work across multiple disciplines

  • Flexible, adaptable, can-do and proactive attitude

  • Highly motivated with a professional manner

  • Committed to high standards with a focus on attention to detail.

jobs.title: Associate Credit Analyst
jobs.description:
About Invesco

As one of the world’s leading independent global investment firms, Invesco is dedicated to rethinking possibilities for our clients. By delivering the combined power of our distinctive investment management capabilities, we provide a wide range of investment strategies and vehicles to our clients around the world. If you're looking for challenging work, intelligent colleagues, and exposure across a global footprint, come explore your potential at Invesco.

What’s in it for you?

Our people are at the very core of our success. Invesco employees get more out of life through our comprehensive compensation and benefit offerings including:

  • Flexible paid time off

  • Hybrid work schedule 

  • 401(K) matching of 100% up to the first 6% with a discretionary supplemental contribution 

  • Health & wellbeing benefits 

  • Parental Leave benefits 

  • Employee stock purchase plan

Job Description

The Associate Credit Research Analyst will perform fundamental credit research within multiple sectors of the US investment grade corporate bond market, with a specific focus on utilities and industrials sectors.  The ideal candidate will be deeply interested in markets and investing, intellectually curious, performance-oriented, collaborative, commercial, competitive, and highly driven.

The analyst is expected to become the sector expert, balancing in-depth knowledge of an expansive coverage universe with multiple other responsibilities supporting the broader success of the Invesco Fixed Income platform. Key responsibilities include generating well-supported investment recommendations, conducting in-depth fundamental investment analysis, developing fundamental credit opinions, using credit valuation analysis and sector knowledge to inform relative value views, assigning allocation ratings to each credit in the analyst’s coverage universe, tracking and communicating corporate updates across the analyst’s coverage universe and maintaining a constant dialogue with portfolio managers and investment colleagues across the Invesco Fixed Income platform. The analyst will also be responsible for monitoring broader market and sector risks and opportunities and proactively communicating these to the fixed income platform. This role requires an individual with a deep passion for markets and investing, a positive attitude, a team-focused & commercial mentality, extremely high standards of personal excellence & integrity and keen attention to detail. The analyst is expected to actively maintain and communicate proprietary investment ratings, sector views, investment recommendations and other risk assessments.  The analyst will be expected to identify, assume and manage credit risks and work effectively with analyst team members, traders, portfolio managers, client relations teams, other internal colleagues as well as external partners.  The analyst will be expected to contribute to the broader success of the Invesco Fixed Income platform, effectively participate in sector team presentations and be an active participant in client relations and marketing efforts.

Over time, this role offers the opportunity to grow into a trusted sector specialist whose views meaningfully impact portfolio positioning, performance and client outcomes across the US investment grade platform.

Key Responsibilities / Duties:

  • Develop and articulate defensible investment recommendations, fundamental credit ratings and allocation ratings for each issuer in the analyst’s coverage universe

  • Conduct deep, fundamentally driven investment research on companies within the utility and industrial sectors

  • Develop and communicate issuer level, sector level and broader market relative value assessments

  • Produce well researched, concise investment reports for global investment professionals

  • Be the subject matter expert on relevant regulatory developments that may affect coverage universe

  • Recommend changes in holdings of assigned credits and changes in investment recommendations, both at the individual credit and sector levels

  • Proactively identify emerging risks, opportunities and mispricing's within coverage universe and surface insights to portfolio managers

  • Communicate effectively with investment teams and investment team partners

  • Contribute to sector team presentations and other platform-wide events

  • Make due diligence visits and attend industry conferences as appropriate

  • Ensure all activities are consistent with Invesco’s conduct principles and assigned training is completed on a timely basis

  • Participate in client relations and marketing efforts

Skills / Other Personal Attributes Required:

  • Deep passion for financial markets and investing

  • Financial & investment analysis skills including financial statement analysis and credit valuation analysis

  • Decision making skills and high self-belief / conviction in investment recommendations

  • Growth mindset - demonstrated intellectual curiosity, desire to learn and commitment to continuous improvement as an investor

  • Effective communication, written & interpersonal skills

  • Collaborative, commercial and team-focused mentality

  • Extremely high personal standards and integrity

  • Motivated, competitive self-starter with ability to work independently

Formal Education:

  • Bachelor’s Degree

Work experience:

  • 2 – 5 years of buyside or sellside experience or experience in another finance and investing-related capacity

License / Registration / Certification:

  • CFA or progress towards CFA preferred

Employee

Yes

This reflects our belief that spending time together in the office helps us build stronger relationships, collaborate more easily, and support each other’s growth and development.

jobs.title: Senior Director, Credit Asset Management (Los Angeles Office)
jobs.description:
About Invesco

As one of the world’s leading independent global investment firms, Invesco is dedicated to rethinking possibilities for our clients. By delivering the combined power of our distinctive investment management capabilities, we provide a wide range of investment strategies and vehicles to our clients around the world. If you're looking for challenging work, intelligent colleagues, and exposure across a global footprint, come explore your potential at Invesco.

What’s in it for you?

Our people are at the very core of our success. Invesco employees get more out of life through our comprehensive compensation and benefit offerings including:

  • Flexible paid time off

  • Hybrid work schedule 

  • 401(K) matching of 100% up to the first 6% with a discretionary supplemental contribution 

  • Health & wellbeing benefits 

  • Parental Leave benefits 

  • Employee stock purchase plan

Job Description

The Senior Director, Credit Asset Management will be responsible for multiple diverse portfolios of commercial real estate loans originated and held by Invesco’s growing commercial real estate lending business line. The role will include oversight of third-party asset managers and leadership of loan modifications, complex workouts and resolutions.

Key Responsibilities / Duties:

  • Manage third party asset management team comprising 15 dedicated employees of Bellwether Asset Management.  Establish standards, responsibilities and expectations.  Monitor team performance and oversee work product to ensure consistency with Invesco standards.

  • Train third party asset management team and ensure accountability to Invesco standards.

  • Actively evaluate performance of individual asset managers and recommend hires, promotions, performance improvement plans, and terminations.

  • Ensure completion of third party scope of work within specified deadlines.  Evaluate additional scope of work as necessary. Negotiate third party contracts.

  • Lead communication and delegation of responsibilities between Invesco in-house credit team and third party asset management team.

  • Proactively monitor portfolio level performance, with a focus on Watchlist, sub-performing and non-performing loans.

  • Review third party work product and assign ad hoc tasks.

  • Oversee all loan asset management matters including payoffs, prepayments, lease reviews/approvals, SNDAs, reserves, escrows, draw requests, cash management, easements, financial reporting, post-closing items, waiver, modifications, valuation process and borrower requests.

  •   Lead the execution of loss-minimizing recovery strategies such as note sales, workouts, restructures, and/or foreclosure.

  • Collaborate with internal loan originators and accounting team, as well as third-party servicer(s), capital markets team, lenders and legal counsel to drive optimal outcomes for Watchlist, sub-performing and non-performing loans.

  • Lead risk rating process, identify problem / watchlist loans, develop monitoring plans, and recommendations.

  • Lead monthly and quarterly loan valuations processes by managing external and internal valuations professionals and external loan asset managers with valuation oversight responsibilities.  Proactively communicate with Portfolio Management team.

  • Identify and implement process efficiencies, best practices and/or standard operating procedures.

Work Experience / Knowledge:

  • 15+ years of commercial loan asset management or underwriting experience preferred.

  • Construction loan, mortgage workout and senior/sub (ie mezzanine) experience is strongly preferred.

  • Strong management skills with attention to detail and ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously.

  • Ability to manage and collaborate with multiple external groups (third party asset manager, third party servicer) and internal groups (portfolio management, valuations, underwriting, origination)

  • Demonstrated expertise in workouts and special situations.

  • Highly proficient user of Microsoft Excel, PowerPoint, and Word.

  • Proficient quantitative analysis, research, and problem-solving capabilities.

Skills / Other Personal Attributes Required:

  • Self-motivated and team oriented, with strong time management skills to prioritize deadlines.

  • Collaborative approach.

  • Excellent analytical, verbal and written communication skills. Ability to distill concepts and present results.

  • Confident individual ability to provide checks and balances within internal and external groups

  • Strong written and verbal communication skills.

Formal Education:

  • Bachelor’s degree in finance or related field.

  • MBA strongly preferred.

Working Conditions:

  • 5 days in-office in Century City, California.

  • 25% travel

#MTIVZ

The total compensation offered for this position, which includes salary and incentive pay, will vary based on skills, experience and location. The salary range for this position in Los Angeles is $180,000 to $250,000 annually.

Yes

jobs.description:
About Invesco

As one of the world’s leading independent global investment firms, Invesco is dedicated to rethinking possibilities for ourclients. By delivering the combined power of our distinctive investment management capabilities, we provide a wide range of investment strategies and vehicles to our clients around the world. If you're looking for challenging work, intelligent colleagues, and exposure across a global footprint, come explore your potential at Invesco.

What's in it for you?

Our people are at the very core of our success. Invesco employees get more out of life through our comprehensive compensation and benefit offerings including: 

  • Generous annual leave plus flexible paid time off

  • Hybrid work schedule 

  • Learning and development programs

  • Health & wellbeing benefits 

  • Parental Leave benefits 

  • Employee stock purchase plan (if applicable in your region)

Job Description

About the Role:

The AVP, Capital Markets will be responsible for supporting and expanding client‑facing capital markets engagement for Taiwan‑focused strategies, working closely with investment, trading, product, and distribution teams. This role will serve as a subject‑matter expert on Taiwan capital markets, acting as a key interface between investors, internal stakeholders, and market participants.

The specialist will support investor education, market insights, execution guidance, and product positioning, with a strong focus on local market structure, regulation, liquidity, and trading dynamics. The role will also contribute to broader APAC capital markets initiatives and data‑driven decision‑making.

Key Responsibilities

  • Lead and support client and distributor engagement related to Taiwan capital markets, including market structure, liquidity, execution considerations, and regulatory developments.
  • Act as a trusted specialist for investors, articulating investment philosophy, process, performance, and market context for Taiwan‑focused products.
  • Provide market updates, capital markets insights, and execution guidance to internal stakeholders and external clients.
  • Partner closely with Portfolio Management, Trading, Operations, Legal/Compliance, Product, and Technology teams to support capital markets initiatives.
  • Produce quantitative analysis, market research, and trading analytics to support client discussions and internal decision‑making.
  • Collect, analyze, and interpret trading, flow, and liquidity data to identify trends, risks, and opportunities in the Taiwan market.
  • Contribute to capital markets strategy reporting, including development of tools, dashboards, and visualization to enhance understanding of investor behavior and market dynamics.
  • Support pre‑trade and post‑trade analytics, execution analysis, and reporting aligned with best‑execution objectives.
  • Write Taiwan capital markets insights, commentary, and research for internal and external audiences.
  • Collaborate with product development and research teams on new product launches and enhancements, ensuring alignment with local market requirements.

Experience & Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree or equivalent with 5+ years of relevant experience in capital markets, trading, or investment roles with a strong focus on Taiwan markets.
  • Deep understanding of Taiwan capital markets, including market structure, trading mechanics, liquidity drivers, and investor behavior.
  • Strong knowledge of multi‑asset classes, including equities, fixed income, derivatives (futures, options, swaps), and FX.
  • Solid understanding of regulatory frameworks relevant to Taiwan (e.g., FSC, TWSE, TPEx) and ability to stay current on regulatory developments.
  • Strong quantitative and analytical skills, with experience producing market, trading, and performance analytics.
  • Proficiency in Bloomberg and market data tools.
  • Working knowledge of programming or data tools (e.g., Python, VBA, SQL) and experience with data visualization or automation is highly desirable.
  • Strong communication and relationship‑building skills, with the ability to explain complex investment and market concepts clearly to diverse audiences.
  • Comfortable operating in a fast‑paced, dynamic environment, managing multiple initiatives simultaneously.
  • Self‑directed, proactive, and able to independently drive initiatives from concept to execution.
  • High learning agility and strong problem‑solving skills, with a mindset focused on continuous improvement and business impact.

About the Role :

Meets with clients, prospects, consultants and/or intermediaries to explain the investment philosophy, process and performance of specific products. Provides updates on product performance and market trends. Works closely with investment and product development staff on product enhancements.

Meets clients, prospects, consultants and/or intermediaries to explain the investment philosophy, process and performance on alternative products (e.g., private equity, venture capital, hedge funds, real estate). Reports to the Investment function.

The general role of the Junior CPM (senior associate) is to support CPM team covering Korea market, ensuring the timely and accurate execution of general business activities. jobs.title: Associate, Private Equity Advisory
jobs.description:
About Kingfish Group
Kingfish is a different kind of private equity firm. We have pioneered a new approach to private equity investing that places us at the intersection of people, ideas and capital. At the core of our efforts are deep, systematic relationships with proven operating executives. Our firm comprises two core business segments; our private equity investment business and an advisory business that supports a limited set of high-performing PE sponsors as they identify, invest in and grow businesses. Our philosophy is to find the best people and give them the freedom to learn, innovate and outperform. In that spirit, we are structured to ensure that each individual contributes substantially to firm strategy and goals, and there is extensive feedback on performance. We pride ourselves on assembling high-performing teams to pursue inspiring goals.
Position Summary
Our Associates work closely with our private equity firm partners, operating as an extension of their deal teams to evaluate investment opportunities and advance portfolio company initiatives. Specifically, Associates are responsible for (a) Building relationships with industry executives and aligning them to support the team throughout the investment process (b) Synthesizing information and translating it into key insights that support investment decision making, (c) Managing projects to deliver high impact insights and relationships that provide an advantage to the team (d) Leading company-wide initiatives that improve how we do our work and make us a stronger team.
Responsibilities
● Build relationships with industry executives and align them to support the team throughout the investment process
  ○ Guide our research team as they identify industry executives and other primary information relevant for a particular investment opportunity
  ○ Lead calls with industry executives, serving as the primary point of contact and ambassador for the firm
●  Synthesize information and translate into key insights that support investment decision making
  ○  Analyze industries, markets and competitive landscapes to better understand investment opportunities
  ○  Perform primary diligence as needed alongside executive advisors throughout the deal process
  ○  Identify key insights from information and recommendations gathered from conversations with industry executives and research
●   Manage projects to deliver high impact insights and relationships, providing an advantage to the team
  ○  Manage project plans and deal timelines, working closely with senior leaders at our private equity partners to ensure our work is focused on the highest priorities and maximum impact
  ○  Create and deliver high quality presentations and memoranda for our Partners as well as Kingfish leadership (e.g. primary diligence deliverables, executive spec documentation, and Kingfish Investment Committee discussions)
●   Lead company-wide initiatives that improve how we do our work and make us a stronger team.
Qualifications
● Bachelor’s degree with 2-3 years of experience within consulting or financial services
● Relationship Building: High EQ with advanced written/ verbal communication skills, executive presence and an ability to build relationships with stakeholders at all levels
● Intellectual Curiosity & Creativity: thoughtful in asking questions and pursuing answers, excited to learn
● Critical Thinking: breaks down complex problems and solves them in creative ways
● Communication & Information Synthesis: effectively identifies key insights and presents insights
● Project Management: ability to manage multiple concurrent projects with tight timelines
● Proactive & Collaborative: team player that takes initiative to improve the team and the firm

jobs.title: Associate, Investor Relations, Private Credit
jobs.description:
Our Company

Oaktree is a leader among global investment managers specializing in alternative investments, with more than $220 billion in assets under management. The firm emphasizes an opportunistic, value-oriented, and risk-controlled approach to investments in credit, equity, and real estate. The firm has more than 1,400 employees and offices in more than 25 cities worldwide.

We are committed to cultivating an environment that is collaborative, curious, inclusive and honors diversity of thought. Providing training and career development opportunities and emphasizing strong support for our local communities through philanthropic initiatives are essential to our culture.

Investor Relations is a part of Oaktree’s investment teams with responsibility for growing and deepening relationships with the clients and prospects investing in our respective strategies. Investor Relations professionals serve as representatives and subject matter experts for Oaktree’s strategies and products, partnering closely with our sales and fundraise management teams to drive growth and deepen client engagement.

For additional information, please visit Oaktree’s website at http://www.oaktreecapital.com/

Responsibilities

This role will be aligned to Oaktree’s corporate direct lending and multi-strategy private credit strategies, supporting the execution of investor communication and materials for the relevant strategies.  This individual will work closely with other investor relations team members, investment professionals, our Global Client Group, and other internal stakeholders to deliver high-quality investor materials, support client engagement efforts, and ensure timely and accurate responses to investor inquiries.  The role offers the opportunity to develop deep knowledge of leveraged finance and private credit strategies within a collaborative, investment-focused environment.

Primary responsibilities would include:

Investor Communications

  • Support preparation for client meetings, including identifying materials to use, ensuring data accuracy and consistency across materials, and coordinating inputs across teams
  • Prepare responses to regular and ad-hoc investor inquiries relating to existing portfolios’ investments and overall positioning
  • Help coordinate materials for periodic client engagement such as LPAC meetings or co-investment opportunities
  • Contribute to fund brochures, talking points on salient topics, and other marketing and client-facing materials

Project Management & Execution

  • Contribute to a high-performing investor relations team by maintaining strong attention to detail and execution quality
  • Track deliverables and timelines to ensure timely reply to clients and timely completion of materials
  • Identify required information and coordinate across investment team, reporting teams, Global Client Group and other stakeholders to collate required information and present it to investors
  • Take ownership of the quality of the end-product, even when multiple teams have contributed
  • Demonstrate a proactive approach to problem-solving

Insight Development

  • Develop a strong understanding of portfolio holdings, strategy positioning, and market dynamics
  • Support the team in synthesizing investment insights into clear, client-ready materials
  • Contribute to written commentary regarding the portfolios, building the foundation to participate more actively in client discussions over time
  • Develop sound judgment with respect to sensitive, client-facing matters


Qualifications

  • 2-4 years of relevant experience in a client-facing role in institutional asset management 
  • Strong interest in markets and leveraged finance, with an ability to communicate effectively (written and verbal)
  • Ability to exercise good judgment regarding investor requests
  • Ability to interact with clients, investment teams and investors
  • Strong project management and teamwork skills
  • FINRA licenses (Series 7 and 63) or the ability to obtain them within a compressed timeframe
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (PowerPoint, Excel, Word), with the ability to create both client-facing and internal deliverables

Education

  • Bachelor’s degree (major in Finance, Business, Marketing, Communications, or related field preferred).
  • MBA, CFA or other recognized advanced credentials is a plus.

Base Salary Range

$125,000 - $155,000

In addition to a competitive base salary, you will be eligible to receive discretionary bonus incentives, a comprehensive benefits package and a flexible work arrangement.  The base salary offered will be commensurate with experience and/or qualifications, industry knowledge and expertise, as well as prior training and education.

Equal Opportunity Employment Policy

Oaktree is committed to diversity and to equal opportunity employment. Oaktree does not make employment decisions on the basis of race, creed, color, ethnicity, national origin, citizenship, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, past or present physical or mental disability, HIV status, medical condition as defined by state law (genetic characteristics or cancer), pregnancy, childbirth and related medical conditions, veteran status, military service, marital status, familial status, genetic information, domestic violence victim status or any other classification protected by applicable federal, state and local laws and ordinances. This policy applies to hiring, placement, internal promotions, training, opportunities for advancement, recruitment advertising, transfers, demotions, layoffs, terminations, recruitment advertising, rates of pay and other forms of compensation and all other terms, conditions and privileges of employment. This policy applies to all Oaktree applicants, employees, clients, and contractors. Staff members wishing to report violations or suspected violations of this policy should contact the head of their department or Human Resources.

For positions based in Los Angeles

For those applying for a position in the city of Los Angeles, the firm will consider for employment qualified applicants with a criminal history in a manner consistent with applicable federal, state and local law.

jobs.title: Investment Analyst, Infrastructure & Natural Resources (Metals & Mining)
jobs.description:
The opportunity

As an Investment Analyst in the Natural Resources (“NR”) team within the Infrastructure and Natural Resources (“INR”) group, you will have a key role in analyzing potential private investment opportunities, executing transactions and working with existing portfolio companies to craft strategy and implement value-enhancing initiatives.

If you are an excellent teammate, highly motivated, while working on multiple projects in a fast-paced transaction-based environment – this is the job for you.

Who You’ll Work With

You will be joining our Toronto office, where you will work in a dynamic team-based environment, directly supporting senior team members in metals and mining, and other natural resources, across geographic regions. You will have the chance to define and differentiate yourself as an investor and build strategic relationships within the global market.

INR consists of a team of ~65 highly-specialized private equity investment professionals, with offices in Toronto, London, Singapore and Mumbai, and an additional presence in Santiago, Chile. INR leads a geographically diverse portfolio with approximately C$47 billion of net assets in North America, South America, Europe, the Middle East, Asia, Australia and New Zealand.

The NR group is a specialized private investments group with approximately C$12 billion of assets as of December 31, 2025. NR owns and manages investments in mining, energy, timberland, agriculture and natural climate solution sectors, located in North America, Latin America, Europe, Australia and New Zealand. The NR portfolio contributes real returns, income, inflation protection and portfolio diversification to the overall investment portfolio of Ontario Teachers’.

What You’ll Do

  • Build and employ financial modeling tools to analyze investment opportunities;
  • Assist in the due diligence process and leading the valuation workstream and incorporate the findings into a financial model;
  • Identify key value drivers and financial risks and perform related financial sensitivity and scenario analysis;
  • Work closely with external advisors, consultants and partners to assess risks and submit bids under tight timelines;
  • Prepare presentations, reports, and investment recommendations to senior management and, where applicable, the Board of OTPP;
  • Review and analyze financial reports, budgets, board books and other information prepared by management of our portfolio companies;
  • Work with portfolio company management to build and maintain financial models that are used to analyze strategic issues and value the company;
  • Support internal strategic project initiatives, and the preparation of internal reporting materials including annual valuations across the team’s portfolio of assets

What You’ll Need

  • Minimum 1 year of relevant work experience, preferably in investment banking, principal investing, or mergers & acquisitions advisory
  • Direct experience with Natural Resource linked industries (especially metals & mining) and a clear understanding of commodity fundamentals
  • Bachelor’s degree required
  • Strong financial modelling and valuation skills
  • Strong communication skills with the ability to influence others and comfortably presenting to senior audiences
  • Excellent analytical and quantitative skills and ability to perform in-depth, creative, critical analysis
  • Ability and desire to work in a vigorous, fast-paced environment, and balance multiple priorities under demanding timelines, while remaining detail oriented
  • A proactive attitude and drive to deliver value in a team of highly self-motivated investment professionals
  • Advanced skills in Excel and PowerPoint
  • A willingness and ability to travel

What we’re offering

Pay-for-performance with base salary and annual incentive based on individual and enterprise performance. The Base salary for this level is $115,000 in Year 1 and $125,000 in Year 2. Individual compensation and placement of this role in the range is based on different factors unique to each candidate, including but not limited to relevant experience, skills, demonstrated competencies, and internal equity as they relate to the role.

  • Numerous opportunities for professional growth and development

  • Comprehensive employer paid benefits coverage

  • Retirement income through a defined benefit pension plan

  • The opportunity to invest back into the fund through our Deferred Incentive Program

  • A flexible work environment combining in office collaboration and remote working

  • Competitive time off

  • Our Flexible Travel Program gives you the option to work abroad in another region/country for up to a month each year

  • Employee discount programs including Edvantage and Perkopolis

At Ontario Teachers', diversity is one of our core strengths. We take pride in ensuring that the people we hire and the culture we create, reflect and embrace diversity of thought, background and experience. Through our Diversity, Equity and Inclusion strategy and our Employee Resource Groups (ERGs), we celebrate diversity and foster inclusion through events for colleagues to connect for professional development, networking & mentoring.  We are building an inclusive and equitable workplace where our talent is respected, accepted and empowered to be themselves.  To learn more about our commitment to Diversity, Equity and Inclusion, check out Life at Teachers'.

How to apply

Are you ready to pursue new challenges and take your career to the next level? Apply today! You may be invited to complete a pre-recorded digital interview as part of your application.

Accommodations are available upon request (peopleandculture@otpp.com) for candidates with a disability taking part in the recruitment process and once hired.

Candidates must be legally entitled to work in the country where this role is located.

Ontario Teachers’ may use AI-based tools to assist in screening and assessing applicants for this position. These tools may help us identify candidates whose skills and experience align with Ontario Teachers’ objectives by analyzing information provided in resumes and applications. Our use of AI does not replace human decision-making.

To learn more about how Teachers’ uses AI with your personal information, please visit our Privacy Centre.

jobs.title: Principal, INR - Natural Resources (Metals & Mining)
jobs.description:
The Opportunity

Exciting opportunity to join our INR Team! This metals & mining focused role is ideal for a motivated team player who enjoy a transaction-oriented environment, can manage multiple projects and thrive on developing new skills. You have a strong understanding of valuation theory, excellent strategic thinking skills and an ability to draw conclusions following in-depth analysis.  You will leverage your strong interpersonal, communication and presentation skills to articulate ideas, issues and recommendations internally, as well as with external parties such as investment partners, senior executives of companies, investment bankers, lawyers, and advisors. Further, the opportunity will include material asset management responsibilities, requiring frequent engagement with senior management and board members of the current portfolio companies.

Who you’ll work with

Based in Toronto, the Natural Resources group is a part of the larger Infrastructure & Natural Resources department.

The Natural Resources (“NR”) group is a specialized private equity group with c.$12 billion of assets as of December 31, 2025. NR owns and manages investments in mining, energy, timberland, agriculture and natural climate solution sectors, located in North America, Latin America, Europe, Australia and New Zealand. The NR portfolio contributes real returns, income, inflation protection and portfolio diversification to the overall investment portfolio of Ontario Teachers’.

What you'll do

Supporting the Metals & Mining team within Natural Resources, you will work as a team member in the evaluation and execution of transactions, as well as the management of existing portfolio investments.

As a member of a transaction execution team, you will:

  • Work closely with internal deal teams and external advisors to conduct due diligence on investment opportunities;

  • Manage or support various workstreams including technical, commercial, financial, tax, legal, ESG, etc.

  • Oversee the development of sophisticated financial models to analyze investment opportunities;

  • Oversee valuation analysis including identifying key value drivers and financial risks;

  • Conduct industry analysis including researching emerging investment opportunities

  • Support the team in business development and building relationships with industry participants

  • Manage the drafting of presentations, reports and investment recommendations to senior management

In managing existing portfolio investments, you will:

  • Work closely with portfolio company management to shape strategy and implement value-enhancing initiatives, including capital projects, refinancing’s and bolt-on acquisitions

  • Review and analyze board materials, business plans, and other information prepared by management of our portfolio companies;

  • Direct the development and maintenance of financial models for our existing investments, and

  • Prepare internal annual valuations in coordination with internal legal, taxation and finance functions

What you’ll need

  • 5 plus years of work experience, preferably in principal investing, investment banking or in a corporate development group of a corporate active in a relevant industry; 

  • Direct experience in metals & mining and a clear understanding of commodity fundamentals;

  • Experience leading material aspects of the investment process, including financial modelling, conducting due diligence and participating financing;

  • Proven history of working collaboratively as part of a team to meet tight timelines within a project based work environment;

  • Strong interpersonal skills, communication and presentation skills

  • Excellent analytical and quantitative skills and ability to perform in-depth, innovative, critical analysis, and

  • A willingness and ability to travel frequently.

What we’re offering

  • Numerous opportunities for professional growth and development

  • Comprehensive employer paid benefits coverage

  • Retirement income through a defined benefit pension plan

  • The opportunity to invest back into the fund through our Deferred Incentive Program

  • A flexible work environment combining in office collaboration and remote working

  • Competitive time off

jobs.title: Investment Associate, Private Capital
jobs.description:
Our Company  

Ontario Teachers' Pension Plan Board (Ontario Teachers') is the administrator of Canada's largest single-profession pension plan, with CAD $270 billion in net assets as of June 30, 2025. We hold a diverse global portfolio of assets, approximately 80% of which is managed in-house. Ontario Teachers’ invests and administers the pensions of the province of Ontario’s 343,000 active and retired teachers. Ontario Teachers’ is an independent organization headquartered in Toronto and our other investment offices are in London, Singapore, New York, Sao Paolo, Dallas, San Francisco and India. We employ more than 1,500 employees globally.   

OTPP manages ~$38B of assets out of the London office, of which ~C$8B is in the Private Capital portfolio. OTPP has been an investor in Europe since 1992, with the London office launched in 2007 to gain enhanced access to opportunities in EMEA.

Private Capital is the investment department within OTPP focused on private equity investing, with global Assets Under Management of $61B. We invest across three core sectors (Financial Services, Technology, and Services) and focus on control and co-control investments. Our European portfolio includes companies such as Busy Bees, 7IM, Donte Group, Max Matthiessen, Group.One and Veonet.

The Opportunity 

We are seeking an Associate for our Private Capital (“PC”) team. Based in London, you will be an integral part of the team focused on direct investments in Europe. You will play a key role in evaluating potential investment opportunities, executing transactions and handling existing portfolio investments that make up our European direct private equity investment strategy. 

What you'll do 

Your time will be spent evaluating new investments and determining whether the company represents an attractive investment opportunity. Among other things, the analysis of investments includes conducting industry and company research, managing third-party advisors, building sophisticated financial models, reviewing and analysing due diligence materials, and preparing internal support materials and written recommendations to PC’s Investment Committee. 

Once a transaction has been completed, you will remain involved with the business, potentially attending board meetings as an observer, and will work with the management team and the board of directors to implement value-enhancing initiatives. In addition, you will work directly with management on various initiatives as required, including with respect to mergers and acquisitions, debt refinancings, budgeting and other strategic projects. 

You get to play an active role in assisting in and identifying target investment sectors and generating proprietary deal flow in specific sectors. Activities will include conducting desk research, working with bankers and consultants, and attending conferences. 

What you'll need 

  • An undergraduate degree  

  • 2-3 years relevant work experience – such as in private equity, investment banking, or consulting 

  • Very strong financial modelling and valuation skills 

  • Robust analytical and quantitative skills and ability to perform in-depth, creative, critical analysis 

  • Deep understanding of fundamental investment analysis 

  • Ability to develop a good knowledge of the economic/business characteristics, conditions and trends of a broad range of industries 

  • Excellent communications skills, both oral and written, with solid presentation skills 

  • Can-do attitude, hunger to learn and grow, maturity and composure

  • Great teammate with strong interpersonal skills, able to establish and build relationships 

  • Self-motivated with the ability to meet tight timelines and operate in a highly complex and demanding work environment 

  • Unquestioned personal and professional integrity 

  • Fluency in English is essential. Other Western European languages considered an asset 

  • Ability and willingness to travel

  • Numerous opportunities for professional growth and development

jobs.title: Investment Associate, Private Capital - Financial Services, Services, or Technology (Multiple Opportunities)
jobs.description:
The opportunity

We are seeking 4 new Associates to join our Private Capital (“PC”) team across multiple sector pods. Based in Toronto, you will be an integral part of a team focused on direct investments in North America. You will play a key role in evaluating potential investment opportunities, executing transactions, conducting sector research including transaction pipeline building, and supporting portfolio company management.

Who you'll work with

You will work with a diverse team of professionals based in Toronto, focused on evaluating new opportunities and managing investments. In your role, you will engage with management teams of portfolio companies, fund partners, investment bankers, board directors, and advisors. You will be aligned to one of three sector pods:

Financial Services: The Financial Services team invests across sub-sectors including insurance, wealth & asset management, lending, professional services, and cards & payments, with a particular need to understand the regulatory, AI, data and digitization themes reshaping the sector. You will work with sector specialists and portfolio company management to evaluate new investments, support value creation initiatives, and develop sub-sector views across priority areas.

Services: A team focused on commercial & residential, industrial & infrastructure, and durable customer services. You will work alongside experienced deal professionals on platform and add-on acquisitions, engaging directly with management teams to drive operational improvements and growth.

Technology: A team focused on investments in durable software and technology-enabled services businesses. Sub-sectors of focus include vertical and horizontal software (including healthcare IT, financial services, Office of the CFO, supply chain, and GRC), cybersecurity software and services, managed service providers (MSPs), and digital transformation. You will work alongside experienced deal professionals on platform and add-on investments, engaging with management teams to support growth, operational improvement, and technology-enabled value creation.

What you'll do

  • You will evaluate new investments and determine whether the company represents an attractive investment opportunity.  The analysis of investments includes conducting industry and company research, managing third-party advisors, building sophisticated financial models, arranging debt financing, reviewing legal documents, reviewing and analyzing due diligence materials, and preparing internal support materials and written recommendations to PC’s Investment Committee.

  • Once a transaction has been completed, you will remain involved with the business, potentially attending board meetings as an observer, and will work with the management team and the board of directors to implement value-enhancing initiatives. In addition, you will work directly with management on various initiatives as required, including with respect to mergers and acquisitions, debt refinancing, budgeting and other strategic projects.

  • You will help develop and refresh sub-sector investment theses through deep dives, market mapping, primary research, target screening, valuation benchmarking, and identification of key diligence themes. Activities will include conducting desk research, working with bankers and consultants, and attending conferences. There is also the expectation that you assist in building and maintaining relationships with fund partners.

What you'll need

  • Minimum 2 years related work experience (such as private equity, investment banking, M&A advisory, management consulting, or corporate development)

  • An undergraduate or graduate degree from a university with a focus on business / finance / accounting / investment analysis, or equivalent experience

  • Experience in developing financial models (LBO, DCF, etc.) from the ground up to aid in investment valuation, transaction structuring, and financing decisions.

  • Experience evaluating companies, transactions, or investment themes in one or more of Financial Services, Services, or Technology is considered an asset.

  • Very strong analytical and quantitative skills and ability to perform in-depth, creative, critical analysis

  • Comfort using data and AI-enabled tools to support research, analysis, diligence and portfolio company work

  • Deep understanding of fundamental investment analysis

  • Experience in the investment process, including conducting due diligence, arranging debt financing and negotiating documentation

  • Ability to develop a good knowledge of the economic/business characteristics, conditions and trends of the target sector

  • Excellent communication skills, both oral and written, with solid presentation skills

  • Collaborative teammate with strong interpersonal skills, able to establish and build relationships

  • Self-motivated, and possess unquestioned personal and professional integrity

  • Ability to meet tight timelines and operate in a highly complex and demanding work environment

  • Ability and willingness to travel

What we’re offering

  • Numerous opportunities for professional growth and development

  • Comprehensive employer paid benefits coverage

  • Retirement income through a defined benefit pension plan

  • The opportunity to invest back into the fund through our Deferred Incentive Program

  • A flexible work environment combining in office collaboration and remote working

  • Competitive time off

  • Our Flexible Travel Program gives you the option to work abroad in another region/country for up to a month each year

  • Employee discount programs including Edvantage and Perkopolis

jobs.title: Investment Analyst, Teachers’ Venture Growth
jobs.description:
The opportunity

We are hiring a junior investment professional at the Investment Analyst level to join Teachers' Venture Growth (TVG), the venture and growth equity arm of Ontario Teachers' Pension Plan (OTPP). Based in our San Francisco office, you will support North American direct investments across origination, due diligence, deal execution, and portfolio monitoring.

OTPP manages US$200+ billion in net assets with a 9.2% annualized total-fund net return since inception in 1990, investing to deliver retirement income for 340,000 working members and pensioners. At Ontario Teachers', we don't just invest to make a return, we invest to shape a better future for the teachers we serve, the businesses we back, and the world we live in.

TVG is the division of OTPP focused on investing in high-growth technology companies. TVG's 30+ investors work from offices in San Francisco, Toronto, London, Singapore, and Mumbai, backing high-growth technology companies at the frontier of AI, enterprise software, climate/energy transition, and financial services. We focus on companies with established product-market fit, with initial commitments ranging from $50M–$250M of equity. Our portfolio includes SpaceX, Anthropic, Databricks, Canva, Gusto, and Epic Games — companies reshaping entire industries.

Who you’ll work with

You will report to a Director or Principal on TVG's North American direct investments team, based in our San Francisco office. You will work cross-functionally with investment professionals across TVG's global offices and partner closely with other teams across OTPP to ensure continued alignment with the organization's mission, vision, and values.

What you’ll do

  • Play a critical role across all stages of the investment process: origination, due diligence, deal execution, and portfolio monitoring

  • Develop investment theses and financial models including projections, sensitivity analysis, and valuations

  • Drive sector research to identify investment targets and perform competitive analysis

  • Prepare investment memos and present recommendations to internal committees

  • Build and maintain relationships with founders, investors, and service providers to support deal sourcing

  • Evaluate and monitor the ongoing portfolio company performance, and support ongoing strategic initiatives and reporting requirements

  • Represent TVG at industry conferences and annual meetings

What you’ll need

  • 1+ years of investment or deal experience (investment banking, private equity, VC, hedge fund, management consulting)

  • Bachelor's degree or equivalent experience

  • Strong financial modelling and quantitative analysis skills, with advanced proficiency in Excel and PowerPoint

  • Strong work ethic, team mindset, communication & presentation skills

  • Ability to work independently and thrive in a high-performance team environment

  • Genuine passion for disruptive technology and the startup ecosystem

  • Willingness to actively experiment with and adopt AI workflow tools as the technology and our practices evolve

The base salary range for this position ranges from USD $110,000 to $150,000.

This range represents the low and high end of Ontario Teachers’ salary range for this position and may be modified from time to time. Not all candidates will be eligible for the upper end of the salary range. The exact placement within the range will be determined by an assessment of the incumbent’s knowledge, skills and experience relative to the requirements of the role and to internal peers. In addition to base pay, this role is eligible to participate in Teachers' annual incentive plan.

What we’re offering

  • Numerous opportunities for professional growth and development

  • Comprehensive employer paid benefits coverage

  • A flexible work environment combining in office collaboration and remote working

  • Competitive time off

  • Our Flexible Travel Program gives you the option to work abroad in another region/country for up to a month each year

jobs.title: Managing Director, Financial Services, Private Capital
jobs.description:
The opportunity

Ontario Teachers' Pension Plan Board (Ontario Teachers') is a global investor with net assets of ~$279 billion as of December 31, 2025. Ontario Teachers’ is a fully funded defined benefit pension plan, and it invests in a broad array of asset classes to deliver retirement security for 346,000 working members and pensioners. Our Private Capital group deploys ~$60 billion across direct private equity investments and private equity funds globally.

The Managing Director will lead investments end-to-end — from origination through exit — sponsor transactions at Underwriting Committee, take active board roles at portfolio companies, and help build and develop the Financial Services investment team. This is a senior seat for a candidate with a proven private equity track record and deep Financial Services expertise.

Who you'll work with

Reporting to the Senior Managing Director, the role will be part of a deal team comprising analysts, associates, principals, directors, and managing directors, supported by third-party consultants, operating executives, and legal and accounting advisors dedicated to each transaction.

The Financial Services team invests across sub-sectors including Insurance, Wealth & Asset Management, Lending, Professional Services, and Cards & Payments, with particular need to understand the regulatory, AI, and digitization themes reshaping the sector. The role will also potentially partner with colleagues in OTPP's global offices on international investment and / or cross asset class opportunities in Financial Services.

What you'll do

  • Lead deal teams end-to-end, from origination through close and through the life of the investment; serve as the named sponsor at Underwriting Committee and author Underwriting Committee papers.

  • Own deal execution with limited oversight — including pricing, structuring, financing, and final commercial and legal negotiating positions.

  • Take active board leadership roles at portfolio companies; drive value creation agendas with management and sponsor follow-on capital, M&A, and exit decisions.

  • Originate proprietary deal flow through personal networks with CEOs, operating executives, sponsors, bankers, and advisors; represent OTPP Financial Services externally.

  • Set coverage priorities and investment theses for one or more sub-sectors; partner with the SMDs on team strategy and sector positioning.

  • Build, lead, and develop the Financial Services investment team; partner with the SMDs on hiring, performance management, and succession. Hold the team accountable for quality of judgment, execution discipline, and alignment with OTPP's mission, vision, and values.

  • Work in a highly collaborative manner, bringing a complex network of stakeholders along (Global and Sector SMDs, deal teams, Portfolio Solutions / Value Creation, international office leaders, portfolio companies). Strike the appropriate balance of being a confident decision-maker and an inclusive leader.

  • Define value creation playbooks across the Financial Services portfolio and hold portfolio company management accountable for delivery.

  • Exercise independent judgment on deal execution within approved mandates; serve as the named sponsor at Underwriting Committee and hold board-level decision authority at portfolio companies.

  • Provide disciplined thinking, sound judgment, and courage of conviction, with a willingness to take balanced, thesis-driven risk. This includes stepping in and intervening directly and effectively when required.

What you'll need

  • ~15+ years of meaningful work experience with a substantial track record of private equity deal leadership. Supplementary experience in management consulting, investment banking, and/or corporate M&A (preferably corporate development or similar) considered in combination with PE tenure.

  • Track record sponsoring deals at Underwriting / Investment Committee and delivering committed returns across a full investment cycle, including conducting and leading multiple diligence streams (financial, operational, legal, tax, insurance), arranging debt financing, and negotiating documentation.

  • Direct investment track record in Financial Services required; insurance, wealth, or payments expertise particularly valued.

  • Deep knowledge of Financial Services sector dynamics — particularly Insurance, Wealth & Asset Management, Lending, Professional Services, and Cards & Payments — and the emerging regulatory, AI, and digitization themes reshaping them.

  • Active “for profit” board director experience, including committee leadership.

  • Strong academic background, with a financial designation (MBA, CFA, CBV, or CA strongly preferred).

  • Demonstrated leadership across the full investment process, including sourcing, due diligence, debt financing, negotiation, and exit.

  • Strong negotiating skills, with ability to lead commercial terms in acquisitions, mergers, and financings.

  • Ability to balance priorities, work independently, lead teams, and thrive in a high-performance environment.

  • Very strong analytical and quantitative skills to perform and guide in-depth, innovative, critical analysis.

  • Deep understanding of fundamental investment analysis and private equity value creation.

  • Strong interpersonal skills and ability to build constructive relationships with internal and external parties, particularly management teams, boards, and senior advisors.

  • Strong communication skills, both oral and written, including IC paper authorship and presentation.

  • Willingness and ability to travel across North America and globally.

  • Numerous opportunities for professional growth and development

  • Comprehensive employer paid benefits coverage

  • Retirement income through a defined benefit pension plan

  • The opportunity to invest back into the fund through our Deferred Incentive Program
