jobs.title: Investment Director, Infrastructure & Natural Resources
jobs.description:
Company: Ontario Teachers’ Pension Plan Board

160 Front Street West, Suite 3200

Toronto, Ontario

M5J 0G4

Job Title: Investment Director, Infrastructure and Natural Resources – Latin America

NOC: 10021 – Banking, Credit, and Other Investment Managers

Wage: Starting at an annual base salary of $195,000-215,000 (37.5 hours per week) - permanent/full-time

Location(s) of work: 160 Front Street West, Suite 3200, Toronto, Ontario, M5J 0G4 Canada

Number of Positions: One (1)

Required Language Skills: English. Portuguese and/or Spanish an asset

The Opportunity

Infrastructure & Natural Resources (INR) is seeking an Investment Director to join our Americas team, with a coverage focus on Latin America. Based in Toronto, this role is particularly well-suited to highly driven professionals with significant direct equity investing and/or investment banking experience in infrastructure.

As an Investment Director, you will play a central role across the investment lifecycle -originating and executing new opportunities, while actively managing portfolio companies within the infrastructure sector. In this capacity, you will serve on boards and partner closely with management teams to shape strategy and drive value creation.

This role provides a platform to further deepen your investment expertise across Latin American markets and sectors, while continuing to build credibility and long-term relationships with management teams, partners, and industry participants.

Who you'll work with

Reporting to the Managing Director and Head of South America Infrastructure coverage, the Investment Director will be a key part of deal and asset management teams comprising investment analysts, investment associates, and investment principals, supported by third-party consultants, operating executives, and legal and accounting professionals dedicated to each particular portfolio company.

The broader INR team includes 70 investment professionals leading the execution of marquee infrastructure transactions and actively managing our portfolio companies. With offices in Toronto, London, Singapore, and Mumbai, INR manages a diverse portfolio of c. C$46.5 billion across the Americas, Europe, and Asia.

Key Responsibilities

The Investment Director will play an integral role in both the evaluation and execution of direct equity transactions, as well as in the ongoing management of existing portfolio companies. A key aspect of the role will also involve overseeing, providing guidance to, and mentoring junior team members.

Additionally, they will:

  • Actively manage INR portfolio companies, including serving on boards and partnering with management teams to shape strategy and drive value creation initiatives

  • Originate, evaluate, and pursue selective investment opportunities, with a particular focus on Latin America

  • Play a central role in all stages of equity investment execution, leading key workstreams, overseeing valuation and financial analysis, managing internal teams and external advisors, and making investment recommendations

  • Identify and diligence key business and investment transaction risks, and determine how best to mitigate them

  • Build and maintain relationships with management teams, directors, partners, lenders, advisors, and broader industry participants

  • Supervise and develop junior staff, providing clear direction and ongoing feedback

  • Represent OTPP at regional industry conferences and portfolio company meetings and events

Required Skills

  • 10+ years of professional experience in direct equity investing or investment banking, with a focus on infrastructure

  • 5+ years of investment coverage experience of Latin America is required

  • In-country experience in Brazil, Chile, and/or Mexico is an asset

  • Willingness and ability to travel frequently (20%+)

  • Strong ability to influence outcomes across a range of stakeholders, including management teams, boards, and partners

  • Sound commercial judgement and ability to form independent views and make decisions with imperfect information in a fast-paced environment

  • Strong financial acumen and quantitative skills, including experience overseeing valuation and financial analysis

  • Proven track record in leading the equity investment process, including due diligence, transaction execution, and debt financing

  • Strong project management skills and ability to manage multiple workstreams and teams effectively under tight timelines

·        Fluency in English required. Portuguese and/or Spanish is an asset

Required Education

·        Undergraduate Degree or Master’s Degree in Economics, Commerce, Finance or Business Administration or a related field.

  • Numerous opportunities for professional growth and development

jobs.title: Intern - Investments (Jul - Dec 2026)
jobs.description:
The deadline to apply for this role is:

Ontario Teachers' is Canada's largest single-profession pension plan with $269.6 billion in net assets. We pay pensions and invest plan assets on behalf of 343,000 working and retired teachers. Since our establishment as an independent organization in 1990, we have built an international reputation for innovation and leadership in investment management and member services. Teachers’ is an experienced global investor and a significant investor in the Asia Pacific region.

Ontario Teachers’ is headquartered in Toronto and has investment offices based in London, Singapore, India, and San Francisco. Ontario Teachers’ employs more than 1,500 employees globally.

The Opportunity

Are you curious to learn more about becoming an investment professional? Do you thrive in a fast-paced working environment? Do you want to join a vibrant team with a mandate to grow? If so, join the Ontario Teachers’ student program! We are looking for dedicated self-starters who share our passion for investing and want to learn how we execute on investment opportunities and manage our existing investments across the different asset-classes.

Infrastructure & Natural Resources

The Infrastructure & Natural Resources (“INR”) Group invests in global infrastructure and natural resources companies to deliver growing, inflation-linked cash flows over a long-term investment horizon. Our global team consists of over 70 investment professionals handle a geographically diverse CAD 40 billion global investment portfolio. Our investments are primarily through direct, privately held, controlling or co-controlling stakes, rather than through funds or passive holdings.

Private Capital

OTPP’s Private Capital (PC) team leads the Plan’s private equity activities. With over C$60 billion of AUM globally, Private Capital invests directly and through private equity funds in companies around the world seeking to grow and add value over the long run. Private Capital stands as one of the largest and most sophisticated pools of capital in the world. Through over 200+ direct and fund investments, Private Capital has generated a net internal rate of return of roughly 20% since inception.

 What you’ll do

  • You will be involved in assisting the team in analysing potential new investment opportunities, managing our existing investments and will gain the following hands-on experience and training:

  • Hone your financial modelling abilities by analysing investment opportunities

  • Learn to identify key value drivers and risks for investments and perform related sensitivity and scenario analyses

  • Support due diligence exercises

  • Prepare valuations, presentations, and reports to senior investment professionals

  • Develop and analyse business cases for portfolio company / asset value creation initiatives (debt refinancings, expansion projects, management compensation, on-going budgeting, etc.)

What you’ll need

  • Currently pursuing an undergraduate with a concentration in investments, finance, or accounting

  • Previous intern experience in M&A, investment banking, private equity, equity research, INR, or real estate.

  • Advanced skills in Microsoft Office (Excel, PowerPoint, Word) required

  • Familiarity with financial modelling – you are a number whiz! 

  • Excellent analytical and quantitative skills and ability to perform in-depth analysis

  • Strong written and verbal communication skills – you are a people person and love working in a dynamic team!

  • Ability to balance multiple priorities and initiatives while remaining detail-oriented

  • Currently enrolled in your 3rd / 4th year

  • You will be returning to your studies after the work term is completed

  • Availability for 6 months internship from Jul 2026 to Dec2026.

jobs.title: Managing Director, Teachers' Venture Growth - North America Direct Investing
jobs.description:
The Opportunity

Ontario Teachers’ Pension Plan (“Teachers’”) manages ~C$270B globally and is recognized as one of the most sophisticated direct investors in the world. With over 80% of assets managed internally, we combine long-term capital with disciplined underwriting and the ability to lead at scale. Teachers’ Venture Growth (TVG) is our dedicated late-stage venture and growth equity platform, managing ~C$15B globally. On the direct investment front, we deploy ~C$1B annually into minority investments in scaled private technology companies. TVG has invested in category leaders such as SpaceX, Databricks, Canva, Anthropic, Gusto, Grafana, Kong, StackAdapt, and many others.

We are seeking a Managing Director in San Francisco to join our North America Direct team. We are intentionally looking to add a Silicon Valley-based investor who brings differentiated access, cycle-tested judgment, and the ambition to help scale a high-performing institutional growth platform.

The Mandate

You will:

  • Own sector coverage within North American technology

  • Develop differentiated investment theses

  • Originate, lead, and close minority growth investments (typically Series C +)

  • Write and defend investment committee recommendations

  • Serve as an active board member or observer

  • Develop junior talent and elevate team performance

TVG’s average initial equity commitment is ~US$100M and we frequently lead or co-lead rounds. You will collaborate closely with senior TVG leadership, the TVG Funds team, and adjacent OTPP asset classes, but investment decisions and portfolio ownership resides within TVG.

What Success Looks Like

Within the first 12–24 months, you will have:

  • Expanded our access to high-quality companies and founders not already in our network

  • Led and closed compelling later-stage investments

  • Demonstrated strong board engagement and value-add through inflection points

  • Raised the bar for analytical rigor and decision quality across the team

  • Developed junior investors through direct coaching and meaningful ownership

We are building the next phase of our North America platform and this role is expected to contribute materially to that evolution.

The Profile

We are seeking an investor who brings:

  • 12+ years of direct investing experience in later-stage venture or growth equity

  • A demonstrated track record leading minority investments in scaled technology companies

  • Meaningful board experience and exposure through exit

  • Deep, credible relationships across Silicon Valley and the broader North American technology ecosystem

  • Strong transaction leadership and negotiation capabilities

We will not consider candidates whose experience is limited to early-stage investing or those without direct underwriting responsibility.

Equally important:

  • Experience investing through multiple market cycles with visible evidence of judgment shaped by volatility

  • High standards, intellectual independence, and accountability

  • A collaborative, strengths-based leadership style suited to a global institutional platform

We focus primarily on later-stage technology assets across a wide range of sectors, however, we are generally less active in consumer internet and health tech.

Why Teachers’

Teachers’ offers something distinctive in Silicon Valley:

  • Long-duration capital that allows us to invest through cycles

  • The ability to write large checks and lead

  • A disciplined, globally integrated investment culture

  • A truly global platform with deep institutional resources

  • A clear purpose: delivering retirement security to 340,000 teachers who rely on us for long-term financial stability in retirement

Our capital is not transient. It represents the deferred earnings of educators. That responsibility shapes how we underwrite risk, support companies, and build enduring value. This is an opportunity to combine institutional scale with entrepreneurial impact.

Compensation

Base salary range: USD $300,000–$380,000, plus eligibility for Teachers’ annual incentive plan and Long Term Incentive Plan compensation programs. Final compensation reflects experience, capability, and internal alignment.

What we’re offering

  • Numerous opportunities for professional growth and development

  • Comprehensive employer paid benefits coverage

  • A flexible work environment combining in office collaboration and remote working

  • Competitive time off

  • Our Flexible Travel Program gives you the option to work abroad in another region/country for up to a month each year

We take pride in ensuring that the people we hire and the culture we create, reflect and embrace diversity of thought, background and experience. Through our Diversity, Equity and Inclusion strategy and our Employee Resource Groups (ERGs), we celebrate diversity and foster inclusion through events for colleagues to connect for professional development, networking & mentoring.  We are building an inclusive and equitable workplace where our talent is respected, accepted and empowered to be themselves.  To learn more about our commitment to Diversity, Equity and Inclusion, check out Life at Teachers'.

jobs.title: Managing Director, Private Equity - Software
jobs.description:
The opportunity

The Managing Director will be working with investment professionals with deep sector and regional expertise and will play a key role in making and managing direct investments in the Technology sector.

Who you'll will work with 

Reporting to the Senior Managing Director (SMD), the Managing Director will lead deal teams comprising analysts, associates and principals, supported by third party consultants, operating executives, legal and accounting professionals dedicated to each particular investment.

The individual will have significant prior expertise and a strong track record investing in Technology, particularly in Software with knowledge of emerging AI and digitization themes.  Key focus areas include Application software, Infrastructure software, Digital & IT Services and other Tech-enabled categories.

In certain instances, the Managing Director may also work with colleagues in the London, Singapore or Mumbai offices in evaluating international investment opportunities in a given sector.

What you'll do

  • When evaluating new investments, the Managing Director will lead, with oversight of SMD, a small team determining whether the company represents an attractive investment opportunity. Among other things, the analysis of investments includes conducting industry and company due diligence, creating sophisticated financial models, arranging debt financing, negotiating legal documents, formulating value creation plans (in partnership with operating teams and other lead investors) and preparing written recommendations.

  • Once a transaction has been completed, the Managing Director will remain involved with the business, and will work with our investment partners and the management team to implement value-enhancing initiatives. The Managing Director may be expected to take lead roles at the board and/or sub-committees of the board in some cases. 

  • They will play an active role in origination, identifying target investments and creating a “right to win”, which should include unique value creation levers.  Activities will include conducting desk research, working with consultants, operating executives, attending conferences and trade shows, networking with management teams and meeting with various industry participants. This will require the individual to build high-impact relationships and networks across the Technology investing ecosystem.

  • They will lead sub-sector research and deep dives as the team evolves its coverage strategy and focus areas over time with shifting industry dynamics such as the emergence of AI impacting investment decisions.

  • Collaborate with other Global Sector teams and across Investments on specific departmental initiatives.

  • Seek to lead deals with input and high level of communication from global sector SMD.

What you'll need

  • 15+ years relevant work experience, ideally the most recent experience leading deals in Technology private equity.  Active Technology M&A in a lead role in corporate development would also be considered. Supplemented by previous experiences in management consulting, investment banking and/or corporations (preferably corporate development or similar roles).

  • Tenured private equity investor with a strong track record (technology/software experience strongly preferred). Please include deal sheets with your application.

  • Experience in playing a leading role in the investment process, including conducting due diligence, leading multiple diligence streams such as financial, operational, legal, tax, insurance as well as arranging debt financing and negotiating documentation.

  • Significant experience serving on Boards of Directors

  • Established network of industry professionals, including mid to bulge bracket private equity, senior executives, and advisors.

  • Experience in playing a leading role in the investment process, including conducting due diligence, arranging debt financing and negotiating documentation.

  • Strong negotiation, collaboration and communication skills.

  • An ability to balance priorities, work independently, lead projects and thrive in a high performance team environment.

  • Willingness and ability to travel globally.

jobs.title: Special Projects Group, Associate
jobs.description:
About TPG

TPG is a leading global alternative asset management firm, founded in San Francisco in 1992, with investment and operational teams around the world. TPG invests across a broadly diversified set of strategies, including private equity, impact, credit, real estate, and market solutions, and our unique strategy is driven by collaboration, innovation, and inclusion. Our teams combine deep product and sector experience with broad capabilities and expertise to develop differentiated insights and add value for our fund investors, portfolio companies, management teams, and communities.

TPG’s success depends on our people, and we build and sustain our world-class team by creating an inclusive, supportive culture within the firm that seeks excellence and encourages humility and transparency. The quality of our investments and our ability to build great companies depend on the originality of our insights. Reaching our firm’s full potential means supporting every team member to bring the fullness of their unique perspective to their work and to our community. We are committed to a diverse, equitable, and inclusive workplace to foster diversity of thought and reflect the breadth of our limited partners and portfolio companies. 

Description of Position

TPG is seeking a versatile Associate to work in the Special Projects Group. The Special Projects Group at TPG leads cross-functional initiatives to increase revenue, mitigate risk, enhance operational efficiency, and drive change throughout the organization. Our team works closely with stakeholders across all TPG Platforms (Real Estate, Capital, Growth, Impact, and Credit) and across business functions (Investment Businesses, Finance, Operations and Technology, Investor Services, Marketing, HR, Compliance, and Legal). The role requires an adaptive and dynamic professional who can build and maintain strong professional relationships across the firm.

Principal Responsibilities

  • Assist with management of the portfolio of firmwide projects, including prioritization, resourcing, status reporting, and communication with senior management
  • Understand strategic objectives of the firm and ensure projects align to firm goals
  • Serve as a primary advisor to business stakeholders during the project lifecycle and be the go-to for details and status updates on the project
  • Collaborate with key stakeholders across business functions to identify and understand project impacts
  • Establish end-to-end project structure, including planning, defining requirements, creating action plans, and resolving conflicts
  • Coordinate with members of the project team, technology and business to ensure effective planning and execution of project tasks
    • Facilitate meetings with project stakeholders and drive decisions
    • Identify, monitor and manage resolution of issues. Propose and establish resolution plans and drive results
    • Create and present executive-friendly deliverables
  • Actively identify opportunities to improve business processes, including:
    • Opportunities for automation through use of technology
    • Utilization of third-party service providers
    • Other process improvements that result in process efficiency and scalability

Requirements

  • 2-4 years of years of full-time work experience in consulting, strategy, project management, business transformation and/or other related fields.
  • Bachelor’s degree required
  • Financial services industry experience required; Experience in the alternative investment management industry preferred
  • Proven track record working on large, complex, cross functional initiatives
  • Experience solving business problems and driving process improvements
  • Experience recommending solutions and facilitating decision making
  • Ability to quickly learn new subject matter as projects span varied topics and functions
  • Excellent verbal and written communication skills
  • Excellent organizational skills and ability to effectively multi-task
  • Demonstrated attention to detail

It is expected that the annual base salary for this role will range from $120,000 – $140,000. Base salary is one component of TPG’s competitive total rewards package which typically includes discretionary compensation, comprehensive health benefits, and paid time off. Other incentives may also be available to certain eligible employees.

jobs.title: Financial Analyst Senior - Underwriting, Product Development & Modernization
jobs.description:
Why USAA?

At USAA, our mission is to empower our members to achieve financial security through highly competitive products, exceptional service and trusted advice. We seek to be the #1 choice for the military community and their families.

Embrace a fulfilling career at USAA, where our core values – honesty, integrity, loyalty and service – define how we treat each other and our members. Be part of what truly makes us special and impactful.

We are proud to support active-duty military spouses. USAA roles may offer remote or hybrid flexibility for active-duty military spouses consistent with applicable policy and business needs.

The Opportunity

As a dedicated Financial Analyst Senior, you will be responsible for solving complex business problems while performing forecasting, planning, and financial modeling related to income, profitability, liquidity, capital, leverage, asset and liability management or operating expense. Mentors team members to execute more complex financial processes and analysis, utilizing financial principles. Ensuring all activities are completed within regulatory and compliance guidelines.

This role is remote eligible in the continental U.S. with occasional business travel. However, individuals residing within a 60-mile radius of a USAA office will be expected to work on-site 4 days per week.

What you'll do:

  • Leads team or process in delivery of financial forecasts/plans for a business area or function; guides team members and oversees implementation of forecasting/planning process and model improvement initiatives and business solutions.

  • Prepares and guides team on complex financial reports and analysis or cost benefit analysis to support and provides insight for internal and external management decision-making.

  • Interpret complex financial information and make recommendations to business leaders. Implements solutions to key financial and operational opportunities.

  • Contributes to achievement of business goals and objectives by delivering value-added business decision support to customers and business partners across the organization.

  • Leads product assignments, product pricing, product profitability and ad-hoc analysis.

  • Acts as a valued business partner by providing informed, proactive analysis and business insights to enable effective decision-making and efficient resource utilization.

  • Interacts with senior management and company leadership, guides team and promotes innovation and productivity to support USAA's mission and operating state.

  • Acquires and applies advanced knowledge of the business, its products and processes and serves as a resource to team members on escalated issues.

  • Ensure risks associated with business activities are effectively identified, measured, monitored, and controlled in accordance with risk and compliance policies and procedures.

What you have:

  • Bachelor's degree in Business, Accounting, Finance, Economics; OR a related field 4 additional years of related experience (in addition to the minimum years of experience required) may be substituted in lieu of degree.

  • 6 years’ experience conducting complex financial/accounting analysis and providing actionable insights to various stakeholders OR Advanced degree in Business, Accounting, Finance, Economics, or a related field, progress towards a CPA or CFA designation and, 4 years of financial / accounting analysis or related work experience.

  • Experience using financial/accounting technologies.

  • Experience communicating key financial/accounting concepts.

  • Finance/accounting experience in a financial services environment.

  • Experience using Microsoft Office Excel for significant financial data analysis.

  • Experience in the application and adherence to financial regulations and compliance.

  • Experience producing financial reports and providing recommendations based on data.

  • Working knowledge of GAAP.

What sets you apart:

  • US military experience through military service or a military spouse/domestic partner.

  • Experience working in a matrixed organization.

Compensation range: The salary range for this position is: $103,450 - $197,730.

USAA does not provide visa sponsorship for this role. Please do not apply for this role if at any time (now or in the future) you will need immigration support (i.e., H-1B, TN, STEM OPT Training Plans, etc.).

Compensation: USAA has an effective process for assessing market data and establishing ranges to ensure we remain competitive. You are paid within the salary range based on your experience and market data of the position. The actual salary for this role may vary by location.

Employees may be eligible for pay incentives based on overall corporate and individual performance and at the discretion of the USAA Board of Directors.

The above description reflects the details considered necessary to describe the principal functions of the job and should not be construed as a detailed description of all the work requirements that may be performed in the job.

Benefits: At USAA our employees enjoy best-in-class benefits to support their physical, financial, and emotional wellness. These benefits include comprehensive medical, dental and vision plans, 401(k), pension, life insurance, parental benefits, adoption assistance, paid time off program with paid holidays plus 16 paid volunteer hours, and various wellness programs. Additionally, our career path planning and continuing education assists employees with their professional goals.

For more details on our outstanding benefits, visit our benefits page on USAAjobs.com.

Applications for this position are accepted on an ongoing basis, this posting will remain open until the position is filled. Thus, interested candidates are encouraged to apply the same day they view this posting.

USAA is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, or status as a protected veteran.

jobs.title: Financial Analyst Lead – Underwriting, Product Development & Modernization
jobs.description:
Why USAA?

At USAA, our mission is to empower our members to achieve financial security through highly competitive products, exceptional service and trusted advice. We seek to be the #1 choice for the military community and their families.

Embrace a fulfilling career at USAA, where our core values – honesty, integrity, loyalty and service – define how we treat each other and our members. Be part of what truly makes us special and impactful.

We are proud to support active-duty military spouses. USAA roles may offer remote or hybrid flexibility for active-duty military spouses consistent with applicable policy and business needs.

The Opportunity

As a dedicated Financial Analyst Lead you will be responsible for leading business financial process and expense categories to solve complex business problems while performing forecasting, planning, and financial modeling related to income, profitability, liquidity, capital, leverage, asset and liability management or operating expense. Mentors about complex financial processes. Drives and motivates team members to provide advanced analytics and insights into key business drivers. Assists management in assigning team tasks and managing workflow ensuring all activities are completed within regulatory and compliance guidelines.

This role is remote eligible in the continental U.S. with occasional business travel. However, individuals residing within a 60-mile radius of a USAA office will be expected to work on-site 4 days per week.

What you'll do:

  • Leads, develops, and motivates the team to provide advanced analytics and insights around key business drivers.

  • Anticipates opportunities to tackle the most complex and / or unique business problems, providing proactive financial solutions with holistic and forward-looking perspectives to enable better decision making by senior management.

  • Consistently interacts with senior management becoming their trusted advisor on all financial issues.

  • Make recommendations to solve short-term, as well as strategic financial business problems.

  • Teach and mentor others about complex financial processes, business drivers and systems ensuring information sharing and knowledge transfer.

  • Reviews analysts work and assist management in assigning tasks and managing workflow.

  • Leads initiatives to develop an integrated enterprise planning process from multiple companies and/or staff agency processes and technologies.

  • Directs analyses and guides the development, enhancement, and implementation of critical processes, programs, and technologies.

  • Anticipates opportunities and potential solutions, leads cross-functional teams through the problem resolution process, and advises senior management on issues.

  • Acquires and applies expert knowledge of the business, its products and processes.

  • Performs complex and often unique work assignments and problem resolution across multiple systems, functions, or processes.

  • Ensure risks associated with business activities are effectively identified, measured, monitored, and controlled in accordance with risk and compliance policies and procedures.

What you have:

  • Bachelor's degree in Business, Accounting, Finance, Economics; OR a related field 4 additional years of related experience (in addition to the minimum years of experience required) may be substituted in lieu of degree.

  • 8 years of financial/accounting analysis experience to include 2 years leading strategic financial/accounting opportunities. OR Advanced degree in Business, Accounting, Finance, Economics, or a related field, progress towards a CPA or CFA designation, and 6 years of financial / accounting analysis experience to include 1 year leading strategic financial/accounting opportunities.

  • Subject-matter-expert knowledge of data analysis and relevant technical accounting/finance acumen required.

  • Subject-matter-expert level of business acumen in the areas of the financial services operations, finance/accounting industry practices and emerging trends required.

  • Experience communicating complex financial/accounting topics to a variety of audiences.

  • Experience using financial/accounting technologies and proposing recommendations for improvements.

  • Experience using Microsoft Office Excel for significant financial data analysis and reporting.

  • Experience in the application and adherence to financial regulations and compliance.

  • Experience producing financial reports and providing recommendations based on data.

  • Working knowledge of GAAP.

What sets you apart:

  • US military experience through military service or a military spouse/domestic partner.

  • Experience collaborating and managing within a matrixed organization.

  • P&L and Product Management experience.

Compensation range: The salary range for this position is: $127,310 - $243,340.

jobs.title: Manager, Life and Health Underwriting
jobs.description:
Why USAA?

At USAA, our mission is to empower our members to achieve financial security through highly competitive products, exceptional service and trusted advice. We seek to be the #1 choice for the military community and their families.

Embrace a fulfilling career at USAA, where our core values – honesty, integrity, loyalty and service – define how we treat each other and our members. Be part of what truly makes us special and impactful.

We are proud to support active-duty military spouses. USAA roles may offer remote or hybrid flexibility for active-duty military spouses consistent with applicable policy and business needs.

The Opportunity

As a dedicated Manager within the Life and Health Underwriting team, you will manage the daily operations of Life and Health Underwriting department for a variety of products to contribute to the achievement of financial objectives of the business. Manage employees (underwriters) who underwrite individual insurance applicants and who are dedicated to knowing our members, understanding their individual needs, and providing risk appropriate solutions. Develop employees through regular coaching and feedback that leads to improved results including establishing individual approval authority from $500K to $5 Million. Execute process improvements and lead organizational process changes.

We offer a flexible work environment that requires an individual to be in the office 4 days per week. This position can be based in one of the following locations: San Antonio, TX.  Relocation assistance is not available for this position.

What you'll do:

  • Select, develop, and support employees in fulfillment of our Corporate Mission, including establishment of individual approval authorities up to $5 mil.
  • Manage employee performance and facilitate professional development and career progression, through effective coaching and feedback, leading to sound business results.
  • Proactively identify opportunities to improve operational effectiveness, through implementation and monitoring of processes and performance standards. Partners with Policy Team to create new Underwriting policies and procedures.
  • Identifies and provides timely feedback for improvements to key stakeholders (e.g. Senior Leaders, Process and Product Owners).
  • Achieve optimal productivity through managing workload volumes, staffing, training needs, and identifying and implementing appropriate solutions. Removes obstacles and champion change.
  • Participate, coordinate and/or independently leads complex projects to include major system and process improvement initiatives.
  • Respond to complex/sensitive customer Service complaints and questions in compliance with State Regulators as well as the National Association on Insurance Commission guidelines.
  • Represent department on action teams and other decision-making forums.
  • Ensure risks associated with business activities are effectively identified, measured, monitored, and controlled in accordance with risk and compliance policies and procedures.

What you have:

  • Bachelor's degree; OR 4 years of relevant education and/or experience.
  • 6 years relevant experience with insurance products (selling, underwriting, issuing, servicing, or paying claims).
  • 3 years of direct team lead, supervisory or management experience required.
  • 2 years of experience in identifying and implementing process improvement opportunities and leading organizational change.
  • Extensive knowledge of the function and department processes.
  • Ability to analyze data, identify alternative solutions, and validate performance metrics.

What sets you apart:

  • Strong people development skills
  • Experience with Bestow Underwriting Workbench and/or Accenture Policy Administration system
  • Expert experience in Excel and Microsoft Office
  • US military experience through military service or a military spouse/domestic partner

jobs.title: Principal, Private Credit Risk Management
jobs.description:
Why USAA?

At USAA, our mission is to empower our members to achieve financial security through highly competitive products, exceptional service and trusted advice. We seek to be the #1 choice for the military community and their families.

Embrace a fulfilling career at USAA, where our core values – honesty, integrity, loyalty and service – define how we treat each other and our members. Be part of what truly makes us special and impactful.

We are proud to support active-duty military spouses. USAA roles may offer remote or hybrid flexibility for active-duty military spouses consistent with applicable policy and business needs.

The Opportunity

This role requires a seasoned professional to manage and oversee risk within the Private Credit book of business, ensuring alignment with strategic objectives. The Principal will be instrumental in enhancing the existing risk framework and applying it across the entire Second Line of Defense (SLOD) Private Credit lifecycle. A key aspect of this position involves identifying and prioritizing capabilities needed to effectively manage risk and support strategic execution. This includes deep expertise in covenant oversight, comprehensive asset manager due diligence and monitoring, and leveraging industry best practices derived from leading insurance LPs and institutional allocators. The Principal will play a crucial role in the oversight of private credit risk management and communicating this vision to leadership.

We offer a flexible work environment that requires an individual to be in the office 4 days per week. This position can be based in one of the following locations: San Antonio, TX, Plano, TX, Phoenix, AZ, Colorado Springs, CO, Charlotte, NC, or Tampa, FL.

Relocation assistance is available for this position.

Key Responsibilities:

  • Risk Framework Development and Implementation: Enhance and operationalize the private credit risk framework, ensuring its consistent application across all stages of the credit lifecycle. This involves bringing a proprietary risk perspective and calibrating it with leadership to establish a comprehensive blueprint.
  • Independent Covenant Monitoring and Challenge: Provide independent oversight and challenge of the first line of defense's loan covenant monitoring and enforcement activities. This involves analyzing covenant structures, assessing the effectiveness of monitoring processes, identifying potential covenant breaches, and evaluating proposed remediation plans to ensure adherence to agreed-upon terms and protect the institution's assets and investor capital.
  • Asset Manager Due Diligence and Monitoring: Conduct thorough due diligence on potential and existing asset managers, assessing their underwriting capabilities, operational processes, risk management frameworks, and compliance with best practices. This includes ongoing monitoring of their performance, risk characteristics, and adherence to investment guidelines, drawing on relevant client experience and practitioner insights.
  • Portfolio Risk Management: Oversee the day-to-day operations of the private credit investment portfolio, ensuring alignment with approved investment guidelines and risk parameters. Manage and monitor the risk and return drivers within the portfolio, including assessing concentration risk and liquidity.
  • Strategic Challenge and Influence: Provide a strong, independent challenge to First Line of Defense (FLOD) activities, risk metrics, and established risk limits. Influence private credit tactical and strategic asset allocation recommendations and provide expert counsel with financial implications to senior leaders. This includes delivering an executive risk narrative that links initiatives to underlying risks and strategic objectives.
  • Capability Identification and Enhancement: Identify critical capability needs within the risk management function and champion initiatives to build the necessary expertise and infrastructure to support strategic objectives. This involves facilitating capability workshops, discussing leading practices, and defining enhancement initiatives.
  • Reporting and Communication: Communicate complex risk analyses and findings concisely to senior stakeholders, committees, and investment professionals. Ensure transparent and accurate reporting to senior leaders, translating complex findings into a prioritized capability narrative.
  • Market Expertise: Maintain expert knowledge of financial markets, emerging risks, and complex asset classes relevant to private credit, including asset-backed securities, direct lending, and private infrastructure debt. This includes understanding observed failure modes and how structural features of private credit elevate management complexity.
  • Collaboration: Work closely with internal teams, including investment, underwriting, legal, and finance, to align private credit strategy and ensure operational excellence. Engage with stakeholders across the 2nd-line lifecycle to discuss capabilities and the effectiveness of risk metrics, analytics, and reporting.

Minimum Education:

  • Bachelor's degree in Economics, Finance, Accounting, or a related quantitative discipline.
  • Alternatively, four additional years of experience in a quantitative discipline may substitute for a degree.
  • 10 years of experience as a financial analyst, portfolio manager, or researcher
  • 5-6 years of experience with managing, monitoring, and/or oversight of a private credit book of business
  • Experience with covenant analysis, loan documentation, and asset manager due diligence and monitoring processes.
  • Demonstrated strategy development and thought leadership within the asset management industry.
  • Leading-edge knowledge and expertise in theories, techniques, and technologies within the financial risk industry, with a focus on private credit.
  • Ability to interpret financial data, assess market trends, and perform due diligence.
  • Proficiency in financial modeling, Excel, and financial software for the analysis of large datasets
  • Expert knowledge of financial markets and emerging risks impacting investment portfolio performance, specifically within private credit.
  • Expert knowledge of complex asset classes such as asset-backed securities, mortgage-backed securities, and/or alternative investments.
  • Mastery of consulting skills with proven experience communicating analytical and modeling results to non-technical business partners, with an emphasis on business recommendations and actionable applications of results.
  • Chartered Financial Analyst (CFA) or Financial Risk Manager (FRM) professional designation is highly desirable.
  • Familiarity with Value-at-Risk (VaR), stress testing, and scenario analysis
  • Familiarity with insurance industry regulations and risk management expectations for Limited Partnerships (LPs).

What sets you apart:

  • Leads Second Line of Defense (SLOD) risk oversight for the Private Credit portfolio, ensuring alignment with enterprise risk appetite and strategic objectives.
  • Drives the design and enhancement of the Private Credit risk framework, embedding robust governance, controls, and lifecycle risk monitoring.
  • Oversees covenant compliance and credit risk exposures, proactively identifying issues and ensuring adherence to investment guidelines.
  • Directs comprehensive asset manager due diligence and ongoing monitoring, leveraging best practices from institutional allocators and insurance LPs.
  • Partners with senior leadership to define and communicate the vision for Private Credit risk management, enabling informed, data-driven decisions.
  • Translates complex credit and quantitative risk concepts into clear, actionable insights for executive and non-technical stakeholders.
  • Leads cross-functional initiatives with strong project management and organizational discipline, delivering high-quality outcomes in fast-paced environments.

Compensation range: The salary range for this position is: $189,370 - $361,950.

USAA does not provide visa sponsorship for this role. Please do not apply for this role if at any time (now or in the future) you will need immigration support (i.e., H-1B, TN, STEM OPT Training Plans, etc.).

Compensation: USAA has an effective process for assessing market data and establishing ranges to ensure we remain competitive. You are paid within the salary range based on your experience and market data of the position. The actual salary for this role may vary by location.

Employees may be eligible for pay incentives based on overall corporate and individual performance and at the discretion of the USAA Board of Directors.

The above description reflects the details considered necessary to describe the principal functions of the job and should not be construed as a detailed description of all the work requirements that may be performed in the job.

Benefits: At USAA our employees enjoy best-in-class benefits to support their physical, financial, and emotional wellness. These benefits include comprehensive medical, dental and vision plans, 401(k), pension, life insurance, parental benefits, adoption assistance, paid time off program with paid holidays plus 16 paid volunteer hours, and various wellness programs. Additionally, our career path planning and continuing education assists employees with their professional goals.

For more details on our outstanding benefits, visit our benefits page on USAAjobs.com.
